# A2RCHITECH_BUILDOUT_MASTER_AGENT_REPORT

## 1. Executive Map
### 1.1 What A2rchitech is in actual code (Atlas baseline)
- Monorepo with Rust workspace members and pnpm workspace packages; primary languages are Rust, TypeScript/JavaScript, and Python. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L5-L7 "Repo structure: monorepo..." and "Primary languages: Rust...TypeScript/JavaScript...Python...")
- Kernel and brain session runtime are implemented; CLI brain PTY runtime is partial. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172849-L172851 "Kernel HTTP API" / "Brain sessions + SSE streams" / "CLI brain PTY runtime" with Partial.)
- Tool gateway with policy gating, skill registry, and workflow engine are implemented. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172854-L172856 "Tool gateway with policy gating" / "Skill manifest + registry" / "Workflow engine + DAG".)
- Memory fabric is partial and memory maintenance daemon is a stub. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172857-L172858 "Memory fabric + retention policy" marked Partial and "Memory maintenance daemon" marked Stub.)
- Shell UI window manager and Electron desktop shell are partial. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172859-L172860 "Shell UI window manager" and "Electron desktop shell" marked Partial.)
- Runtime services include WebVM, voice service, browser runtime, A2A gateway, AGUI gateway; UI-TARS operator and superconductor are partial. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172861-L172867.)
- Security and governance red flags exist: unsandboxed Python exec, auth bypass for kernel endpoints, API keys stored in JSON, in-memory registries/policy services. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172879-L172885.)

### 1.2 What A2rchitech is supposed to be (Sessions evidence)
- A productivity OS / agentic platform: an agent harness that reduces context switching, provides one-place discovery, and supports modular swarm orchestration. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/a2r_session_2026-01-26_external-signals_agent-harness_a2ui.md` L3-L4.)
- An agentic engineering OS with windowed capsules, browser capsule, miniapp capsules, A2UI/AG-UI plane, and a top-level CLI orchestrating multiple external CLI “brains” via PTY; OpenWork is first-class in Shell UI; UI-TARS is a computer-use tool layer. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-ShellUI-Browsercapsule-openwork-cliorchestrator-uitars.md` L3-L33.)
- Core architecture: “Everything is a Brain Session” with a single frontend contract to the Brain Gateway, stateful/resumable sessions, and dual-stream UX (chat + console). (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/a2r_session_2026-01-26_shellui_brain_runtime_ui_e2e.md` L17-L30.)
- Memory v2 is truth-preserving, proof-gated, and graph-enabled; external memory engines are backends, not authority layers. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R_Session_Summary__Memory_v2_Proof_Gating_and_External_Memory_Systems.md` L7-L12, L31-L50, L60-L69, L107-L110, L127-L130.)
- Registry + Marketplace are distinct control-plane concepts but one UI surface, with immutable pinning, draft→review→activate lifecycle, and asset types (skills/tools/agents/prompts/workflows/packs). (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/a2r_session_marketplace_registry.md` L15-L50.)
- AG-UI is a real-time agent↔user interaction protocol (not a UI toolkit) that standardizes event-driven UI mutation, state sync, and interruptions; MCP is tool execution and A2A is agent coordination. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-AG-UI.md` L32-L119.)
- A law layer / repo law is required: policy gates, contracts, boot order, and enforcement to prevent drift and destructive actions. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/a2r_session_2026-01-26_external-signals_agent-harness_a2ui.md` L52-L57.)

### 1.3 Biggest mismatches (top 10)
1) Capsule OS windowing requirements vs partial window manager implementation.
   - Intended: capsules must support move/drag/resize/snap/minimize/tabbing/reopen. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-ShellUI-Browsercapsule-openwork-cliorchestrator-uitars.md` L56-L66.)
   - Current: Shell UI window manager is marked Partial. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172859.)
2) Browser capsule UX requirements vs current code inventory limited to browser runtime service.
   - Intended: browser capsule must include tab strip, nav controls, omnibox, mode switch, and dockable miniapps. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-ShellUI-Browsercapsule-openwork-cliorchestrator-uitars.md` L67-L74.)
   - Current: Atlas documents the browser runtime service (Playwright) as implemented, without UI capsule behavior in capability matrix. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172864.)
3) A2 CLI as top process vs current boot flow that starts services directly.
   - Intended: “A2 CLI is the controlling parent process” that spawns CLI brains via PTY. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-ShellUI-Browsercapsule-openwork-cliorchestrator-uitars.md` L10-L18.)
   - Current: dev/run.sh boot flow starts voice/webvm/shell UI/kernel directly. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172906-L172914.)
4) Marketplace control plane with immutable pinning and draft lifecycle vs atlas showing only skill registry.
   - Intended: marketplace import → draft → registry review/test → activate; no execution without immutable pinning. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/a2r_session_marketplace_registry.md` L27-L50.)
   - Current: skill manifest + registry is implemented; no marketplace lifecycle is listed in capability matrix. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172855.)
5) Proof-gated Memory v2 and maintenance integration vs stubbed memory maintenance daemon.
   - Intended: proof-gating mandatory; maintenance daemon must be integrated and instrumented. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R_Session_Summary__Memory_v2_Proof_Gating_and_External_Memory_Systems.md` L60-L69, L127-L139.)
   - Current: memory maintenance daemon is a stub; memory fabric only partial. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172857-L172858.)
6) Governance and law-layer enforcement vs explicit security bypasses and unsandboxed execution.
   - Intended: law layer and policy gates prevent drift/destructive actions. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/a2r_session_2026-01-26_external-signals_agent-harness_a2ui.md` L52-L57.)
   - Current: auth bypass for kernel endpoints, unsandboxed Python exec, API keys stored in JSON. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172879-L172883.)
7) Swarm orchestration emphasis vs no swarm capability listed in Atlas capability matrix.
   - Intended: swarm orchestration is first-class (e.g., “agent swarm orchestration” and swarm controller language). (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-AG-UI.md` L22-L26; `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-Brain CLI-ShellUI.md` L634-L672.)
   - Current: capability matrix enumerates kernel/brain/memory/UI/etc. but does not list swarm orchestration capability. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172846-L172869.)
8) UI-TARS as tool layer requirement vs UI-TARS operator only partial.
   - Intended: UI-TARS is the computer-use tool layer. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-ShellUI-Browsercapsule-openwork-cliorchestrator-uitars.md` L26-L30.)
   - Current: UI-TARS operator is Partial. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172863.)
9) OpenWork as first-class Shell UI tab vs OpenWork integration described as a stub wrapper.
   - Intended: OpenWork must be a first-class Shell UI tab/view, not an iframe. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R Session-ShellUI-Browsercapsule-openwork-cliorchestrator-uitars.md` L20-L23.)
   - Current: OpenWork UI integration is a stub wrapper with demo content. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2RCHITECH_CODEBASE_ATLAS.md` L172893.)
10) Fork vs rebuild strategy for the UI baseline is inconsistent across sessions.
   - Session A: avoid forking OSS chat UI for core product; use as reference only. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/a2r_session_2026-01-26_shellui_brain_runtime_ui_e2e.md` L10-L13.)
   - Session B: Open WebUI listed as a fork baseline for browser shell/web console. (Evidence: `/Users/macbook/Desktop/a2r-sessions/A2R_Session_Summary_2026-01-26_browser_os_cli_capsules.md` L250-L254.)

```text
L0020 | 🧠 First-Principles Definition: What an Agent Runtime Must Do

L0022 | An agent runtime must satisfy five irreducible functions:
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — 2026-01-26  
L0002 | **Topic:** Agentic Context Engineering & Long-Running Agent Architecture  
L0003 | **Trigger:** gizzi save a2r session
L0004 | 
L0005 | ---
L0006 | 
L0007 | ## 1. Session Focus
L0008 | 
L0009 | This session analyzed a long-form X thread by Lance Martin outlining **design patterns emerging across successful production agents** (Claude Code, Manus, Cursor, Amp, etc.), with emphasis on:
L0010 | 
L0011 | - Context as a scarce resource
L0012 | - OS-level agent control
L0013 | - Filesystem-based memory
L0014 | - Progressive disclosure of tools
L0015 | - Sub-agent isolation
L0016 | - Long-running orchestration loops
L0017 | - Learned / evolving agent memory
L0018 | 
L0019 | The synthesis explicitly mapped these ideas into **foundational laws for A2rchitech’s agent-OS vision**.
L0020 | 
L0021 | ---
L0022 | 
L0023 | ## 2. Core First-Principles Conclusion
L0024 | 
L0025 | > **Agents are memory-allocation systems that reason incidentally.**
L0026 | 
L0027 | All effective architectures converge on:
L0028 | - Minimizing token growth
L0029 | - Externalizing state
L0030 | - Delaying tokenization
L0031 | - Isolating interference
L0032 | - Amortizing cost through caching
L0033 | - Moving expressivity into OS primitives
L0034 | 
L0035 | ---
L0036 | 
L0037 | ## 3. Observed Industry Patterns (Extracted)
L0038 | 
L0039 | ### 3.1 Context Scarcity
L0040 | - Context windows degrade with size.
L0041 | - Tool schemas and traces overload attention budgets.
L0042 | - Long-running tasks are growing faster than usable context.
L0043 | 
L0044 | ### 3.2 “Give Agents a Computer”
L0045 | Agents increasingly:
L0046 | - Live on a real or virtual OS
L0047 | - Use:
L0048 |   - Filesystems as persistent memory
L0049 |   - Shells as compressed action interfaces
L0050 |   - Code execution to chain steps without re-tokenizing outputs
L0051 | 
L0052 | **Primitive shift:**
L0053 | LLM → CLI → OS → World.
L0054 | 
L0055 | ---
L0056 | 
L0057 | ### 3.3 Collapsing Tool Explosion
L0058 | Instead of hundreds of tools:
L0059 | - Successful agents use <20 atomic tools
L0060 | - Expressivity pushed downward:
L0061 |   - bash → CLIs → scripts → generated code
L0062 | 
L0063 | ---
L0064 | 
L0065 | ### 3.4 Progressive Disclosure
L0066 | Tools and skills are:
L0067 | - Indexed first
L0068 | - Fully loaded only on demand
L0069 | 
L0070 | Filesystem becomes a **tool registry**.
L0071 | 
L0072 | SKILL.md pattern:
L0073 | - YAML header always loaded
L0074 | - Full skill text pulled conditionally
L0075 | 
L0076 | ---
L0077 | 
L0078 | ### 3.5 Filesystem as External Memory
L0079 | Agents:
L0080 | - Dump traces, plans, tool outputs to disk
L0081 | - Re-ingest selectively
L0082 | - Summarize only when necessary
L0083 | 
L0084 | Avoids irreversible compression early.
L0085 | 
L0086 | ---
L0087 | 
L0088 | ### 3.6 Prompt Caching as a Production Constraint
L0089 | - Cache hit rate is a primary KPI.
L0090 | - Mutating history freely destroys economics.
L0091 | - Long-running agents must preserve prefix stability.
L0092 | 
L0093 | ---
L0094 | 
L0095 | ### 3.7 Context Isolation with Sub-Agents
L0096 | Sub-agents used for:
L0097 | - Parallelism
L0098 | - Verification
L0099 | - Map-reduce tasks
L0100 | - Long-running loops
L0101 | 
L0102 | Ralph Loop:
L0103 | - Plan lives in files
L0104 | - Workers iterate until satisfied
L0105 | - Progress tracked externally (git / logs)
L0106 | 
L0107 | ---
L0108 | 
L0109 | ### 3.8 Evolving Context Over Time
L0110 | Agents learn in token-space:
L0111 | 
L0112 | Pipeline:
L0113 | 1. Log trajectories
L0114 | 2. Reflect
L0115 | 3. Distill heuristics
L0116 | 4. Append to memory files / playbooks / skills
L0117 | 
L0118 | Memory becomes **append-only living prompts**.
L0119 | 
L0120 | ---
L0121 | 
L0122 | ### 3.9 Learned Context Management
L0123 | Research trend:
L0124 | - Recursive Language Models
L0125 | - Agents trained to manage retrieval & compaction themselves
L0126 | - External scaffolding gradually absorbed into models
L0127 | 
L0128 | ---
L0129 | 
L0130 | ### 3.10 Multi-Agent Coordination Pressure
L0131 | Problems:
L0132 | - Conflicting parallel decisions
L0133 | - No shared discourse layer
L0134 | - No standard observability
L0135 | 
L0136 | Early solutions:
L0137 | - Mayor / orchestrator agents
L0138 | - Git-backed coordination
L0139 | - Merge queues
L0140 | 
L0141 | ---
L0142 | 
L0143 | ## 4. A2rchitech-Relevant Architectural Laws
L0144 | 
L0145 | Derived explicitly for your platform:
L0146 | 
L0147 | ### LAW-01: OS Is the Action Plane
L0148 | All agents must operate primarily through:
L0149 | - Files
L0150 | - Processes
L0151 | - Shells
L0152 | - Git / logs
L0153 | 
L0154 | ---
L0155 | 
L0156 | ### LAW-02: Narrow Tools, Wide Compute
L0157 | Keep tool APIs minimal.
L0158 | Push expressivity into:
L0159 | - Code
L0160 | - CLI composition
L0161 | - Local scripts
L0162 | 
L0163 | ---
L0164 | 
L0165 | ### LAW-03: Filesystem = Primary Memory
L0166 | Context window is cache.
L0167 | Disk is truth.
L0168 | 
L0169 | ---
L0170 | 
L0171 | ### LAW-04: Progressive Disclosure Everywhere
L0172 | Tools, skills, plans, logs, and MCP servers are indexed—not injected.
L0173 | 
L0174 | ---
L0175 | 
L0176 | ### LAW-05: Cache-Aware Context Mutation
L0177 | Prefix stability must be preserved.
L0178 | Context rewriting must be deliberate.
L0179 | 
L0180 | ---
L0181 | 
L0182 | ### LAW-06: Context Isolation by Default
L0183 | Parallel work always uses:
L0184 | - Separate windows
L0185 | - Shared files
L0186 | - Explicit coordination artifacts
L0187 | 
L0188 | ---
L0189 | 
L0190 | ### LAW-07: Append-Only Learning
L0191 | No silent overwrites.
L0192 | Memories evolve through reflection logs and distilled playbooks.
L0193 | 
L0194 | ---
L0195 | 
L0196 | ### LAW-08: Orchestration Requires New Abstractions
L0197 | A2rchitech will require:
L0198 | - Agent debuggers
L0199 | - Observability UIs
L0200 | - Loop inspectors
L0201 | - Failure recovery systems
L0202 | - Human-in-the-loop gates
L0203 | 
L0204 | ---
L0205 | 
L0206 | ## 5. Open Research Directions Logged
L0207 | 
L0208 | - Learned retrieval vs manual compaction
L0209 | - Offline “sleep-time compute”
L0210 | - Recursive self-management
L0211 | - Multi-agent conflict resolution protocols
L0212 | - Standards for observability & debugging
L0213 | 
L0214 | ---
L0215 | 
L0216 | ## 6. Session Outcome
L0217 | 
L0218 | This session establishes:
L0219 | 
L0220 | **A2rchitech = Agentic Operating System for Long-Running, Context-Constrained Swarms**
L0221 | 
L0222 | Where:
L0223 | - Memory is external
L0224 | - Agents are modular
L0225 | - Learning is append-only
L0226 | - Orchestration is first-class
L0227 | - CLI is the universal interface
L0228 | - Context is budgeted
L0229 | 
L0230 | ---
L0231 | 
L0232 | ## 7. Status
L0233 | 
L0234 | Saved for later consolidation into the canonical A2rchitech buildout thread.
L0235 | 
L0236 | ---
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | Got it. Below is the canonical Markdown session summary for this A2rchitech-focused session, ready to be consolidated into the main buildout chat.
L0002 | 
L0003 | ⸻
L0004 | 
L0005 | 
L0006 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — Dynamic Context, Retrieval, and Quantization
L0007 | **Session Topic:** Context Discovery + Binary/int8 + LEANN Integration  
L0008 | **Command Invoked:** gizzi save a2r session  
L0009 | **Date:** 2026-01-26
L0010 | 
L0011 | ---
L0012 | 
L0013 | ## 1. Session Goal
L0014 | 
L0015 | Establish a **scalable, CPU-first architecture** for:
L0016 | - Long-lived agent context & memory
L0017 | - High-frequency semantic search
L0018 | - Large-scale retrieval without fp32 embeddings or GPUs
L0019 | 
L0020 | The session unified:
L0021 | - Dynamic Context Discovery (Cursor-style)
L0022 | - Binary + int8 scalar quantized retrieval
L0023 | - LEANN for extreme-scale, cold corpora
L0024 | into a **single deterministic A2rchitech integration plan**.
L0025 | 
L0026 | ---
L0027 | 
L0028 | ## 2. Core Architectural Principle (Agreed)
L0029 | 
L0030 | > **Everything is a file.  
L0031 | > Context is discovered, not preloaded.  
L0032 | > Retrieval is tiered, not monolithic.**
L0033 | 
L0034 | This principle underpins *all* layers of A2rchitech going forward.
L0035 | 
L0036 | ---
L0037 | 
L0038 | ## 3. Dynamic Context Discovery (DCD)
L0039 | 
L0040 | ### 3.1 ContextStore (Kernel Service)
L0041 | - All agent-accessible information is stored as **ContextFiles**
L0042 | - No large artifact is injected into prompts by default
L0043 | 
L0044 | Canonical paths:
L0045 | 
L0046 | /ctx/chat/raw/
L0047 | /ctx/chat/summary/
L0048 | /ctx/tools/mcp/
L0049 | /ctx/term/
L0050 | /ctx/docs/
L0051 | /ctx/skills/
L0052 | 
L0053 | ### 3.2 ContextFiles
L0054 | Each artifact includes:
L0055 | - stable ID + URI
L0056 | - type (chat_raw, summary, tool_output, terminal_log, doc, skill)
L0057 | - size + token estimate
L0058 | - provenance (tool / agent / user)
L0059 | - tags for retrieval
L0060 | 
L0061 | ---
L0062 | 
L0063 | ## 4. Progressive Disclosure Model
L0064 | 
L0065 | Agents interact with context via **file syscalls**, not memory dumps:
L0066 | 
L0067 | - ctx.list
L0068 | - ctx.search (lexical / semantic / hybrid)
L0069 | - ctx.peek / ctx.tail
L0070 | - ctx.read
L0071 | - ctx.grep
L0072 | - ctx.jq
L0073 | 
L0074 | This mirrors an OS model:
L0075 | - Disk ≠ RAM
L0076 | - Files ≠ process memory
L0077 | 
L0078 | ---
L0079 | 
L0080 | ## 5. Chat Memory Model (Lossless)
L0081 | 
L0082 | Two parallel artifacts per session:
L0083 | 
L0084 | 1. **Raw log (never lost)**
L0085 | 
L0086 | /ctx/chat/raw/.ndjson
L0087 | 
L0088 | 2. **Rolling summary (prompt-safe)**
L0089 | 
L0090 | /ctx/chat/summary/_vN.md
L0091 | 
L0092 | Summaries must include:
L0093 | - goals
L0094 | - decisions
L0095 | - constraints
L0096 | - open tasks
L0097 | - canonical URIs
L0098 | 
L0099 | **Recovery rule:**  
L0100 | If details are missing → search raw log.
L0101 | 
L0102 | ---
L0103 | 
L0104 | ## 6. Retrieval Architecture (Hard-Coded)
L0105 | 
L0106 | ### 6.1 Unified Retrieval Gateway
L0107 | - All semantic search goes through a single gateway
L0108 | - Direct vector DB access is disallowed
L0109 | 
L0110 | ---
L0111 | 
L0112 | ## 7. Hot Retrieval Tier (Default)
L0113 | 
L0114 | ### 7.1 Binary + int8 Two-Stage Retrieval
L0115 | 
L0116 | Pipeline:
L0117 | 1. Query → fp32 embedding
L0118 | 2. Quantize → binary
L0119 | 3. Binary index → candidate set
L0120 | 4. int8 scalar rescoring
L0121 | 5. Optional cross-encoder rerank
L0122 | 
L0123 | Why this is default:
L0124 | - Agents query continuously
L0125 | - Latency compounds across loops
L0126 | - CPU-only requirement
L0127 | - 4× memory reduction vs fp32
L0128 | - Cache- and SIMD-friendly
L0129 | 
L0130 | **Sweet spot:**
L0131 | - ~1–10M chunks
L0132 | - Frequent queries
L0133 | - Hot context (skills, tools, chat, code)
L0134 | 
L0135 | ---
L0136 | 
L0137 | ## 8. Cold Retrieval Tier (Optional / Future)
L0138 | 
L0139 | ### 8.1 LEANN Usage Policy
L0140 | 
L0141 | LEANN is **not a replacement**, but a **secondary tier**.
L0142 | 
L0143 | Use LEANN only if:
L0144 | - ≥ ~50–100M chunks
L0145 | - Rare / bursty queries
L0146 | - Extreme storage constraints
L0147 | - Cold or archival corpora
L0148 | 
L0149 | Key distinction clarified:
L0150 | - Binary + int8 → optimizes **hot path / QPS**
L0151 | - LEANN → optimizes **cold path / storage & traversal**
L0152 | 
L0153 | ---
L0154 | 
L0155 | ## 9. Retrieval Profiles (Deterministic Policy)
L0156 | 
L0157 | | Profile | Pipeline | Use Case |
L0158 | |------|---------|---------|
L0159 | | FAST (default) | Binary → int8 | Agent loops |
L0160 | | ACCURATE | Binary → int8 → cross-encoder | Reports / synthesis |
L0161 | | COLD | LEANN | Archives |
L0162 | | DEBUG | fp32 (restricted) | Evaluation |
L0163 | 
L0164 | Agents never choose profiles arbitrarily — policy decides.
L0165 | 
L0166 | ---
L0167 | 
L0168 | ## 10. Skills & Tool Discovery
L0169 | 
L0170 | ### 10.1 Skills
L0171 | Stored as files:
L0172 | 
L0173 | /skills/<skill_name>/skill.md
L0174 | 
L0175 | Only names + short descriptions appear in prompts.
L0176 | 
L0177 | ### 10.2 MCP Tools
L0178 | Mapped as:
L0179 | 
L0180 | /ctx/tools/mcp//
L0181 | tools.json
L0182 | .md
L0183 | status.json
L0184 | 
L0185 | This prevents prompt inflation as MCP servers scale.
L0186 | 
L0187 | ---
L0188 | 
L0189 | ## 11. Terminal & Execution Logs
L0190 | 
L0191 | All terminal sessions write:
L0192 | 
L0193 | /ctx/term/.log
L0194 | 
L0195 | Agents debug via grep + read_range.
L0196 | 
L0197 | ---
L0198 | 
L0199 | ## 12. Context Budget Manager
L0200 | 
L0201 | Hard rules:
L0202 | - Always include goals + latest summary + pointers
L0203 | - Never include full logs or large JSON
L0204 | - ≤ 70% context for retrieved snippets
L0205 | - ≥ 30% reserved for reasoning and tool calls
L0206 | 
L0207 | ---
L0208 | 
L0209 | ## 13. Hard Defaults (Non-Negotiable)
L0210 | 
L0211 | - Binary + int8 is the default retrieval method
L0212 | - Progressive disclosure only
L0213 | - No raw tool payloads in prompts
L0214 | - Recovery is always possible via files
L0215 | - Deterministic agent behavior enforced by policy
L0216 | 
L0217 | ---
L0218 | 
L0219 | ## 14. Integration Artifact Produced
L0220 | 
L0221 | **File created and downloaded:**
L0222 | - `A2rchitech_Integration_Chat_Plan.md`
L0223 | 
L0224 | This file is the **integration baseline** for future research merges.
L0225 | 
L0226 | ---
L0227 | 
L0228 | ## 15. Strategic Outcome
L0229 | 
L0230 | This session locked in:
L0231 | - A CPU-first, agent-native retrieval strategy
L0232 | - A lossless, file-based memory model
L0233 | - A scalable path from IDE-scale agents → archive-scale knowledge
L0234 | - Clear boundaries between hot vs cold retrieval
L0235 | 
L0236 | All future A2rchitech research must **map to this structure** or explicitly extend it.
L0237 | 
L0238 | ---
L0239 | 
L0240 | **End of session summary.**
L0241 | 
L0242 | This session is now canonically captured and ready to be merged into the main A2rchitech buildout thread.
```

```text
L0001 | Got it. You’re not chasing frontier models—you want a practical, modular, multi-model stack that:
L0002 | 
L0003 | • is cheap enough to run continuously
L0004 | • covers every modality
L0005 | • swaps models by capability tier
L0006 | • plugs cleanly into A2rchitech’s agent harness / capsule OS
L0007 | • supports routing, orchestration, CLI agents, skills, swarms, and artifacts
L0008 | 
L0009 | Below is a production-grade Multimodal Tool Stack Blueprint that fits directly into your A2rchitech philosophy.
L0010 | 
L0011 | This is written as a systems design guide, not a vendor pitch.
L0012 | 
L0013 | ⸻
L0014 | 
L0015 | 🔩 A2rchitech Modular Multimodal Stack (Non-Frontier Tier)
L0016 | 
L0017 | Design Principles
L0018 | 
L0019 | First principles:
L0020 | 	1.	Capability-first routing
L0021 | → choose model by task, not brand.
L0022 | 	2.	Stateless inference + stateful orchestration
L0023 | → models are dumb engines; A2rchitech holds memory.
L0024 | 	3.	CLI-first deployment
L0025 | → every model wrapped as an executable service.
L0026 | 	4.	Artifact-first outputs
L0027 | → OCR → JSON
L0028 | → code → diff
L0029 | → images → PNG + metadata
L0030 | → reasoning → trace graph
L0031 | 	5.	Hot-swappable providers
L0032 | → same interface, multiple backends.
L0033 | 
L0034 | ⸻
L0035 | 
L0036 | 🧠 Capability Routing Layer (Core)
L0037 | 
L0038 | This is the router brain inside A2rchitech.
L0039 | 
L0040 | Every request is classified into:
L0041 | 
L0042 | capability:
L0043 |   - ocr
L0044 |   - vision_reasoning
L0045 |   - cheap_code
L0046 |   - heavy_code
L0047 |   - reasoning
L0048 |   - writing
L0049 |   - general
L0050 |   - image_gen
L0051 |   - image_edit
L0052 | cost_tier:
L0053 |   - low
L0054 |   - medium
L0055 |   - high
L0056 | latency_requirement:
L0057 |   - realtime
L0058 |   - batch
L0059 | context_length:
L0060 |   - short
L0061 |   - long
L0062 | artifact_type:
L0063 |   - diff
L0064 |   - json
L0065 |   - image
L0066 |   - doc
L0067 |   - log
L0068 | 
L0069 | 
L0070 | ⸻
L0071 | 
L0072 | 🧱 Model Stack Mapping
```

```text
L0001 | A2rchitech Session Summary — NL Shells as Productivity Layer for Non-Technical Users
L0002 | 
L0003 | Date: 2026-01-22
L0004 | Topic: Natural-Language Shells (nlsh-style tools) as an A2rchitech Productivity Feature
L0005 | 
L0006 | ⸻
L0007 | 
L0008 | 1. Core Observation
L0009 | 
L0010 | We examined nlsh (Natural Language Shell) as a category:
L0011 | 
L0012 | A shell that accepts natural-language commands alongside traditional CLI commands, uses LLMs to translate intent into executable shell operations, and maintains environmental context.
L0013 | 
L0014 | This was framed as a productivity unlock for non-technical users inside A2rchitech.
L0015 | 
L0016 | ⸻
L0017 | 
L0018 | 2. What Makes nlsh-Type Tools Distinct
L0019 | 
L0020 | Key properties identified:
L0021 | 
L0022 | Embedded in the Terminal
L0023 | 	•	Not a chatbot wrapper.
L0024 | 	•	Replaces or augments the shell itself.
L0025 | 	•	NL prompts and raw bash/zsh commands coexist.
L0026 | 
L0027 | Context-Aware Execution
L0028 | 	•	Reads:
L0029 | 	•	Current directory
L0030 | 	•	File tree
L0031 | 	•	Shell history
L0032 | 	•	OS / runtime
L0033 | 	•	Generates commands scoped to the actual environment.
L0034 | 
L0035 | Safety Gate
L0036 | 	•	Typically previews generated commands.
L0037 | 	•	Requires confirmation before execution.
L0038 | 	•	Important for non-technical operators.
L0039 | 
L0040 | Backend-Agnostic LLM Use
L0041 | 	•	Can support:
L0042 | 	•	Cloud APIs
L0043 | 	•	Local models
L0044 | 	•	Aligns with A2rchitech’s multi-model routing layer.
L0045 | 
L0046 | Intent → Multi-Command Composition
L0047 | 	•	Produces full workflows:
L0048 | 	•	pipelines
L0049 | 	•	chained commands
L0050 | 	•	loops
L0051 | 	•	Goes beyond autocomplete/snippets.
L0052 | 
L0053 | ⸻
L0054 | 
L0055 | 3. Why This Is Strategically Important for A2rchitech
L0056 | 
L0057 | A. Human-Facing Capsule Layer
L0058 | 
L0059 | Fits directly into:
L0060 | 	•	HUMAN renderer capsules
L0061 | 	•	Beginner-friendly operational surfaces
L0062 | 	•	“Do this” → system performs safe infra ops
L0063 | 
L0064 | Example use cases:
L0065 | 	•	“Archive all logs older than 30 days.”
L0066 | 	•	“Start the dev cluster.”
L0067 | 	•	“Find the last crash report.”
L0068 | 	•	“Deploy staging.”
L0069 | 
L0070 | Non-technical users operate agent infrastructure without touching bash.
L0071 | 
L0072 | ⸻
L0073 | 
L0074 | B. Agent-Facing Tool Primitive
L0075 | 
L0076 | nlsh-style systems map cleanly into:
L0077 | 	•	/tools/shell-nl
L0078 | 	•	MCP-style wrappers
L0079 | 	•	CLI skills registry
L0080 | 
L0081 | Agents can:
L0082 | 	•	Ask for shell tasks in NL
L0083 | 	•	Receive deterministic shell plans
L0084 | 	•	Execute with sandboxing
L0085 | 
L0086 | This becomes:
L0087 | 
L0088 | A2rchitech Shell Skill Primitive
L0089 | 
L0090 | ⸻
L0091 | 
L0092 | C. Operator Onboarding Acceleration
L0093 | 
L0094 | For enterprise / consumer A2rchitech:
L0095 | 	•	Removes CLI intimidation.
L0096 | 	•	Makes OS-level automation approachable.
L0097 | 	•	Enables “AI-OS” narrative:
L0098 | 	•	speak intent
L0099 | 	•	system executes infrastructure.
L0100 | 
L0101 | ⸻
L0102 | 
L0103 | 4. Proposed A2rchitech Integration Pattern
L0104 | 
L0105 | Module: capsules/nlshell
L0106 | 
L0107 | Capabilities
L0108 | 	•	NL → command planner
L0109 | 	•	execution preview
L0110 | 	•	dry-run mode
L0111 | 	•	permission gating
L0112 | 	•	audit logs
L0113 | 	•	replayable scripts
L0114 | 	•	sandbox contexts
L0115 | 
L0116 | Hooks
L0117 | 	•	Memory layer (task recall)
L0118 | 	•	WIH metadata injection
L0119 | 	•	CODEBASE.md references
L0120 | 	•	agent-policy gates
L0121 | 	•	Repo Law permissions
L0122 | 
L0123 | ⸻
L0124 | 
L0125 | 5. Architectural Alignment
L0126 | 
L0127 | Strong fit with existing A2rchitech doctrine:
L0128 | 
L0129 | Principle	nlsh Alignment
L0130 | Agent Harness OS	Yes
L0131 | Capsule-based UX	Yes
L0132 | CLI as Skills	Yes
L0133 | Human + Agent split	Yes
L0134 | Guardrails / audit	Required
L0135 | Multi-LLM routing	Yes
L0136 | Spec-driven execution	Expand
L0137 | 
L0138 | 
L0139 | ⸻
L0140 | 
L0141 | 6. Design Direction for A2rchitech
L0142 | 
L0143 | Instead of copying nlsh directly:
L0144 | 
L0145 | Position A2rchitech as:
L0146 | 
L0147 | A governed, multi-agent, multi-model natural-language operations layer over the OS, repo, infra, and runtime.
L0148 | 
L0149 | nlsh = single-user shell
L0150 | 
L0151 | A2rchitech = fleet-scale agentic OS interface
L0152 | 
L0153 | ⸻
L0154 | 
L0155 | 7. Candidate Roadmap Slot
L0156 | 
L0157 | Add to A2rchitech roadmap:
L0158 | 
L0159 | Phase: Human-Intent Interface
L0160 | 	•	NL Shell Capsule
L0161 | 	•	NL File Explorer
L0162 | 	•	NL Git Operator
L0163 | 	•	NL Infra Operator
L0164 | 	•	NL Agent Launcher
L0165 | 	•	NL Data Ops
L0166 | 
L0167 | ⸻
L0168 | 
L0169 | 8. Key Takeaway
L0170 | 
L0171 | nlsh-style tools are not just convenience CLIs.
L0172 | 
L0173 | They represent:
L0174 | 
L0175 | The user-facing surface of an agentic operating system.
L0176 | 
L0177 | For A2rchitech, this becomes:
L0178 | 	•	a productivity moat
L0179 | 	•	onboarding funnel
L0180 | 	•	enterprise differentiator
L0181 | 	•	control plane for non-technical operators.
L0182 | 
L0183 | ⸻
L0184 | 
L0185 | Status
L0186 | 
L0187 | Saved as A2rchitech session concept:
```

```text
L0021 | This aligns with the broader A2rchitech goals:
L0022 | 	•	Agent harness OS
L0023 | 	•	Dynamic capsule rendering
L0024 | 	•	Artifact-first UX
L0025 | 	•	Human-in-the-loop steering
L0026 | 	•	Agent swarm orchestration with live UI mutation
L0027 | 
L0028 | ⸻
L0029 | 
L0030 | 2. What AG-UI Is (Extracted + Interpreted)
L0031 | 
L0032 | AG-UI = Agent ↔ User Interaction Protocol.
L0033 | 
L0034 | It is not a UI toolkit — it is a real-time, event-based interaction layer between:
L0035 | 	•	backend agents
L0036 | 	•	frontend surfaces (desktop/web/mobile)
L0037 | 	•	generated mini-apps/widgets
L0038 | 
L0039 | It standardizes:
L0040 | 	•	streaming agent output
L0041 | 	•	UI intents
L0042 | 	•	state synchronization
L0043 | 	•	interruptions / corrections
L0044 | 	•	artifact delivery
L0045 | 	•	user steering events
L0046 | 
L0047 | ⸻
L0048 | 
L0049 | 3. Core Properties Relevant to A2rchitech
L0050 | 
L0051 | 3.1 Event-Driven
L0052 | 
L0053 | Agents emit structured events, not only text:
L0054 | 	•	partial outputs
L0055 | 	•	UI mutation commands
L0056 | 	•	tool status
L0057 | 	•	artifact creation
L0058 | 	•	state diffs
L0059 | 
L0060 | 3.2 Bi-Directional Sync
L0061 | 	•	UI → Agent: user input, clicks, edits, commands
L0062 | 	•	Agent → UI: state updates, widget creation, progress
L0063 | 
L0064 | 3.3 Shared State Model
L0065 | 
L0066 | Supports:
L0067 | 	•	snapshots
L0068 | 	•	diffs
L0069 | 	•	reconciliation
L0070 | 	•	persistence
L0071 | 
L0072 | This maps directly to A2rchitech’s:
L0073 | 	•	memory layers
L0074 | 	•	artifact registry
L0075 | 	•	capsule state persistence
L0076 | 	•	Brain runtime state engine
L0077 | 
L0078 | 3.4 Streaming + Multimodal
L0079 | 	•	token streaming
L0080 | 	•	file attachments
L0081 | 	•	previews
L0082 | 	•	voice
L0083 | 	•	images
L0084 | 
L0085 | Matches A2rchitech’s:
L0086 | 	•	Artifact system
L0087 | 	•	Browser preview surfaces
L0088 | 	•	Render capsules
L0089 | 	•	Diff viewers
L0090 | 	•	Logs
L0091 | 	•	Tables
L0092 | 	•	Generated mini-apps
L0093 | 
L0094 | 3.5 Human-in-the-Loop Interrupts
L0095 | 
L0096 | Built-in affordances for:
L0097 | 	•	pause
L0098 | 	•	retry
L0099 | 	•	modify
L0100 | 	•	redirect
L0101 | 	•	approve
L0102 | 
L0103 | Direct alignment with:
L0104 | 	•	gated agents
L0105 | 	•	reviewer roles
L0106 | 	•	Security agent vetoes
L0107 | 	•	WIH enforcement loops
L0108 | 
L0109 | ⸻
L0110 | 
L0111 | 4. AG-UI in the Standards Stack
L0112 | 
L0113 | AG-UI occupies the Agent ↔ User layer.
L0114 | 
L0115 | Complementary protocols:
L0116 | 
L0117 | Layer	Protocol	Role
L0118 | Agent ↔ User	AG-UI	Dynamic UI & mini-apps
L0119 | Agent ↔ Tools	MCP	Tool execution
L0120 | Agent ↔ Agent	A2A	Coordination
L0121 | 
L0122 | This confirms:
L0123 | 
L0124 | AG-UI = the UI transport layer for A2rchitech capsules.
L0125 | 
L0126 | ⸻
L0127 | 
L0128 | 5. Strategic Implications for A2rchitech
L0129 | 
L0130 | AG-UI should become:
L0131 | 
L0132 | 5.1 Canonical UI Event Bus
L0133 | 
L0134 | All capsules (Terminal, Browser, Canvas, Inspector, Diff Viewer, Mini-Apps) should speak:
L0135 | 
L0136 | Agent ⇄ AG-UI ⇄ Capsule Renderer
L0137 | 
L0138 | 5.2 Mini-Apps as Generated Artifacts
L0139 | 
L0140 | Agents should emit:
L0141 | 	•	UI schemas
L0142 | 	•	layouts
L0143 | 	•	control definitions
L0144 | 	•	reactive state bindings
L0145 | 
L0146 | which A2rchitech renders inside:
L0147 | 	•	WindowedBrowserView
L0148 | 	•	Canvas capsules
L0149 | 	•	Side panels
L0150 | 	•	Inspector tabs
L0151 | 
L0152 | 5.3 Artifact-First UI Philosophy (Reconfirmed)
L0153 | 
L0154 | AG-UI reinforces prior architecture:
L0155 | 
L0156 | Artifacts:
L0157 | 
L0158 | Artifact {
L0159 |   id
L0160 |   type: diff | preview | doc | image | log | table | ui
L0161 |   source_agent
L0162 |   session_id
L0163 | }
L0164 | 
L0165 | UI = one of many artifact types.
L0166 | 
L0167 | ⸻
L0168 | 
L0169 | 6. Required A2rchitech Integration Points
L0170 | 
L0171 | This session implies the following new platform requirements:
L0172 | 
L0173 | 6.1 AG-UI Gateway Service
L0174 | 
L0175 | New service:
L0176 | 
L0177 | services/ui_gateway/
L0178 | 
L0179 | Responsibilities:
L0180 | 	•	websocket / SSE transport
L0181 | 	•	event routing
L0182 | 	•	state sync
L0183 | 	•	replay
L0184 | 	•	auth / gating
L0185 | 	•	persistence hooks
L0186 | 
L0187 | 6.2 Capsule Renderer Extensions
L0188 | 
L0189 | Each capsule:
L0190 | 	•	subscribes to AG-UI streams
L0191 | 	•	renders declarative UI payloads
L0192 | 	•	exposes UI → Agent event hooks
L0193 | 
L0194 | 6.3 UI Schema Registry
L0195 | 
L0196 | Agents must emit versioned UI definitions:
L0197 | 
L0198 | /spec/Contracts/ui_schema.json
L0199 | 
L0200 | For:
L0201 | 	•	generated widgets
L0202 | 	•	control panels
L0203 | 	•	dashboards
L0204 | 	•	workflow editors
L0205 | 
L0206 | 6.4 Brain Runtime Integration
L0207 | 
L0208 | Brain registry must include:
L0209 | 	•	AG-UI-capable runtimes
L0210 | 	•	streaming state handlers
L0211 | 	•	UI emitters
L0212 | 
L0213 | ⸻
L0214 | 
L0215 | 7. Persistence Rule Confirmed
L0216 | 
L0217 | Joe reinforced:
L0218 | 
L0219 | Conversations related to A2rchitech are to be summarized into a single canonical buildout chat, and the phrase
L0220 | gizzi save a2r session
L0221 | is the persistent command that triggers this.
L0222 | 
L0223 | This session has now been captured accordingly.
L0224 | 
L0225 | ⸻
L0226 | 
L0227 | 8. Items to Merge into Canonical A2rchitech Buildout
L0228 | 
L0229 | This Markdown should be merged under sections:
L0230 | 	•	UI Architecture
L0231 | 	•	Capsule Rendering Layer
L0232 | 	•	Protocols & Standards
L0233 | 	•	AG-UI Transport

L0235 | 	•	Human-in-the-Loop Controls
L0236 | 
L0237 | ⸻
L0238 | 
L0239 | 9. Net-New Strategic Conclusion
L0240 | 
L0241 | AG-UI validates A2rchitech’s trajectory:
L0242 | 	•	dynamic agent-generated interfaces
L0243 | 	•	event-driven UI
L0244 | 	•	capsule-based OS metaphor
L0245 | 	•	artifact-first rendering
L0246 | 	•	swarm-friendly orchestration surfaces
L0247 |
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — 2026-01-26  
L0002 | **Topic:** Agentic Context Engineering & Long-Running Agent Architecture  
L0003 | **Trigger:** gizzi save a2r session
L0004 | 
L0005 | ---
L0006 | 
L0007 | ## 1. Session Focus
L0008 | 
L0009 | This session analyzed a long-form X thread by Lance Martin outlining **design patterns emerging across successful production agents** (Claude Code, Manus, Cursor, Amp, etc.), with emphasis on:
L0010 | 
L0011 | - Context as a scarce resource
L0012 | - OS-level agent control
L0013 | - Filesystem-based memory
L0014 | - Progressive disclosure of tools
L0015 | - Sub-agent isolation
L0016 | - Long-running orchestration loops
L0017 | - Learned / evolving agent memory
L0018 | 
L0019 | The synthesis explicitly mapped these ideas into **foundational laws for A2rchitech’s agent-OS vision**.
L0020 | 
L0021 | ---
L0022 | 
L0023 | ## 2. Core First-Principles Conclusion
L0024 | 
L0025 | > **Agents are memory-allocation systems that reason incidentally.**
L0026 | 
L0027 | All effective architectures converge on:
L0028 | - Minimizing token growth
L0029 | - Externalizing state
L0030 | - Delaying tokenization
L0031 | - Isolating interference
L0032 | - Amortizing cost through caching
L0033 | - Moving expressivity into OS primitives
L0034 | 
L0035 | ---
L0036 | 
L0037 | ## 3. Observed Industry Patterns (Extracted)
L0038 | 
L0039 | ### 3.1 Context Scarcity
L0040 | - Context windows degrade with size.
L0041 | - Tool schemas and traces overload attention budgets.
L0042 | - Long-running tasks are growing faster than usable context.
L0043 | 
L0044 | ### 3.2 “Give Agents a Computer”
L0045 | Agents increasingly:
L0046 | - Live on a real or virtual OS
L0047 | - Use:
L0048 |   - Filesystems as persistent memory
L0049 |   - Shells as compressed action interfaces
L0050 |   - Code execution to chain steps without re-tokenizing outputs
L0051 | 
L0052 | **Primitive shift:**
L0053 | LLM → CLI → OS → World.
L0054 | 
L0055 | ---
L0056 | 
L0057 | ### 3.3 Collapsing Tool Explosion
L0058 | Instead of hundreds of tools:
L0059 | - Successful agents use <20 atomic tools
L0060 | - Expressivity pushed downward:
L0061 |   - bash → CLIs → scripts → generated code
L0062 | 
L0063 | ---
L0064 | 
L0065 | ### 3.4 Progressive Disclosure
L0066 | Tools and skills are:
L0067 | - Indexed first
L0068 | - Fully loaded only on demand
L0069 | 
L0070 | Filesystem becomes a **tool registry**.
L0071 | 
L0072 | SKILL.md pattern:
L0073 | - YAML header always loaded
L0074 | - Full skill text pulled conditionally
L0075 | 
L0076 | ---
L0077 | 
L0078 | ### 3.5 Filesystem as External Memory
L0079 | Agents:
L0080 | - Dump traces, plans, tool outputs to disk
L0081 | - Re-ingest selectively
L0082 | - Summarize only when necessary
L0083 | 
L0084 | Avoids irreversible compression early.
L0085 | 
L0086 | ---
L0087 | 
L0088 | ### 3.6 Prompt Caching as a Production Constraint
L0089 | - Cache hit rate is a primary KPI.
L0090 | - Mutating history freely destroys economics.
L0091 | - Long-running agents must preserve prefix stability.
L0092 | 
L0093 | ---
L0094 | 
L0095 | ### 3.7 Context Isolation with Sub-Agents
L0096 | Sub-agents used for:
L0097 | - Parallelism
L0098 | - Verification
L0099 | - Map-reduce tasks
L0100 | - Long-running loops
L0101 | 
L0102 | Ralph Loop:
L0103 | - Plan lives in files
L0104 | - Workers iterate until satisfied
L0105 | - Progress tracked externally (git / logs)
L0106 | 
L0107 | ---
L0108 | 
L0109 | ### 3.8 Evolving Context Over Time
L0110 | Agents learn in token-space:
L0111 | 
L0112 | Pipeline:
L0113 | 1. Log trajectories
L0114 | 2. Reflect
L0115 | 3. Distill heuristics
L0116 | 4. Append to memory files / playbooks / skills
L0117 | 
L0118 | Memory becomes **append-only living prompts**.
L0119 | 
L0120 | ---
L0121 | 
L0122 | ### 3.9 Learned Context Management
L0123 | Research trend:
L0124 | - Recursive Language Models
L0125 | - Agents trained to manage retrieval & compaction themselves
L0126 | - External scaffolding gradually absorbed into models
L0127 | 
L0128 | ---
L0129 | 
L0130 | ### 3.10 Multi-Agent Coordination Pressure
L0131 | Problems:
L0132 | - Conflicting parallel decisions
L0133 | - No shared discourse layer
L0134 | - No standard observability
L0135 | 
L0136 | Early solutions:
L0137 | - Mayor / orchestrator agents
L0138 | - Git-backed coordination
L0139 | - Merge queues
L0140 | 
L0141 | ---
L0142 | 
L0143 | ## 4. A2rchitech-Relevant Architectural Laws
L0144 | 
L0145 | Derived explicitly for your platform:
L0146 | 
L0147 | ### LAW-01: OS Is the Action Plane
L0148 | All agents must operate primarily through:
L0149 | - Files
L0150 | - Processes
L0151 | - Shells
L0152 | - Git / logs
L0153 | 
L0154 | ---
L0155 | 
L0156 | ### LAW-02: Narrow Tools, Wide Compute
L0157 | Keep tool APIs minimal.
L0158 | Push expressivity into:
L0159 | - Code
L0160 | - CLI composition
L0161 | - Local scripts
L0162 | 
L0163 | ---
L0164 | 
L0165 | ### LAW-03: Filesystem = Primary Memory
L0166 | Context window is cache.
L0167 | Disk is truth.
L0168 | 
L0169 | ---
L0170 | 
L0171 | ### LAW-04: Progressive Disclosure Everywhere
L0172 | Tools, skills, plans, logs, and MCP servers are indexed—not injected.
L0173 | 
L0174 | ---
L0175 | 
L0176 | ### LAW-05: Cache-Aware Context Mutation
L0177 | Prefix stability must be preserved.
L0178 | Context rewriting must be deliberate.
L0179 | 
L0180 | ---
L0181 | 
L0182 | ### LAW-06: Context Isolation by Default
L0183 | Parallel work always uses:
L0184 | - Separate windows
L0185 | - Shared files
L0186 | - Explicit coordination artifacts
L0187 | 
L0188 | ---
L0189 | 
L0190 | ### LAW-07: Append-Only Learning
L0191 | No silent overwrites.
L0192 | Memories evolve through reflection logs and distilled playbooks.
L0193 | 
L0194 | ---
L0195 | 
L0196 | ### LAW-08: Orchestration Requires New Abstractions
L0197 | A2rchitech will require:
L0198 | - Agent debuggers
L0199 | - Observability UIs
L0200 | - Loop inspectors
L0201 | - Failure recovery systems
L0202 | - Human-in-the-loop gates
L0203 | 
L0204 | ---
L0205 | 
L0206 | ## 5. Open Research Directions Logged
L0207 | 
L0208 | - Learned retrieval vs manual compaction
L0209 | - Offline “sleep-time compute”
L0210 | - Recursive self-management
L0211 | - Multi-agent conflict resolution protocols
L0212 | - Standards for observability & debugging
L0213 | 
L0214 | ---
L0215 | 
L0216 | ## 6. Session Outcome
L0217 | 
L0218 | This session establishes:
L0219 | 
L0220 | **A2rchitech = Agentic Operating System for Long-Running, Context-Constrained Swarms**
L0221 | 
L0222 | Where:
L0223 | - Memory is external
L0224 | - Agents are modular
L0225 | - Learning is append-only
L0226 | - Orchestration is first-class
L0227 | - CLI is the universal interface
L0228 | - Context is budgeted
L0229 | 
L0230 | ---
L0231 | 
L0232 | ## 7. Status
L0233 | 
L0234 | Saved for later consolidation into the canonical A2rchitech buildout thread.
L0235 | 
L0236 | ---
```

```text
L0177 | 4. CLI Runtime Lifecycle Model
L0178 | 
L0179 | CLI runtimes follow:
L0180 | 
L0181 | Not Installed
L0182 |    ↓
L0183 | Install (PTY subprocess)
L0184 |    ↓
L0185 | Auth Required (PTY subprocess)
L0186 |    ↓
L0187 | Ready
L0188 |    ↓
L0189 | Launch → Brain session
L0190 | 
L0191 | Each phase:
L0192 | 	•	Runs as subprocess
L0193 | 	•	Streams output through PTY
L0194 | 	•	Supports stdin
L0195 | 	•	Emits SSE to UI
L0196 | 	•	No simulated timers
L0197 |

L0330 | 1. CLI Subprocess Wrapping = Real Power
L0331 | 
L0332 | Before:

L0335 | 	•	Some manual local tooling

L0342 | 	•	streamed over SSE
L0343 | 	•	routed into:
L0344 | 	•	Chat
L0345 | 	•	Cowork
L0346 | 	•	Conductor
L0347 | 	•	Terminal panes
L0348 | 	•	install/auth/launch handled centrally

L0351 |

L0366 |

L0368 | 
L0369 | That is huge.

L0600 | A2rchitech Session Addendum — Parallel Coding GUIs (1Code) vs Brain Terminal Architecture
L0601 | 
L0602 | Date: 2026-01-26
L0603 | Topic: Parallel AI coding UIs, terminal-centric agent orchestration, and enterprise scaling posture
L0604 | 
L0605 | ⸻
L0606 | 
L0607 | 1. What “1Code” Is Trying To Do
L0608 | 
L0609 | 1Code positions itself as:
L0610 | 	•	A GUI workspace for:
L0611 | 	•	Parallel AI coding agents
L0612 | 	•	Research tasks
L0613 | 	•	Repo exploration
L0614 | 	•	Code diffs
L0615 | 	•	Task delegation
L0616 | 	•	Focused on:
L0617 | 	•	Developer-centric orchestration
L0618 | 	•	Visual agent panels
L0619 | 	•	Running multiple coding sessions concurrently
L0620 | 	•	Switching between models
L0621 | 
L0622 | Functional primitives inferred:
L0623 | 
L0624 | Capability	Description
L0625 | Multi-agent coding	Spawn several AI coding sessions
L0626 | Task panels	Parallel tasks per repo
L0627 | Chat+code hybrid	IDE-like interface
L0628 | Model switching	Run different LLMs
L0629 | Research panes	Agents exploring docs/repos
L0630 | Central GUI	Visual workspace
L0631 | 
L0632 | This is essentially:
L0633 | 
L0634 | A GUI-first coding swarm controller.
L0635 | 
L0636 | ⸻
L0637 | 
L0638 | 2. What A2rchitech Already Has (Or Now Surpasses)
L0639 | 
L0640 | From the Brain Runtime overhaul + CLI wrapping + terminal streaming work:
L0641 | 
L0642 | A2rchitech’s Terminal-Centric Stack
L0643 | 
L0644 | Layer	Status
L0645 | Kernel-spawned PTY subprocesses	✅
L0646 | CLI wrapping (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, Goose, etc.)	✅
L0647 | SSE event bus	✅

L0649 | Cowork + Conductor swarm views	✅

L0658 | Architectural Difference
L0659 | 
L0660 | 1Code:
L0661 |

L0663 | 
L0664 | A2rchitech:

L0670 |

L0672 | 
L0673 | ⸻
L0674 | 
L0675 | 3. What Using the Terminal Actually Gives A2rchitech
L0676 | 
L0677 | The terminal approach is not cosmetic. It unlocks:
L0678 | 
L0679 | ✅ Vendor-agnostic agent control
L0680 | 
L0681 | Any CLI can become a Brain runtime:
L0682 | 	•	Claude Code
L0683 | 	•	Aider
L0684 | 	•	Codex
L0685 | 	•	Qwen CLI
L0686 | 	•	Cursor CLI
L0687 | 	•	OpenCode
L0688 | 	•	Goose
L0689 | 
L0690 | No SDK coupling.
L0691 | 
L0692 | ⸻
L0693 | 
L0694 | ✅ Horizontal swarm scaling
L0695 | 
L0696 | Because everything is:
L0697 | 	•	subprocess-based
L0698 | 	•	session-scoped
L0699 | 	•	event-driven
L0700 | 	•	streamable
L0701 | 
L0702 | You can:
L0703 | 	•	run 5 agents locally
L0704 | 	•	run 200 on a cluster
L0705 | 	•	migrate to remote execution later
L0706 | 	•	containerize runtimes
L0707 | 	•	dispatch via queue
L0708 | 
L0709 | ⸻
L0710 | 
L0711 | ✅ Deterministic auditing
L0712 | 
L0713 | Every CLI event is:
L0714 | 	•	streamed
L0715 | 	•	logged
L0716 | 	•	replayable
L0717 | 	•	attachable to artifacts
L0718 | 	•	linked to plans
L0719 | 
L0720 | 1Code does not appear to have:
L0721 | 	•	audit ledger
L0722 | 	•	spec enforcement
L0723 | 	•	contract gates
L0724 | 	•	law layer
L0725 | 
L0726 | ⸻
L0727 | 
L0728 | ✅ Enterprise path
L0729 | 
L0730 | With:
L0731 | 	•	policy enforcement
L0732 | 	•	runtime registry
L0733 | 	•	execution scopes
L0734 | 	•	tool sandboxing
L0735 | 	•	auth flows
L0736 | 	•	memory isolation
L0737 | 	•	artifact stores
L0738 | 
L0739 | A2rchitech maps cleanly to:
L0740 | 
L0741 | Palantir-style agent platforms.
L0742 | 
L0743 | 1Code does not.
L0744 | 
L0745 | ⸻
L0746 | 
L0747 | 4. Feature Parity Matrix — 1Code vs A2rchitech
L0748 | 
L0749 | Capability	1Code	A2rchitech
L0750 | Parallel coding agents	✅	✅
L0751 | Visual workspace	✅	✅
L0752 | CLI wrapping	❌	✅
L0753 | Kernel orchestrator	❌	✅
L0754 | Policy gates	❌	✅
L0755 | Runtime registry	❌	✅
L0756 | Install/auth flows	❌	✅
L0757 | Subprocess isolation	❌	✅
L0758 | SSE event bus	❌	✅
L0759 | Artifact graph	❌	🟡
L0760 | Spec enforcement	❌	✅
L0761 | Session replay	❌	🔜
L0762 | Enterprise governance	❌	✅
L0763 | Skill marketplace	❌	🔜
L0764 |

L0766 | ⸻
L0767 | 
L0768 | 5. Which One Scales?
L0769 | 
L0770 | Verdict: A2rchitech scales.
L0771 | 
L0772 | Reason:
L0773 | 	•	kernel-owned lifecycle
L0774 | 	•	PTY-level isolation
L0775 | 	•	runtime registry
L0776 | 	•	portable execution model
L0777 | 	•	policy system
L0778 | 	•	swarm orchestration layer
L0779 | 	•	spec/law governance
L0780 | 	•	observability
L0781 | 	•	marketplace primitives
L0782 | 
L0783 | 1Code is an interface product.
L0784 | 
L0785 | A2rchitech is an agent operating system.
L0786 | 
L0787 | Different class.
L0788 | 
L0789 | ⸻
L0790 | 
L0791 | 6. What 1Code Still Gives Us Strategically
L0792 | 
L0793 | We should steal the UX ideas, not the architecture:
L0794 | 
L0795 | UI Ideas to Adopt
L0796 | 	•	Parallel agent panels
L0797 | 	•	Repo-scoped agents
L0798 | 	•	Task swimlanes
L0799 | 	•	Diff viewers
L0800 | 	•	Research tabs
L0801 | 	•	Agent grouping
L0802 | 	•	Live code streams
L0803 | 	•	Status chips
L0804 | 	•	Work graphs
L0805 | 
L0806 | These become:
L0807 | 
L0808 | Shell projections over Brain sessions.
L0809 | 
L0810 | Not separate systems.
L0811 | 
L0812 | ⸻
L0813 | 
L0814 | 7. New Requirements Inserted Into A2rchitech Roadmap
L0815 | 
L0816 | 🔴 R-A2R-CLI-01 — Swarm Panels
L0817 | 
L0818 | Shell UI must render:
L0819 | 	•	multiple simultaneous CLI brains
L0820 | 	•	grouped by project
L0821 | 	•	with collapse/expand
L0822 | 	•	per-agent terminals
L0823 | 
L0824 | ⸻
L0825 | 
L0826 | 🔴 R-A2R-SWARM-02 — Conductor Graph
L0827 | 
L0828 | Conductor view upgraded to:
L0829 | 	•	DAG of tasks
L0830 | 	•	edges = dependencies
L0831 | 	•	node = agent
L0832 | 	•	runtime = brain id
L0833 | 	•	artifact outputs attached
L0834 | 
L0835 | ⸻
L0836 | 
L0837 | 🔴 R-A2R-ARTIFACT-03 — Artifact Graph
L0838 | 
L0839 | Every:
L0840 | 	•	diff
L0841 | 	•	patch
L0842 | 	•	report
L0843 | 	•	test run
L0844 | 
L0845 | becomes:
L0846 | 
L0847 | Artifact {
L0848 | id
L0849 | session
L0850 | runtime
L0851 | produced_by
L0852 | inputs
L0853 | outputs
L0854 | }
L0855 | 
L0856 | ⸻
L0857 | 
L0858 | 🔴 R-A2R-REPLAY-04 — Session Replay
L0859 | 
L0860 | All CLI sessions replayable:
L0861 | 	•	terminal output
L0862 | 	•	chat
L0863 | 	•	tool calls
L0864 | 	•	diffs
L0865 | 	•	timelines
L0866 | 
L0867 | ⸻
L0868 | 
L0869 | 🔴 R-A2R-MARKET-05 — Skill Marketplace Integration
L0870 | 
L0871 | Tie terminal agents to:
L0872 | 	•	installable skills
L0873 | 	•	policy sets
L0874 | 	•	budget limits
L0875 | 	•	cost meters
L0876 | 
L0877 | ⸻
L0878 | 
L0879 | 8. Strategic Conclusion (Saved)
L0880 | 
L0881 | A2rchitech is not competing with 1Code.
L0882 | 
L0883 | A2rchitech absorbs 1Code-class tooling as a UI layer over a stronger kernel.
L0884 | 
L0885 | Terminal-driven subprocess agents + law-governed orchestration is the winning architecture.
L0886 |

L0895 | A2rchitech Session Addendum — Parallel Coding GUIs (1Code) vs Brain Terminal Architecture
L0896 |

L0901 | 
L0902 | 1. What “1Code” Is Trying To Do
L0903 | 
L0904 | 1Code positions itself as:
L0905 | 	•	A GUI workspace for:
L0906 | 	•	Parallel AI coding agents
L0907 | 	•	Research tasks
L0908 | 	•	Repo exploration
L0909 | 	•	Code diffs
L0910 | 	•	Task delegation
L0911 | 	•	Focused on:
L0912 | 	•	Developer-centric orchestration
L0913 | 	•	Visual agent panels
L0914 | 	•	Running multiple coding sessions concurrently
L0915 | 	•	Switching between models
L0916 | 
L0917 | Functional primitives inferred:
L0918 | 
L0919 | Capability	Description
L0920 | Multi-agent coding	Spawn several AI coding sessions
L0921 | Task panels	Parallel tasks per repo
L0922 | Chat+code hybrid	IDE-like interface
L0923 | Model switching	Run different LLMs
L0924 | Research panes	Agents exploring docs/repos
L0925 | Central GUI	Visual workspace
L0926 | 
L0927 | This is essentially:
L0928 | 
L0929 | A GUI-first coding swarm controller.
L0930 | 
L0931 | ⸻
L0932 | 
L0933 | 2. What A2rchitech Already Has (Or Now Surpasses)
L0934 | 
L0935 | From the Brain Runtime overhaul + CLI wrapping + terminal streaming work:
L0936 | 
L0937 | A2rchitech’s Terminal-Centric Stack
L0938 | 
L0939 | Layer	Status
L0940 | Kernel-spawned PTY subprocesses	✅
L0941 | CLI wrapping (Claude Code, Aider, Codex, Goose, etc.)	✅
L0942 | SSE event bus	✅
L0943 | Session orchestration	✅
L0944 | Cowork + Conductor swarm views	✅
L0945 | Timeline + lifecycle tracking	✅
L0946 | Tool call mediation	✅
L0947 | Artifact pipeline	🟡 partial
L0948 | Policy gates	✅
L0949 | Law/spec layer	✅
L0950 | Skill marketplace	🔜
L0951 | Runtime registry + presets	✅

L0953 | Architectural Difference

L0956 | 
L0957 | GUI → spawn agents → run tasks
L0958 | 
L0959 | A2rchitech:
L0960 | 
L0961 | Kernel → Brain runtime → PTY subprocess → Event bus → UI projections
L0962 |                                   ↘ Law / Policy / Memory
L0963 | 
L0964 | This is materially stronger:
L0965 | 
L0966 | Kernel-first beats GUI-first for scale.

L1341 | 📄 /spec/Deltas/DELTA-A2R-SWARM-UX.md
L1342 |

L1344 |

L1346 | 
L1347 | ⸻
L1348 | 
L1349 | Requirements
L1350 | 
L1351 | R-SWARM-01

L1353 | 
L1354 | R-SWARM-02
L1355 | Cowork panel must group sessions by project.
L1356 |

L1361 | session_id
L1362 | status
L1363 | artifacts[]
L1364 | policy_state

L1378 |

L1401 |

L1432 | ⸻
L1433 | 
L1434 | 🚀 Strategic Verdict (Locked)
L1435 | 	•	1Code-style GUIs = interface tier.
L1436 | 	•	A2rchitech = operating system tier.
L1437 | 	•	Terminal-driven brains + law layer + artifact DAGs scale horizontally.
L1438 | 	•	Kernel-owned orchestration beats IDE-embedded control.
L1439 | 
L1440 | ⸻
L1441 |

L1448 | 
L1449 | 📦 A2rchitech Addendum — Swarm Execution at Scale
L1450 |

L1457 | 
L1458 | ④ ROADMAP TRANCHE — From Terminal to Planet-Scale Swarms
L1459 | 
L1460 | This defines how A2rchitech evolves from local PTYs → distributed compute fabric.
L1461 | 
L1462 | ⸻
L1463 |

L1466 | Status: partially complete.
L1467 |

L1469 | 	•	Kernel-owned Brain sessions

L1488 | 	•	Replay endpoints

L1490 | 	•	Parallel CLI execution
L1491 | 	•	Policy gates enforced before apply

L1555 | ⑤ CI GATING & LAW-LAYER ENFORCEMENT
L1556 | 
L1557 | This is where A2rchitech becomes safer than IDE plugins.
L1558 | 
L1559 | ⸻
L1560 | 
L1561 | 🔐 Pipeline Topology
L1562 | 
L1563 | Agent CLI ─▶ Kernel ─▶ Artifact DAG ─▶ Policy Engine ─▶ CI Runner ─▶ Apply
L1564 | 
L1565 | 
L1566 | ⸻
L1567 | 
L1568 | ⸻
L1569 | 
L1570 | 🔷 Policy Gates
L1571 | 
L1572 | Every artifact passes:
L1573 | 
L1574 | Gate	Rule
L1575 | L-001	No secrets
L1576 | L-002	No destructive ops
L1577 | L-003	Tests must pass
L1578 | L-004	Spec deltas updated
L1579 | L-005	ADR required
L1580 | L-006	Reviewer agent approval
L1581 | 
L1582 | 
L1583 | ⸻
L1584 | 
L1585 | ⸻
L1586 | 
L1587 | 🔷 CI Execution
L1588 | 
L1589 | CI itself is a Brain runtime.
L1590 | 
L1591 | Examples:
L1592 | 	•	ci-test-runner
L1593 | 	•	security-audit-agent
L1594 | 	•	license-checker
L1595 | 	•	diff-validator
L1596 | 
L1597 | They run as CLI brains.

L1604 |

L1606 |

L1610 |

L1653 | 	•	trust domain
L1654 | 	•	cost tier
L1655 |

L1659 | 	•	priority queue
L1660 | 	•	speculative fan-out
L1661 | 	•	redundancy for consensus

L1855 | ⸻
L1856 | 
L1857 | 📦 A2rchitech Addendum — Swarm Execution Protocol Layer
L1858 | 
L1859 | Topic:
L1860 | ① Swarm Protocol Wire Format
L1861 | ② Worker Handshake & Registration
L1862 | ③ Artifact CAS Schema
L1863 |

L1865 | 
L1866 | ⸻
L1867 | 
L1868 | ⸻
L1869 | 
L1870 | ① SWARM PROTOCOL — WIRE FORMAT
L1871 | 
L1872 | This defines how kernels, workers, and agents talk at runtime.
L1873 | 
L1874 | This is the foundation that allows:
L1875 | 	•	local PTYs
L1876 | 	•	remote VMs
L1877 | 	•	Kubernetes pods
L1878 | 	•	federated clusters
L1879 | 
L1880 | to all participate in the same swarm.
L1881 | 
L1882 | ⸻
L1883 | 
L1884 | 🔷 Transport
L1885 | 
L1886 | Primary:
L1887 | 	•	gRPC (binary)
L1888 | 	•	HTTP+JSON fallback
L1889 | 	•	SSE for UI
L1890 | 	•	WebSocket for interactive terminals
L1891 | 
L1892 | ⸻
L1893 | 
L1894 | ⸻
L1895 | 
L1896 | 🔷 Core Message Envelope
L1897 | 
L1898 | Every message uses a signed envelope:
L1899 | 
L1900 | {
L1901 |   "version": "1.0",
L1902 |   "swarm_id": "swarm-abc",
L1903 |   "session_id": "sess-123",
L1904 |   "job_id": "job-42",
L1905 |   "sender": "kernel|worker|agent",
L1906 |   "timestamp": 1737399221,
L1907 |   "signature": "ed25519:...",
L1908 |   "payload": { }
L1909 | }
L1910 | 
L1911 | 
L1912 | ⸻
L1913 | 
L1914 | ⸻
L1915 |

L1926 | }

L2005 | ② WORKER HANDSHAKE & REGISTRATION SPEC
L2006 | 
L2007 | This defines how machines join the swarm.
L2008 | 
L2009 | ⸻
L2010 | 
L2011 | ⸻
L2012 | 
L2013 | 🔷 Registration Flow
L2014 | 
L2015 | workerctl register
L2016 |       ↓
L2017 | Kernel issues challenge
L2018 |       ↓
L2019 | Worker signs nonce
L2020 |       ↓
L2021 | Capabilities exchange
L2022 |       ↓
L2023 | Worker admitted to pool
L2024 | 
L2025 | 
L2026 | ⸻
L2027 | 
L2028 | ⸻
L2029 | 
L2030 | 🔷 Worker Identity Document
L2031 | 
L2032 | {
L2033 |   "worker_id": "wk-us-east-01",
L2034 |   "hostname": "gpu-node-1",
L2035 |   "platform": "linux",
L2036 |   "runtimes": ["claude-cli", "ollama"],
L2037 |   "resources": {
L2038 |     "cpu": 64,
L2039 |     "gpu": "A100",
L2040 |     "memory_gb": 256
L2041 |   },
L2042 |   "trust_domain": "corp-prod"
L2043 | }
L2044 | 
L2045 | 
L2046 | ⸻
L2047 | 
L2048 | ⸻
L2049 | 
L2050 | 🔷 Handshake API
L2051 | 
L2052 | POST /v1/swarm/workers/register
L2053 | 
L2054 | {
L2055 |   "public_key": "ed25519:...",
L2056 |   "capabilities": { ... }
L2057 | }
L2058 | 
L2059 | 
L2060 | ⸻
L2061 | 
L2062 | Kernel Response
L2063 | 
L2064 | {
L2065 |   "worker_id": "wk-01",
L2066 |   "cert": "mTLS-cert",
L2067 |   "expires_at": 1737499999
L2068 | }
L2069 | 
L2070 | 
L2071 | ⸻
L2072 | 
L2073 | ⸻
L2074 | 
L2075 | 🔷 Lease Heartbeats
L2076 | 
L2077 | POST /v1/swarm/workers/heartbeat
L2078 | 
L2079 | {
L2080 |   "worker_id": "wk-01",
L2081 |   "jobs_running": 2,
L2082 |   "load": 0.71
L2083 | }
L2084 | 
L2085 | Missed heartbeats → eviction.
L2086 | 
L2087 | ⸻
L2088 | 
L2089 | ⸻
L2090 | 
L2091 | 🔷 Security
L2092 | 	•	mTLS everywhere
L2093 | 	•	ephemeral certs
L2094 | 	•	signed job envelopes
L2095 | 	•	sandbox per runtime
L2096 | 	•	seccomp profiles
L2097 | 	•	filesystem namespaces
L2098 | 
L2099 | ⸻
L2100 | 
L2101 | ⸻
L2102 | 
L2103 | 🔷 Revocation
L2104 | 
L2105 | {
L2106 |   "type": "worker.revoke",
L2107 |   "worker_id": "wk-01"
L2108 | }
L2109 | 
L2110 | Worker immediately stops accepting leases.
L2111 | 
L2112 | ⸻
L2113 | 
L2114 | ⸻
L2115 | 
L2116 | ⸻
L2117 | 
L2118 | ③ ARTIFACT CAS (CONTENT ADDRESSABLE STORAGE)
L2119 | 
L2120 | This is the truth ledger of A2rchitech.
L2121 | 
L2122 | Everything agents create lives here.
L2123 | 
L2124 | ⸻
L2125 | 
L2126 | ⸻
L2127 | 
L2128 | 🔷 Artifact ID
L2129 | 
L2130 | cas:sha256:<hash>
L2131 | 
L2132 | Immutable.
L2133 | 
L2134 | ⸻
L2135 | 
L2136 | ⸻
L2137 | 
L2138 | 🔷 Artifact Record
L2139 | 
L2140 | {
L2141 |   "artifact_id": "cas:sha256:abc",
L2142 |   "kind": "diff|log|binary|dataset|report",
L2143 |   "producer_job": "job-42",
L2144 |   "swarm_id": "swarm-abc",
L2145 |   "created_at": 1737399000,
L2146 |   "size_bytes": 22913,
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — Browser Control Frontier (browser-use vs Playwright)
L0002 | 
L0003 | **Date:** 2026-01-26  
L0004 | **Topic:** Agent browser runtime strategy (MCP + CLI tool gateway)  
L0005 | **Stance captured:** “browser-use is the new frontier; do not use Playwright directly.”
L0006 | 
L0007 | ---
L0008 | 
L0009 | ## 1) Core claim under discussion
L0010 | You want A2rchitech’s browser capability to standardize on **browser-use as the primary browser skill**, invoked through a **tool gateway** (MCP and/or CLI-wrapped tools), and you want to **avoid Playwright** because you believe it is deprecated and slow.
L0011 | 
L0012 | ---
L0013 | 
L0014 | ## 2) What is factually true (important distinctions)
L0015 | 
L0016 | ### 2.1 Playwright is not “deprecated” as a framework
L0017 | - The **Playwright framework** remains actively released and maintained (official release notes continue to publish versions).  [oai_citation:0‡Playwright](https://playwright.dev/docs/release-notes?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0018 | - What *is* being retired is **Microsoft Playwright Testing** (Azure-hosted testing product), not the Playwright automation framework itself.  [oai_citation:1‡Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/playwright/includes/retirement-banner?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0019 | 
L0020 | ### 2.2 browser-use is a strong agent-facing control layer, but often depends on Playwright
L0021 | - Multiple sources describe **browser-use running “under the hood” on Playwright** (i.e., Playwright may still be a dependency even if you never author Playwright code).  [oai_citation:2‡Bright Data](https://brightdata.com/blog/ai/browser-use-with-scraping-browser?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0022 | 
L0023 | **Implication:** “Do not use Playwright” can mean two different things:
L0024 | 1) **Do not write Playwright code** (compatible with browser-use-first).  
L0025 | 2) **Do not include Playwright as any dependency** (may be incompatible if browser-use requires it in your chosen mode).
L0026 | 
L0027 | A2rchitech can satisfy (1) immediately. Satisfying (2) depends on browser-use’s backend options and your selected runtime mode.
L0028 | 
L0029 | ---
L0030 | 
L0031 | ## 3) browser-use integration surfaces that match A2rchitech’s tool gateway model
L0032 | 
L0033 | ### 3.1 MCP server (cloud HTTP + local stdio)
L0034 | - browser-use provides a hosted MCP endpoint: `https://api.browser-use.com/mcp`.  [oai_citation:3‡docs.cloud.browser-use.com](https://docs.cloud.browser-use.com/usage/mcp-server?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0035 | - It also documents a local stdio MCP mode via `uvx browser-use --mcp`.  [oai_citation:4‡docs.cloud.browser-use.com](https://docs.cloud.browser-use.com/usage/mcp-server?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0036 | 
L0037 | ### 3.2 Existing community MCP wrappers
L0038 | - There are OSS MCP servers that wrap browser-use; some prefer HTTP transport to avoid stdio timeouts on long browser operations.  [oai_citation:5‡GitHub](https://github.com/Saik0s/mcp-browser-use?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0039 | 
L0040 | ---
L0041 | 
L0042 | ## 4) Platform decision recorded for A2rchitech (what you want)
L0043 | 
L0044 | ### Decision: Browser-first frontier = browser-use skill
L0045 | - **Canonical “BrowserSkill” for agents is browser-use-based**.
L0046 | - A2rchitech should treat browser automation as **a tool-callable capability** surfaced via:
L0047 |   - MCP (preferred for agent ecosystems)
L0048 |   - CLI wrapper (fallback + local scripting)
L0049 | 
L0050 | ### Non-goal (your directive)
L0051 | - **Do not build new first-class Playwright automation scripts** as the primary path.
L0052 | - If Playwright exists, it should be **an implementation detail**, not an API.
L0053 | 
L0054 | ---
L0055 | 
L0056 | ## 5) Implementation blueprint (gateway-level)
L0057 | 
L0058 | ### 5.1 Tool contract (A2rchitech-facing)
L0059 | Expose one stable interface regardless of backend:
L0060 | 
L0061 | - `browser.open(url, session?)`
L0062 | - `browser.state(session?)` → returns structured clickable targets
L0063 | - `browser.click(target, session?)`
L0064 | - `browser.type(target, text, session?)`
L0065 | - `browser.screenshot(session?)`
L0066 | - `browser.close(session?)`
L0067 | 
L0068 | This maps cleanly onto browser-use’s documented action style and supports multi-session operation patterns. (CLI-style control is explicitly part of the ecosystem around browser-use + MCP wrappers.)  [oai_citation:6‡GitHub](https://github.com/Saik0s/mcp-browser-use?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0069 | 
L0070 | ### 5.2 Two runtimes
L0071 | 1) **Local**: stdio MCP (`uvx ... --mcp`) for dev + Claude Desktop-style clients.  [oai_citation:7‡docs.browser-use.com](https://docs.browser-use.com/customize/integrations/mcp-server?utm_source=chatgpt.com)  
L0072 | 2) **Remote**: HTTP MCP (`https://api.browser-use.com/mcp`) for cloud agents, long-running tasks, and persistent daemon behavior.  [oai_citation:8‡docs.cloud.browser-use.com](https://docs.cloud.browser-use.com/usage/mcp-server?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0073 | 
L0074 | ---
L0075 | 
L0076 | ## 6) Risk register (explicit)
L0077 | 
L0078 | ### R1 — “No Playwright at all” may be infeasible if browser-use depends on it
L0079 | If browser-use requires Playwright, eliminating Playwright completely may require:
L0080 | - a different backend,
L0081 | - CDP-only workflows,
L0082 | - or a browser grid provider.
L0083 | 
L0084 | For now, you can still achieve the practical goal: **no Playwright-authored code and no Playwright exposed to agents**, while tolerating it as a hidden dependency if needed.  [oai_citation:9‡Bright Data](https://brightdata.com/blog/ai/browser-use-with-scraping-browser?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0085 | 
L0086 | ### R2 — Confusion from Microsoft product retirement
L0087 | “Playwright is deprecated” is often a misread of “Microsoft Playwright Testing is retired.” The platform decision should not be based on that confusion; base it on A2rchitech’s desired abstraction (agent-friendly browsing).  [oai_citation:10‡Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/playwright/includes/retirement-banner?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
L0088 | 
L0089 | ---
L0090 | 
L0091 | ## 7) Concrete next tasks (handoff-ready)
L0092 | 
L0093 | 1) **Define `BrowserSkill` contract** in /spec/Contracts (tool schema + result schema).  
L0094 | 2) **Implement gateway adapters**:
L0095 |    - Adapter A: local stdio MCP (`uvx browser-use --mcp`)  
L0096 |    - Adapter B: remote HTTP MCP (`https://api.browser-use.com/mcp`)  
L0097 | 3) **Add session routing**: session-id keyed browser contexts; support concurrent agents.  
L0098 | 4) **Add timeout + retry policy** tuned for long browser ops (especially if using stdio).  [oai_citation:11‡GitHub](https://github.com/Saik0s/mcp-browser-use?utm_source=chatgpt.com)  
L0099 | 5) **Enforce “no Playwright code”**: lint/policy gate in /agent/POLICY.md (A2rchitech repo law layer).  
L0100 | 6) **Add observability hooks**: log tool calls + screenshots + state snapshots as first-class artifacts.
L0101 | 
L0102 | ---
L0103 | 
L0104 | ## 8) Final recorded position (verbatim intent, normalized)
L0105 | A2rchitech’s browser automation should treat **browser-use** as the standard agent browser skill and the dominant “frontier” interface (MCP/CLI callable). Playwright is not a platform API and should not be the recommended integration surface, even if it remains a hidden underlying dependency in some modes.
L0106 | 
L0107 | ---
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — Claude Task System vs Governed Agent Swarms
L0002 | 
L0003 | **Topic:** Mapping Anthropic’s Claude Task System to A2rchitech’s Swarm-Oriented Orchestration Architecture  
L0004 | **Date:** 2026-01-26  
L0005 | **Scope:** Multi-agent DAG scheduling, persistence, recursive delegation, verification layers, and UX parity targets.
L0006 | 
L0007 | ---
L0008 | 
L0009 | ## 1. What Claude Actually Shipped (De-Hyped)
L0010 | 
L0011 | Claude Code’s new “task system” is not merely todo tracking. It provides four structural primitives:
L0012 | 
L0013 | ### Core Capabilities
L0014 | | Capability | Description |
L0015 | |-----------|------------|
L0016 | | Persistent DAG | Tasks stored outside chat sessions; survive terminal restarts |
L0017 | | Dependency Enforcement | `blockedBy` edges enforce order structurally |
L0018 | | Context Isolation | Each task spawns a fresh agent with its own token window |
L0019 | | Parallel Scheduling | All unblocked tasks run simultaneously |
L0020 | | Auto Model Routing | Lightweight models for search, heavy models for reasoning |
L0021 | 
L0022 | **Key Shift:**  
L0023 | > Cognition moved from transient LLM context → durable symbolic structures (graphs + artifacts).
L0024 | 
L0025 | ---
L0026 | 
L0027 | ## 2. What Problem This Solves
L0028 | 
L0029 | Classic failure mode:
L0030 | 
L0031 | - Plan lives in chat
L0032 | - Context window saturates
L0033 | - Dependencies forgotten
L0034 | - Humans re-anchor
L0035 | - Drift accumulates
L0036 | 
L0037 | Claude’s approach:
L0038 | 
L0039 | | Old | New |
L0040 | |----|----|
L0041 | | Plan in prompt | Plan in persistent graph |
L0042 | | Sequential execution | Parallel scheduling |
L0043 | | Memory fragile | Durable |
L0044 | | Order implicit | Order enforced |
L0045 | 
L0046 | ---
L0047 | 
L0048 | ## 3. What Is Not Novel
L0049 | 
L0050 | Architecturally:
L0051 | 
L0052 | - DAG schedulers → Airflow / Temporal
L0053 | - Worker pools → Ray / Celery
L0054 | - Hierarchical planning → HTN planners
L0055 | - Build graphs → `make`
L0056 | - Agent recursion → AutoGPT-era designs
L0057 | 
L0058 | **Novelty = productization + UX**, not research.
L0059 | 
L0060 | ---
L0061 | 
L0062 | ## 4. Recursive Swarms — Reality Check
L0063 | 
L0064 | CJ Hess proposes agents spawning agents recursively (“layer 4”).
L0065 | 
L0066 | Feasible only with:
L0067 | 
L0068 | - Immutable specs
L0069 | - Contract-driven IO
L0070 | - Acceptance tests
L0071 | - Security sandboxes
L0072 | - Cost governance
L0073 | - Human release gates
L0074 | 
L0075 | Without these, recursion produces faster garbage.
L0076 | 
L0077 | ---
L0078 | 
L0079 | ## 5. A2rchitech vs Claude: Feature-Level Mapping
L0080 | 
L0081 | ### Orchestration Layer
L0082 | 
L0083 | | Claude Task System | A2rchitech |
L0084 | |------------------|-----------|
L0085 | | Task DAG | Work Item Header (WIH) + /spec Deltas |
L0086 | | Persistent tasks on disk | /SOT.md + repo-native law |
L0087 | | blockedBy edges | AcceptanceTests gating |
L0088 | | Sub-agents per task | Router-assigned Agents (Architect / Implementer / Tester / Security) |
L0089 | | Auto scheduling | Brain Runtime Registry |
L0090 | | Model routing | Multi-LLM Router |
L0091 | | IDE UI | Capsule + AG-UI shells |
L0092 | | Task artifacts | Artifact model (diff/log/image/doc) |
L0093 | 
L0094 | ---
L0095 | 
L0096 | ### Governance & Safety (Claude lacks / A2rchitech has)
L0097 | 
L0098 | | Control | Claude | A2rchitech |
L0099 | |--------|-------|-----------|
L0100 | | Spec baseline | ❌ | ✅ |
L0101 | | Append-only deltas | ❌ | ✅ |
L0102 | | Repo law enforcement | ❌ | ✅ |
L0103 | | Contract schemas | ❌ | ✅ |
L0104 | | CI gating | ❌ | ✅ |
L0105 | | Threat models | ❌ | ✅ |
L0106 | | Audit logs | Partial | First-class |
L0107 | | Tool permission tiers | Partial | Formal |
L0108 | 
L0109 | ---
L0110 | 
L0111 | ### Strategic Positioning
L0112 | 
L0113 | - **Claude:** velocity-first swarm scheduler.
L0114 | - **A2rchitech:** industrial-grade autonomous software factory.
L0115 | 
L0116 | ---
L0117 | 
L0118 | ## 6. What Claude Does Better Right Now
L0119 | 
L0120 | These are gaps to close:
L0121 | 
L0122 | ### UX Advantages to Replicate
L0123 | 
L0124 | 1. **Instant DAG visualization**
L0125 | 2. **One-keystroke task view**
L0126 | 3. **Trivial persistence env var**
L0127 | 4. **Zero-config parallel spawn**
L0128 | 5. **Auto model tiering**
L0129 | 6. **IDE-native integration**
L0130 | 
L0131 | ---
L0132 | 
L0133 | ## 7. Required A2rchitech Parity Modules
L0134 | 
L0135 | ### MoX-A2R-Swarm-001 — Swarm Control Plane
L0136 | - DAG runtime engine
L0137 | - Recursive agent spawning
L0138 | - Budget caps per subtree
L0139 | - Deadlock detection
L0140 | - Cost telemetry
L0141 | 
L0142 | ### MoX-A2R-Swarm-002 — Persistent Graph Store
L0143 | - SQLite/Postgres task graph
L0144 | - Artifact registry
L0145 | - Resume-from-crash semantics
L0146 | - Multi-session attach
L0147 | 
L0148 | ### MoX-A2R-Swarm-003 — DAG UI Capsule
L0149 | - Visual dependency editor
L0150 | - Live execution states
L0151 | - Artifact drill-down
L0152 | - Cost per node
L0153 | - Contract links
L0154 | 
L0155 | ### MoX-A2R-Swarm-004 — Recursive Delegation Policy
L0156 | - Depth limits
L0157 | - Budget ceilings
L0158 | - Spec inheritance
L0159 | - Auto-promotion to human review
L0160 | - Risk escalation rules
L0161 | 
L0162 | ---
L0163 | 
L0164 | ## 8. Architecture Principle Reinforced
L0165 | 
L0166 | This session reconfirms A2rchitech’s core thesis:
L0167 | 
L0168 | > Durable structures > context windows.
L0169 | 
L0170 | Execution must be anchored in:
L0171 | 
L0172 | - specs
L0173 | - contracts
L0174 | - tests
L0175 | - laws
L0176 | - artifacts
L0177 | - audit logs
L0178 | 
L0179 | ---
L0180 | 
L0181 | ## 9. Strategic Takeaways
L0182 | 
L0183 | - Claude proved the UX path.
L0184 | - Governance remains unsolved.
L0185 | - Verification is the long-term moat.
L0186 | - Orchestration engineering replaces prompt engineering.
L0187 | - A2rchitech is positioned above IDE-level copilots.
L0188 | 
L0189 | ---
L0190 | 
L0191 | ## 10. Follow-On Build Targets
L0192 | 
L0193 | Immediate:
L0194 | 
L0195 | - DAG engine prototype
L0196 | - Task persistence store
L0197 | - Swarm visualization capsule
L0198 | - Recursive delegation rules
L0199 | - Budget governor
L0200 | - Artifact lifecycle manager
L0201 | 
L0202 | Mid-term:
L0203 | 
L0204 | - Multi-repo orchestration
L0205 | - Distributed swarm execution
L0206 | - Security-isolated sandboxes
L0207 | - Economic scheduler
L0208 | - Compliance/audit export
L0209 | 
L0210 | ---
L0211 | 
L0212 | ## 11. Session Classification
L0213 | 
L0214 | **Domain:** A2rchitech Core Architecture  
L0215 | **Sub-Topic:** Swarm Orchestration vs Claude Task Runtime  
L0216 | **Importance:** High — validates design direction and surfaces UX parity targets.
L0217 | 
L0218 | ---
```

```text
L0001 | Here is the A2rchitech Session Markdown Summary for this thread, focused on:
L0002 | 
L0003 | Claude Code Tasks vs Beads — Agent Coordination & Memory Systems
L0004 | 
L0005 | ⸻
L0006 | 
L0007 | A2R Session — Claude Tasks vs Beads
L0008 | 
L0009 | Topic: Agent Coordination Layers & Persistent Task Graphs
L0010 | Date: 2026-01-26
L0011 | Project: A2rchitech OS
L0012 | Status: Saved for canonical buildout thread
L0013 | 
L0014 | ⸻
L0015 | 
L0016 | 1. Problem Being Examined
L0017 | 
L0018 | You requested research and architectural comparison between:
L0019 | 	•	Claude Code’s new “Tasks” feature
L0020 | 	•	The Beads project (git-native agent memory / task graph system)
L0021 | 
L0022 | Goal:
L0023 | 
L0024 | Determine whether Claude Tasks meaningfully replace Beads, whether they complement it, and what this implies for A2rchitech’s orchestration and persistence layer at scale—especially under agent swarm conditions.
L0025 | 
L0026 | ⸻
L0027 | 
L0028 | ⸻
L0029 | 
L0030 | 2. Claude Code Tasks — Technical Characterization
L0031 | 
L0032 | Core Function
L0033 | 
L0034 | Claude Tasks is a native coordination primitive inside Claude Code.
L0035 | 
L0036 | It replaces simple todos with:
L0037 | 	•	Structured task lists
L0038 | 	•	Dependency graphs
L0039 | 	•	Blocking relationships
L0040 | 	•	Persistent task IDs
L0041 | 	•	Optional cross-session survival
L0042 | 
L0043 | Observed Capabilities
L0044 | 	•	Tasks can depend on other tasks
L0045 | 	•	Can persist across restarts via CLAUDE_CODE_TASK_LIST_ID
L0046 | 	•	Supports long-running coding workflows
L0047 | 	•	Integrated directly into Claude Code UI + runtime
L0048 | 	•	Optimized for within-tool planning & execution
L0049 | 
L0050 | Design Intent
L0051 | 	•	Lightweight coordination layer
L0052 | 	•	No external DB
L0053 | 	•	No repo-native persistence
L0054 | 	•	No cross-tool memory
L0055 | 	•	No Git branching semantics
L0056 | 
L0057 | Architectural Role
L0058 | 
L0059 | Claude Tasks acts as:
L0060 | 
L0061 | An in-runtime DAG scheduler for work units inside a single agent environment.
L0062 | 
L0063 | ⸻
L0064 | 
L0065 | ⸻
L0066 | 
L0067 | 3. Beads Project — Technical Characterization
L0068 | 
L0069 | Core Function
L0070 | 
L0071 | Beads is:
L0072 | 	•	A git-tracked task graph
L0073 | 	•	Stored in .beads/
L0074 | 	•	Versioned with code
L0075 | 	•	Agent-agnostic
L0076 | 	•	Durable across sessions, branches, and models
L0077 | 
L0078 | Capabilities
L0079 | 	•	JSONL task nodes
L0080 | 	•	Dependency edges
L0081 | 	•	Blocking relations
L0082 | 	•	History
L0083 | 	•	Branching
L0084 | 	•	Merge conflicts resolved like code
L0085 | 	•	External memory store for agents
L0086 | 
L0087 | Architectural Role
L0088 | 
L0089 | Beads is:
L0090 | 
L0091 | A repo-native persistent cognition layer for agents.
L0092 | 
L0093 | It acts as:
L0094 | 	•	Long-horizon planning DB
L0095 | 	•	Multi-agent coordination substrate
L0096 | 	•	Externalized agent memory
L0097 | 	•	Replayable reasoning substrate
L0098 | 
L0099 | ⸻
L0100 | 
L0101 | ⸻
L0102 | 
L0103 | 4. Direct Comparison Matrix
L0104 | 
L0105 | Axis	Claude Tasks	Beads
L0106 | Location	Inside Claude Code	Repo-native
L0107 | Persistence	Optional	Default
L0108 | Branch-aware	No	Yes
L0109 | Git-versioned	No	Yes
L0110 | Tool-agnostic	No	Yes
L0111 | Long-term memory	Limited	Yes
L0112 | DAG dependencies	Yes	Yes
L0113 | Multi-agent coordination	Partial	Strong
L0114 | Distributed workflows	Weak	Native
L0115 | Install effort	None	Required
L0116 | 
L0117 | 
L0118 | ⸻
L0119 | 
L0120 | ⸻
L0121 | 
L0122 | 5. Key Architectural Insight for A2rchitech
L0123 | 
L0124 | Claude Tasks and Beads are not equivalent systems.
L0125 | 
L0126 | They operate at different layers:
L0127 | 
L0128 | ┌─────────────────────────────┐
L0129 | │ Claude Code Runtime         │
L0130 | │  └── Tasks (local DAG)      │
L0131 | ├─────────────────────────────┤
L0132 | │ A2rchitech Orchestrator     │
L0133 | │  └── Work Graph Engine      │
L0134 | ├─────────────────────────────┤
L0135 | │ Repo-Native Memory Layer    │
L0136 | │  └── Beads-like System      │
L0137 | └─────────────────────────────┘
L0138 | 
L0139 | Interpretation:
L0140 | 
L0141 | Claude Tasks is:
L0142 | 	•	A runtime scheduler
L0143 | 
L0144 | Beads is:
L0145 | 	•	A stateful cognition substrate
L0146 | 
L0147 | For A2rchitech:
L0148 | 
L0149 | You want BOTH layers abstracted.
L0150 | 
L0151 | ⸻
L0152 | 
L0153 | ⸻
L0154 | 
L0155 | 6. Strategic Direction for A2rchitech
L0156 | 
L0157 | Recommendation:
L0158 | 
L0159 | A2rchitech should implement:
L0160 | 
L0161 | Layer 1 — Runtime Task Graphs
L0162 | 
L0163 | Claude-like:
L0164 | 	•	Ephemeral DAGs
L0165 | 	•	Fast scheduling
L0166 | 	•	Per-session execution
L0167 | 	•	In-memory
L0168 | 	•	Tool-specific
L0169 | 
L0170 | Layer 2 — Repo-Native Durable Memory
L0171 | 
L0172 | Beads-like:
L0173 | 	•	Git-tracked
L0174 | 	•	Branch-aware
L0175 | 	•	Agent-agnostic
L0176 | 	•	External truth store
L0177 | 	•	Long-horizon cognition
L0178 | 
L0179 | ⸻
L0180 | 
L0181 | ⸻
L0182 | 
L0183 | 7. Implications for Agent Swarms
L0184 | 
L0185 | In swarm scenarios:
L0186 | 	•	Claude Tasks ≈ local worker queue
L0187 | 	•	Beads ≈ global project ledger
L0188 | 
L0189 | For A2rchitech:
L0190 | 
L0191 | The orchestrator should compile Beads-style task graphs into runtime DAGs inside worker agents.
L0192 | 
L0193 | ⸻
L0194 | 
L0195 | ⸻
L0196 | 
L0197 | 8. A2rchitech Actionable Design Hooks
L0198 | 
L0199 | Add to Architecture:
L0200 | 
L0201 | ✅ Work Graph Compiler
L0202 | 	•	Reads repo memory graph
L0203 | 	•	Emits runtime DAG
L0204 | 	•	Assigns to agents
L0205 | 
L0206 | ✅ Durable Memory Store
L0207 | 	•	Git-native
L0208 | 	•	JSON schemas
L0209 | 	•	Conflict resolution rules
L0210 | 
L0211 | ✅ Orchestrator Arbitration
L0212 | 	•	Prevents two agents editing same file
L0213 | 	•	Assigns shards of work graph
L0214 | 
L0215 | ✅ Tool-Native Task Adapters
L0216 | 	•	Claude Tasks adapter
L0217 | 	•	Cursor adapter
L0218 | 	•	OpenAI task adapter (future)
L0219 | 	•	CLI schedulers
L0220 | 
L0221 | ⸻
L0222 | 
L0223 | ⸻
L0224 | 
L0225 | 9. Canonical Terminology Added
L0226 | 
L0227 | For A2rchitech:
L0228 | 	•	Persistent Work Graph (PWG) — Beads-like layer
L0229 | 	•	Runtime Task DAG (RTD) — Claude-like layer
L0230 | 	•	Work Compiler — translates PWG → RTD
```
