L0025 | 
L0026 | # A2rchitech Session — Multimodal Modular Stack (Non‑Frontier)
L0027 | 
L0028 | ## 0) Session Intent
L0029 | 
L0030 | Build a **practical, modular, multi-model tool stack** (non-frontier models) that is easy to use inside **A2rchitech**:
L0031 | - capability-first routing (OCR / coding / reasoning / writing / image gen / image edit)
L0032 | - hot-swappable providers
L0033 | - artifact-first outputs
L0034 | - CLI-first execution (local + remote + cloud)
L0035 | - clean contracts for skills + adapters + acceptance tests
L0036 | 
L0037 | ## 1) Capability → Model Map (Source of Truth)
L0038 | 
L0039 | > These models are treated as **providers** behind stable A2rchitech capabilities.
L0040 | 
L0041 | ### Vision / OCR
L0042 | - `qwen-3-vl`
L0043 | - `glm-4.6v`
L0044 | 
L0045 | ### Coding (cheap / throughput)
L0046 | - `qwen-3-coder`
L0047 | 
L0048 | ### Coding (high capability)
L0049 | - `glm-4.7`
L0050 | - `minimax-m2.1`
L0051 | 
L0052 | ### Reasoning
L0053 | - `deepseek-speciale`
L0054 | 
L0055 | ### Writing
L0056 | - `kimi-k2`
L0057 | - `kimi-k2-thinking`
L0058 | 
L0059 | ### General Purpose
L0060 | - `deepseek-v3.2`
L0061 | 
L0062 | ### Image Generation
L0063 | - `flux-2-dev`
L0064 | - `z-image-turbo`
L0065 | 
L0066 | ### Image Editing
L0067 | - `qwen-image-edit-2509`
L0068 | 
L0069 | ---
L0070 | 
L0071 | # /spec/MultimodalStack.md (Proposed Spec)
L0072 | 
L0073 | ## 2) Problem
L0074 | 
L0075 | A2rchitech needs a **modular multimodal stack** where:
L0076 | - different tasks route to different non-frontier models for cost/perf
L0077 | - the OS owns state (memory + policy), models are stateless engines
L0078 | - every call yields durable artifacts (json/diff/image/log) usable by swarms
L0079 | - the system runs locally, remotely, or via vendor APIs using the same interface
L0080 | 
L0081 | ## 3) Goals / Non‑Goals
L0082 | 
L0083 | ### Goals
L0084 | 1. Deterministic capability routing (with explicit fallbacks).
L0085 | 2. Stable interfaces: **Skill API** and **Provider Adapter API**.
L0086 | 3. Artifact-first outputs with typed metadata.
L0087 | 4. CLI-first execution with a uniform command surface (`a2r run ...`).
L0088 | 5. Observability: logs, cost, latency, provider selection trace.
L0089 | 6. Safe execution boundaries: policy gates for tools, data, network.
L0090 | 
L0091 | ### Non‑Goals (for v1)
L0092 | - Training or fine-tuning models.
L0093 | - Building a new model serving framework (reuse vLLM/llama.cpp/vendor APIs).
L0094 | - Perfect auto-classification (allow explicit override + guardrails).
L0095 | 
L0096 | ## 4) Core Architecture
L0097 | 
L0098 | ### 4.1 Components
L0099 | - **Capability Router**: chooses capability + provider + tier.
L0100 | - **Skill Registry**: declarative catalog of skills and available providers.
L0101 | - **Provider Adapter Layer**: provider-specific wrappers behind a common interface.
L0102 | - **Execution Runtimes**: local (docker/vLLM/llama.cpp), remote (ssh), cloud (API).
L0103 | - **Artifact Store**: content-addressed artifacts + metadata.
L0104 | - **Policy Engine**: gates tools, memory scopes, and side effects.
L0105 | - **Telemetry**: traces, cost estimates, latency, failures.
L0106 | 
L0107 | ### 4.2 Data Flow
L0108 | 1. Request enters Brain Runtime.
L0109 | 2. Classifier assigns `capability`, `cost_tier`, `latency_requirement`, `artifact_type`.
L0110 | 3. Router selects provider via rules + constraints.
L0111 | 4. Adapter executes in appropriate runtime.
L0112 | 5. Output normalized into **Artifact**.
L0113 | 6. Artifacts stored + indexed; returned to agent / UI.
L0114 | 
L0115 | ## 5) Capability Contract (Skill Surface)
L0116 | 
L0117 | A2rchitech exposes stable capabilities, independent of provider:
L0118 | 
L0119 | - `vision.ocr(image)->artifact:ocr_json`
L0120 | - `vision.vl_reason(image, prompt)->artifact:json|doc`
L0121 | - `code.fast(repo|prompt)->artifact:diff|patch`
L0122 | - `code.heavy(repo|prompt)->artifact:diff|patch|report`
L0123 | - `reason.solve(prompt)->artifact:plan|proof|trace`
L0124 | - `write.longform(prompt, style)->artifact:doc`
L0125 | - `general.chat(messages)->artifact:chatlog|json`
L0126 | - `image.generate(prompt)->artifact:image_png`
L0127 | - `image.edit(image, mask, instruction)->artifact:image_png`
L0128 | 
L0129 | ## 6) Routing Rules (Deterministic Core)
L0130 | 
L0131 | Routing decision is based on:
L0132 | 
L0133 | - `capability` (required)
L0134 | - `tier` (low|medium|high)
L0135 | - `constraints`:
L0136 |   - max_cost_per_call
L0137 |   - max_latency_ms
L0138 |   - required_context_len
L0139 |   - required_modalities
L0140 |   - privacy_mode (local_only|remote_ok|cloud_ok)
L0141 |   - availability (provider health)
L0142 | 
L0143 | ### Default mapping (v1)
L0144 | - OCR: `qwen-3-vl` → fallback `glm-4.6v`
L0145 | - Cheap code: `qwen-3-coder` → fallback `glm-4.7`
L0146 | - Heavy code: `glm-4.7` → fallback `minimax-m2.1`
L0147 | - Reasoning: `deepseek-speciale` → fallback `deepseek-v3.2`
L0148 | - Writing: `kimi-k2-thinking` → fallback `kimi-k2`
L0149 | - General: `deepseek-v3.2` → fallback `glm-4.7`
L0150 | - Image gen: `flux-2-dev` → fallback `z-image-turbo`
L0151 | - Image edit: `qwen-image-edit-2509` → fallback (future)
L0152 | 
L0153 | ## 7) Artifact Model (First‑Class)
L0154 | 
L0155 | All outputs normalize into:
L0156 | 
L0157 | ```json
L0158 | {
L0159 |   "id": "artf_...",
L0160 |   "type": "diff|patch|json|doc|image|log|trace",
L0161 |   "created_at": "2026-01-26T00:00:00-06:00",
L0162 |   "producer": {
L0163 |     "capability": "code.heavy",
L0164 |     "provider": "glm-4.7",
L0165 |     "runtime": "local|remote|cloud",
L0166 |     "model": "glm-4.7"
L0167 |   },
L0168 |   "inputs": {
L0169 |     "content_refs": ["..."],
L0170 |     "params": {}
L0171 |   },
L0172 |   "content_ref": "cas://sha256/...",
L0173 |   "metadata": {
L0174 |     "tokens_in": 0,
L0175 |     "tokens_out": 0,
L0176 |     "latency_ms": 0,
L0177 |     "cost_usd_est": 0.0
L0178 |   }
L0179 | }
L0180 | ```
L0181 | 
L0182 | ---
L0183 | 
L0184 | # /spec/Contracts/SkillSchema.json (Contract)
L0185 | 
L0186 | > Defines Skill Registry entries consumed by Router + CLI.
L0187 | 
L0188 | ```json
L0189 | {
L0190 |   "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
L0191 |   "title": "A2rchitech Skill Schema",
L0192 |   "type": "object",
L0193 |   "required": ["name", "version", "capability", "inputs", "outputs", "providers", "policy"],
L0194 |   "properties": {
L0195 |     "name": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^[a-z]+\.[a-z0-9_]+$" },
L0196 |     "version": { "type": "string" },
L0197 |     "capability": {
L0198 |       "type": "string",
L0199 |       "enum": ["ocr", "vision_reasoning", "cheap_code", "heavy_code", "reasoning", "writing", "general", "image_gen", "image_edit"]
L0200 |     },
L0201 |     "inputs": {
L0202 |       "type": "array",
L0203 |       "items": {
L0204 |         "type": "object",
L0205 |         "required": ["name", "kind", "required"],
L0206 |         "properties": {
L0207 |           "name": { "type": "string" },
L0208 |           "kind": { "type": "string", "enum": ["text", "messages", "image_path", "repo_path", "json", "mask_path"] },
L0209 |           "required": { "type": "boolean" }
L0210 |         }
L0211 |       }
L0212 |     },
L0213 |     "outputs": {
L0214 |       "type": "array",
L0215 |       "items": {
L0216 |         "type": "object",
L0217 |         "required": ["type"],
L0218 |         "properties": {
L0219 |           "type": { "type": "string", "enum": ["diff", "patch", "json", "doc", "image_png", "log", "trace"] }
L0220 |         }
L0221 |       }
L0222 |     },
L0223 |     "providers": {
L0224 |       "type": "array",
L0225 |       "minItems": 1,
L0226 |       "items": {
L0227 |         "type": "object",
L0228 |         "required": ["id", "priority", "adapter", "runtime_modes"],
L0229 |         "properties": {
L0230 |           "id": { "type": "string" },
L0231 |           "priority": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1 },
L0232 |           "adapter": { "type": "string" },
L0233 |           "runtime_modes": {
L0234 |             "type": "array",
L0235 |             "items": { "type": "string", "enum": ["local", "remote", "cloud"] }
L0236 |           },
L0237 |           "constraints": {
L0238 |             "type": "object",
L0239 |             "properties": {
L0240 |               "max_cost_usd": { "type": "number" },
L0241 |               "max_latency_ms": { "type": "integer" },
L0242 |               "min_context_tokens": { "type": "integer" },
L0243 |               "privacy": { "type": "string", "enum": ["local_only", "remote_ok", "cloud_ok"] }
L0244 |             }
L0245 |           }
L0246 |         }
L0247 |       }
L0248 |     },
L0249 |     "fallback": {
L0250 |       "type": "array",
L0251 |       "items": { "type": "string" }
L0252 |     },
L0253 |     "policy": {
L0254 |       "type": "object",
L0255 |       "required": ["safety_level"],
L0256 |       "properties": {
L0257 |         "safety_level": { "type": "string", "enum": ["read_only", "write", "destructive"] },
L0258 |         "requires_user_confirm": { "type": "boolean" },
L0259 |         "allowed_memory_scopes": {
L0260 |           "type": "array",
L0261 |           "items": { "type": "string", "enum": ["ephemeral", "session", "project", "global"] }
L0262 |         }
L0263 |       }
L0264 |     }
L0265 |   }
L0266 | }
L0267 | ```
L0268 | 
L0269 | ---
L0270 | 
L0271 | # /spec/Contracts/ModelProviderAdapter.json (Contract)
L0272 | 
L0273 | > Standard interface each provider adapter must implement.
L0274 | 
L0275 | ```json
L0276 | {
L0277 |   "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
L0278 |   "title": "Model Provider Adapter Interface",
L0279 |   "type": "object",
L0280 |   "required": ["id", "capabilities", "invoke"],
L0281 |   "properties": {
L0282 |     "id": { "type": "string" },
L0283 |     "capabilities": {
L0284 |       "type": "array",
L0285 |       "items": { "type": "string" }
L0286 |     },
L0287 |     "invoke": {
L0288 |       "type": "object",
L0289 |       "required": ["input", "context", "runtime"],
L0290 |       "properties": {
L0291 |         "input": { "type": "object" },
L0292 |         "context": {
L0293 |           "type": "object",
L0294 |           "properties": {
L0295 |             "trace_id": { "type": "string" },
L0296 |             "project_id": { "type": "string" },
L0297 |             "memory_scope": { "type": "string" }
L0298 |           }
L0299 |         },
L0300 |         "runtime": {
L0301 |           "type": "object",
L0302 |           "required": ["mode"],
L0303 |           "properties": {
L0304 |             "mode": { "type": "string", "enum": ["local", "remote", "cloud"] },
L0305 |             "endpoint": { "type": "string" },
L0306 |             "docker_image": { "type": "string" },
L0307 |             "ssh_host": { "type": "string" }
L0308 |           }
L0309 |         }
L0310 |       }
L0311 |     }
L0312 |   }
L0313 | }
L0314 | ```
L0315 | 
L0316 | ---
L0317 | 
L0318 | # /spec/AcceptanceTests/MultimodalStack.acceptance.md
L0319 | 
L0320 | ## Acceptance Criteria (v1)
L0321 | 
L0322 | ### A) Routing Determinism
L0323 | - Given a request with `capability=ocr`, the router selects `qwen-3-vl` when healthy, else falls back to `glm-4.6v`.
L0324 | - Given `capability=heavy_code` and `privacy_mode=local_only`, the router must never select a `cloud` runtime.
L0325 | 
L0326 | ### B) Artifact Normalization
L0327 | - Every invocation yields an Artifact JSON with `id`, `type`, `producer`, `content_ref`, and `metadata`.
L0328 | - Artifacts are content-addressed (hash stable for identical content).
L0329 | 
L0330 | ### C) CLI Uniformity
L0331 | - `a2r run vision.ocr --image <path>` executes end-to-end and returns Artifact ID.
L0332 | - `a2r run code.fast --repo <path>` returns a patch artifact.
L0333 | 
L0334 | ### D) Provider Swap
L0335 | - Updating a Skill Registry entry to prefer a different provider changes selection without modifying calling code.
L0336 | 
L0337 | ### E) Observability
L0338 | - Each invocation emits: provider selected, latency, cost estimate, and any fallback reason.
L0339 | 
L0340 | ---
L0341 | 
L0342 | # Implementation Blueprint (Tasks)
L0343 | 
L0344 | ## 8) Phase 1 — Capability Router (MUST FIRST)
L0345 | 
L0346 | 1. **Define request classification schema**
L0347 |    - inputs: modality, task_type, constraints
L0348 |    - outputs: capability, tier, artifact_type
L0349 | 
L0350 | 2. **Implement routing engine**
L0351 |    - rules: static map + constraints solver
L0352 |    - features: provider health checks + fallback
L0353 | 
L0354 | 3. **Add cost governor**
L0355 |    - hard caps: max_cost_usd, max_tokens
L0356 |    - soft policy: choose cheaper provider when “good enough”
L0357 | 
L0358 | Deliverable:
L0359 | - `packages/router/*` with unit tests
L0360 | 
L0361 | ## 9) Phase 2 — Skill Registry + Contracts
L0362 | 
L0363 | 1. Implement Skill Registry loader:
L0364 |    - reads `/skills/**/*.yaml`
L0365 |    - validates against `SkillSchema.json`
L0366 | 
L0367 | 2. Add registry query API:
L0368 |    - list skills
L0369 |    - list providers for skill
L0370 |    - resolve default provider
L0371 | 
L0372 | Deliverable:
L0373 | - `packages/skills-registry/*`
L0374 | 
L0375 | ## 10) Phase 3 — Provider Adapter Layer
L0376 | 
L0377 | Adapters (one per provider family):
L0378 | - `adapters/qwen/*`
L0379 | - `adapters/glm/*`
L0380 | - `adapters/deepseek/*`
L0381 | - `adapters/kimi/*`
L0382 | - `adapters/flux/*`
L0383 | - `adapters/minimax/*`
L0384 | 
L0385 | Each adapter implements:
L0386 | - `invoke() -> Artifact`
L0387 | - streaming support (optional v1)
L0388 | - runtime modes (local/remote/cloud)
L0389 | 
L0390 | Deliverable:
L0391 | - `packages/adapters/*`
L0392 | 
L0393 | ## 11) Phase 4 — CLI Surface (a2r)
L0394 | 
L0395 | Commands:
L0396 | - `a2r skills list`
L0397 | - `a2r providers list`
L0398 | - `a2r run <skill> [args]`
L0399 | - `a2r artifacts get <id>`
L0400 | - `a2r trace <id>`
L0401 | 
L0402 | Deliverable:
L0403 | - `/tools/a2r/*` (node/rust, whichever your repo standardizes)
L0404 | 
L0405 | ## 12) Phase 5 — Artifact Store
L0406 | 
L0407 | - content-addressed storage: `cas://sha256/...`
L0408 | - metadata index: sqlite/postgres (v1 can be sqlite)
L0409 | - folder mirror for human browsing
L0410 | 
L0411 | Deliverable:
L0412 | - `packages/artifacts/*`
L0413 | 
L0414 | ---
L0415 | 
L0416 | # Pitfalls to Avoid (Hard Lessons)
L0417 | 
L0418 | 1. **Provider-specific prompts leaking into callers**
L0419 |    - Fix: keep provider prompts inside adapters; callers use capability prompts.
L0420 | 
L0421 | 2. **No stable artifact contract**
L0422 |    - Fix: normalize EVERYTHING into Artifact with content_ref + metadata.
L0423 | 
L0424 | 3. **Routing that is “LLM magic”**
L0425 |    - Fix: deterministic rules first; allow LLM classification later as optional.
L0426 | 
L0427 | 4. **UI-first thinking**
L0428 |    - Fix: CLI + contracts first; UI consumes registry + artifacts.
L0429 | 
L0430 | 5. **No fallback discipline**
L0431 |    - Fix: explicit fallback chains and health checks.
L0432 | 
L0433 | ---
L0434 | 
L0435 | # Minimal Skill YAML Templates (Drop-In)
L0436 | 
L0437 | ## Example: vision.ocr
L0438 | ```yaml
L0439 | name: vision.ocr
L0440 | version: 0.1.0
L0441 | capability: ocr
L0442 | inputs:
L0443 |   - name: image
L0444 |     kind: image_path
L0445 |     required: true
L0446 | outputs:
L0447 |   - type: json
L0448 | providers:
L0449 |   - id: qwen-3-vl
L0450 |     priority: 1
L0451 |     adapter: adapters/qwen/vl
L0452 |     runtime_modes: [local, remote, cloud]
L0453 |   - id: glm-4.6v
L0454 |     priority: 2
L0455 |     adapter: adapters/glm/vision
L0456 |     runtime_modes: [cloud, remote]
L0457 | fallback: [glm-4.6v]
L0458 | policy:
L0459 |   safety_level: read_only
L0460 |   requires_user_confirm: false
L0461 |   allowed_memory_scopes: [session, project]
L0462 | ```
L0463 | 
L0464 | ## Example: code.fast
L0465 | ```yaml
L0466 | name: code.fast
L0467 | version: 0.1.0
L0468 | capability: cheap_code
L0469 | inputs:
L0470 |   - name: prompt
L0471 |     kind: text
L0472 |     required: true
L0473 |   - name: repo
L0474 |     kind: repo_path
L0475 |     required: false
L0476 | outputs:
L0477 |   - type: patch
L0478 | providers:
L0479 |   - id: qwen-3-coder
L0480 |     priority: 1
L0481 |     adapter: adapters/qwen/coder
L0482 |     runtime_modes: [local, remote, cloud]
L0483 | fallback: [glm-4.7]
L0484 | policy:
L0485 |   safety_level: write
L0486 |   requires_user_confirm: true
L0487 |   allowed_memory_scopes: [session, project]
L0488 | ```
L0489 | 
L0490 | ---
L0491 | 
L0492 | # Next: How to Use This in A2rchitech Immediately
L0493 | 
L0494 | 1. Land the spec files in your repo under `/spec` and `/skills`.
L0495 | 2. Implement router + registry (Phases 1–2).
L0496 | 3. Add a single adapter end-to-end (recommend: `vision.ocr`).
L0497 | 4. Wire CLI (`a2r run vision.ocr --image ...`).
L0498 | 5. Expand adapters + skills incrementally.
L0499 | 
L0500 | ---
L0501 | 
L0502 | # Session Closeout
L0503 | 
L0504 | This session produced:
L0505 | - A full **Multimodal Modular Stack** spec for A2rchitech
L0506 | - Contracts for Skill Registry and Provider Adapters
L0507 | - Acceptance tests for determinism, artifacts, CLI, swaps, and telemetry
L0508 | - A phased implementation blueprint + pitfalls
L0509 | - Drop-in YAML templates for immediate use
L0510 | 
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | ---
L0002 | title: "A2rchitech Session Summary — BrowserSkill + Artifacts + Extensions"
L0003 | date: "2026-01-26_221919"
L0004 | scope: "a2rchitech"
L0005 | topic: "browser-skill / artifact-first / controlled extensions"
L0006 | status: "canonical-session-summary"
L0007 | ---
L0008 | 
L0009 | # Canonical Focus
L0010 | This session narrows A2rchitech’s near-term build focus to a **complete modern agent stack** with strict separation of concerns:
L0011 | 
L0012 | 1. **Execution** — local + background + parallel (including browser execution)
L0013 | 2. **Coordination** — sessions, task graphs, verification gates
L0014 | 3. **Presentation** — canvas + GenTabs + artifact viewer (never panes, empty-by-default)
L0015 | 
L0016 | The emphasis is on making **browser control + artifact viewing + controlled skills/connectors** first-class, with additive integration only.
L0017 | 
L0018 | ---
L0019 | 
L0020 | # 1) BrowserSkill (Seamless Chrome Control) — Contract
L0021 | **BrowserSkill** is the canonical skill interface for web control, designed to run in both foreground and background contexts.
L0022 | 
L0023 | ## Minimal API (v1)
L0024 | - `open(url)`
L0025 | - `click(selector)`
L0026 | - `type(selector, text)`
L0027 | - `screenshot()`
L0028 | 
L0029 | ## Reliability additions (required for real web variance)
L0030 | - `waitFor(selector | timeoutMs)`
L0031 | - `extract(selector | script)` → DOM snapshot or extracted text
L0032 | - `url()` → current URL
L0033 | - optional: `html()` and later `networkLog()`
L0034 | 
L0035 | ## Standard tool result
L0036 | Every BrowserSkill call returns a structured `BrowserResult`:
L0037 | - `ok` boolean
L0038 | - `step_id`
L0039 | - `url`
L0040 | - `screenshot_artifact_id?`
L0041 | - `dom_artifact_id?`
L0042 | - `extracted?`
L0043 | - `error?` (structured)
L0044 | 
L0045 | ---
L0046 | 
L0047 | # 2) Execution Contexts
L0048 | BrowserSkill must be executable with the **same contract** across:
L0049 | 
L0050 | ## Foreground (interactive)
L0051 | - User-visible, low-latency
L0052 | - Streams incremental artifacts
L0053 | 
L0054 | ## Background (headless)
L0055 | - Used for parallel runs and unattended workflows
L0056 | - Determinism + sandbox + verification prioritized
L0057 | - Produces coherent artifact bundles per run
L0058 | 
L0059 | **Invariant:** the **executor changes**, not the tool contract.
L0060 | 
L0061 | ---
L0062 | 
L0063 | # 3) Artifact System (First-Class, Immutable)
L0064 | Artifacts are the stable boundary between execution and presentation.
L0065 | 
L0066 | ## Artifact (v1)
L0067 | - `id`
L0068 | - `type`: `diff | preview | doc | image | log | table | dom | trace | verification`
L0069 | - `source_agent`
L0070 | - `session_id`
L0071 | - `created_at`
L0072 | - `payload_ref` (file/blob/url pointer)
L0073 | - optional: `summary`, `metadata`
L0074 | 
L0075 | ## Production rules
L0076 | - Browser actions generate artifacts when meaningful:
L0077 |   - screenshot → `image`
L0078 |   - DOM snapshot / html → `dom`
L0079 |   - preview URL → `preview`
L0080 | - Artifacts are **immutable**; new state produces a new artifact.
L0081 | 
L0082 | ---
L0083 | 
L0084 | # 4) Event Topics (Execution → Presentation Binding)
L0085 | Canvas/GenTabs never render raw tool output; they render by artifact IDs.
L0086 | 
L0087 | ## Browser topics
L0088 | - `render.browser.preview` → `{ artifact_id }`
L0089 | - `render.browser.screenshot` → `{ artifact_id }`
L0090 | - `render.browser.dom` → `{ artifact_id }`
L0091 | - `status.browser` → `{ state, reason? }`
L0092 | 
L0093 | ## Artifact viewer topics
L0094 | - `render.artifact.open` → `{ artifact_id }`
L0095 | - `render.artifact.list` → `{ session_id, filter?, sort? }`
L0096 | 
L0097 | **Invariant:** If the canvas monitor is absent, events are ignored and execution must not fail.
L0098 | 
L0099 | ---
L0100 | 
L0101 | # 5) Extensions & Connectors Marketplace (Controlled)
L0102 | A2rchitech treats tools/skills as **extensions** with explicit constraints.
L0103 | 
L0104 | ## Extension (v1)
L0105 | - `name`, `version`
L0106 | - `capabilities[]`
L0107 | - `execution_scope`: `local | background | browser | any`
L0108 | - `safety_level`: `read_only | write | destructive`
L0109 | - `allowed_in_background` (bool)
L0110 | - `requires_browser` (bool)
L0111 | - `requires_network` (bool)
L0112 | - optional: `rate_limits`, `auth_requirements`
L0113 | 
L0114 | ## Enforcement at dispatch time
L0115 | - `requires_browser=true` → route to BrowserExecutor
L0116 | - `allowed_in_background=false` → deny background execution
L0117 | - `requires_network=true` → enforce sandbox network policy
L0118 | - `destructive` → requires explicit security/consent gate
L0119 | 
L0120 | Purpose: enable safe background runs, prevent skill misuse, and support marketplaces later.
L0121 | 
L0122 | ---
L0123 | 
L0124 | # 6) Minimal Implementation Units
L0125 | To deliver this slice, the minimum required units are:
L0126 | 
L0127 | - **BrowserExecutor**: implements BrowserSkill contract and produces artifacts
L0128 | - **ArtifactStore**: append-only storage + retrieval by `artifact_id` + session indexing
L0129 | - **Event Publisher**: emits `CanvasEvent` topics referencing artifact IDs only
L0130 | 
L0131 | Everything else (CLI, canvas monitor, background orchestrator) consumes these contracts.
L0132 | 
L0133 | ---
L0134 | 
L0135 | # 7) Acceptance Tests (Proof of Real Integration)
L0136 | - BrowserSkill open → waitFor → screenshot produces an `image` artifact and returns `BrowserResult.ok=true`
L0137 | - DOM snapshot produces a `dom` artifact and can be opened by `artifact_id`
L0138 | - Artifact immutability enforced (no overwrite semantics)
L0139 | - Extension enforcement:
L0140 |   - denies background runs when `allowed_in_background=false`
L0141 |   - denies browser tools when browser executor absent
L0142 |   - enforces `requires_network` policies
L0143 | 
L0144 | ---
L0145 | 
L0146 | # Note (explicit)
L0147 | A coder-agent “prompt to look for something” was discussed as useful operational tooling, but it is **not the primary objective** of this session; the primary objective is the **BrowserSkill + Artifact + Extension control plane** integration described above.
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — ElizaOS Primitives Integration (2026-01-26)
L0002 | 
L0003 | ## Session Scope
L0004 | This session evaluated **ElizaOS** (as referenced by the user’s X/Twitter link) as a source of agent-runtime primitives that could be extracted and upgraded for **A2rchitech / Gizzi OS / A2rchitectOS**. The output was normalized into a **canonical integration spec** intended to prevent architecture drift.
L0005 | 
L0006 | ---
L0007 | 
L0008 | ## Inputs Provided
L0009 | - User shared an X/Twitter link referencing ElizaOS primitives for potential adoption:
L0010 |   - https://x.com/elizaos/status/2008641553649332224?s=46
L0011 | 
L0012 | ---
L0013 | 
L0014 | ## Core Deliverables Produced
L0015 | 
L0016 | ### 1) Canonical Integration Spec (Markdown)
L0017 | A binding, policy-style spec was produced to define how ElizaOS-style primitives map into A2rchitech’s architecture with strict constraints.
L0018 | 
L0019 | **Previously generated file:**
L0020 | - `ElizaOS_A2rchitech_Unified_Integration_Spec.md`
L0021 | - Expected local path: `/mnt/data/ElizaOS_A2rchitech_Unified_Integration_Spec.md`
L0022 | - Download link (from this chat):  
L0023 |   `sandbox:/mnt/data/ElizaOS_A2rchitech_Unified_Integration_Spec.md`
L0024 | 
L0025 | ---
L0026 | 
L0027 | ## Key Technical Conclusions Captured in the Spec
L0028 | 
L0029 | ### A. What ElizaOS Contributes (Abstracted Primitives)
L0030 | The integration spec treats ElizaOS as a source of four primitives:
L0031 | 1. **Stateful Agent Runtime**
L0032 | 2. **Event-driven execution model**
L0033 | 3. **Plugin-based capability system**
L0034 | 4. **Multi-agent coordination fabric**
L0035 | 
L0036 | The spec explicitly rejects “adopt wholesale”; only “extract + upgrade.”
L0037 | 
L0038 | ### B. Non-Negotiable A2rchitech Upgrades (Governance Layer)
L0039 | The spec asserts A2rchitech must enforce:
L0040 | - determinism where possible
L0041 | - typed interfaces (schemas/contracts)
L0042 | - explicit permissions
L0043 | - verification gates prior to irreversible actions
L0044 | - audit logging and rollback semantics
L0045 | 
L0046 | ### C. Canonical Mapping Into A2rchitech Layers
L0047 | The session produced a direct mapping table:
L0048 | - ElizaOS runtime → A2rchitech agent execution layer (typed goals, quotas, lifecycle)
L0049 | - ElizaOS memory → A2rchitech memory/knowledge layer (KG + audit)
L0050 | - ElizaOS event bus → orchestration layer (deterministic event calculus)
L0051 | - ElizaOS plugins → tool/capability layer (contract-verified modules)
L0052 | - ElizaOS swarms → coordination layer (role-bound DAG + consensus)
L0053 | 
L0054 | ### D. Runtime Control Model (Supervisor/Sub-runtime)
L0055 | A2rchitech is defined as the **supervisor OS** and ElizaOS runtime as a **sub-runtime**:
L0056 | - no self-authorization
L0057 | - no self-expanding memory
L0058 | - no irreversible actions without verification
L0059 | 
L0060 | Lifecycle checkpoints required:
L0061 | 1) Init (identity + role binding)
L0062 | 2) Context hydration (scoped)
L0063 | 3) Execution window (bounded)
L0064 | 4) Verification checkpoint
L0065 | 5) Commit/rollback
L0066 | 6) Audit write
L0067 | 
L0068 | ### E. Memory Policy (Hard Constraints)
L0069 | - Long-term memory is to be replaced/anchored in a **structured Knowledge Graph** with append-only auditing.
L0070 | - Disallowed: self-modifying memory or uncontrolled persistence.
L0071 | - Allowed: short-term context, controlled retrieval, quantized recall (implementation detail to be decided elsewhere).
L0072 | 
L0073 | ### F. Event Normalization (No Raw-Text Execution)
L0074 | All inbound stimuli are normalized into constrained event objects with declared intent and verification requirements.
L0075 | Raw text must not directly trigger actions.
L0076 | 
L0077 | ### G. Plugin/Capability Contracting
L0078 | Plugins become contract-bound modules with:
L0079 | - typed inputs/outputs
L0080 | - declared side-effects
L0081 | - permission scopes
L0082 | - policy compliance checks
L0083 | 
L0084 | ### H. Multi-Agent Orchestration Rules
L0085 | Agents are not peers by default; roles are explicit:
L0086 | - Planner / Executor / Verifier / Observer / Archivist
L0087 | 
L0088 | Tasks are DAG-based; consensus required for high-risk actions.
L0089 | 
L0090 | ### I. Hard Rules (Anti-Drift Guardrails)
L0091 | 1. No agent is trusted by default
L0092 | 2. No memory is permanent by default
L0093 | 3. No action executes without validation
L0094 | 4. No plugin runs without a contract
L0095 | 5. No swarm acts without coordination logic
L0096 | 
L0097 | ---
L0098 |

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary (A2R) — Browser-OS + CLI + Agentic Runtime (Contracts / Capsules / Tools)
L0002 | 
L0003 | **Session command:** gizzi save a2r session  
L0004 | **Date (America/Chicago):** January 26, 2026  
L0005 | **Scope:** Consolidate this session’s decisions, idealized architecture, implementation plan, and integration-first OSS candidates so we don’t lose focus.
L0006 | 
L0007 | ---
L0008 | 
L0009 | ## 0) What triggered this session
L0010 | 
L0011 | You asked for a better “game plan” because “UI” could mean:
L0012 | - cloud web console
L0013 | - terminal-first CLI
L0014 | - desktop app
L0015 | - full operating system
L0016 | 
L0017 | You chose: **Web console + CLI now**, while preserving a long-term **OS-like control plane** vision that can later span cloud, sovereign local, and edge/IoT/robot contexts.
L0018 | 
L0019 | ---
L0020 | 
L0021 | ## 1) Top-level thesis we converged on
L0022 | 
L0023 | ### 1.1 The OS is not the UI
L0024 | - The “OS” is the **contract-enforced runtime and governance layer**, not the visual shell.
L0025 | - The web console and CLI are **shells / gateways** into the same underlying primitives.
L0026 | 
L0027 | ### 1.2 The “Browser-OS” is a desktop shell in the browser
L0028 | - “Desktop OS in browser” = a **workspace shell** (ClickUp-like) with panes and modes:
L0029 |   - **Authoring mode**
L0030 |   - **Ops mode**
L0031 |   - **Shell mode** (terminal + assistant)
L0032 | - It can embed a terminal and later embed WebVM replay/sandbox as a plugin.
L0033 | 
L0034 | ### 1.3 The real unifier is Capsule
L0035 | - **Capsule** becomes the unit of deployment across:
L0036 |   - cloud runner (ship first)
L0037 |   - WebVM replay/sandbox (later)
L0038 |   - edge agents (later)
L0039 | - This avoids “ISO images” distribution and matches “web distribution” logic for devices.
L0040 | 
L0041 | ---
L0042 | 
L0043 | ## 2) The key primitives (your “OS kernel invariants”)
L0044 | 
L0045 | These primitives are the non-negotiable backbone that make it OS-like:
L0046 | 
L0047 | 1. **ToolABI** (Unix-style “syscall interface”)
L0048 |    - Stable, versioned interface for tools.
L0049 |    - Tools must be dynamically discoverable at runtime.
L0050 |    - Each tool declares required capabilities.
L0051 | 
L0052 | 2. **Capsule**
L0053 |    - Signed, content-addressed package.
L0054 |    - Includes workflow definitions + tool references + policies + context hash + signatures.
L0055 |    - Must be deterministic and replayable.
L0056 | 
L0057 | 3. **PolicyDecision**
L0058 |    - Every tool call is gated by policy (deny-by-default).
L0059 |    - Policy decisions are artifacts and appear in timelines/audit.
L0060 | 
L0061 | 4. **VerifyArtifacts**
L0062 |    - Code signing/integrity for capsules and artifacts.
L0063 |    - Gate execution on verification.
L0064 | 
L0065 | 5. **EventEnvelope / Event Ledger**
L0066 |    - Append-only event stream for runs, tool invocations, policy checks, artifacts.
L0067 |    - Drives audit, replay, debugging, and UI timelines.
L0068 | 
L0069 | 6. **RunModel**
L0070 |    - Process lifecycle for a run (start/stop/status/targets).
L0071 |    - Runs emit events and produce verifiable artifacts.
L0072 | 
L0073 | ---
L0074 | 
L0075 | ## 3) The product surface: Browser-OS shell + CLI
L0076 | 
L0077 | ### 3.1 Modes (the desktop-shell model)
L0078 | 
L0079 | **Authoring Mode**
L0080 | - Workflow/capsule editor: YAML + schema validation + compile errors inline.
L0081 | - Persona + task template editors (schema-driven).
L0082 | - “Validate” / “Compile” / “Build Capsule” actions wired to API.
L0083 | 
L0084 | **Ops Mode**
L0085 | - Runs dashboard: list/filter.
L0086 | - Run detail: event timeline, tool invocations, artifacts, verification results, policy decisions.
L0087 | - Audit log view.
L0088 | - Device/fleet view can be stubbed early, expanded later.
L0089 | 
L0090 | **Shell Mode**
L0091 | - Persistent terminal (first-class).
L0092 | - Persistent “Gizzi” assistant pane.
L0093 | - Voice capture and “action execution” with confirmations.
L0094 | 
L0095 | ### 3.2 CLI parity rule
L0096 | - Anything you can do in UI should be doable in CLI:
L0097 |   - validate/build capsules
L0098 |   - register/list tools
L0099 |   - start/inspect runs
L0100 |   - tail event stream
L0101 | - CLI and UI must produce the same event semantics.
L0102 | 
L0103 | ---
L0104 | 
L0105 | ## 4) WebVM / Browser-OS angle: what it realistically adds
L0106 | 
L0107 | ### 4.1 WebVM is not the product
L0108 | It is a **sandbox / replay engine** that plugs into the shell.
L0109 | 
L0110 | Best uses:
L0111 | - **Deterministic replay** of runs (“flight recorder” playback).
L0112 | - **Capsule preview**: open a capsule in a safe environment before deploying to a fleet.
L0113 | - Support/debug: reproduce failures in-browser without local setup.
L0114 | 
L0115 | ### 4.2 Digital twins and replay debugger
L0116 | - Each device/agent has a “digital twin” in Ops view.
L0117 | - WebVM can replay exact context + event stream from a run snapshot.
L0118 | 
L0119 | ### 4.3 Connectivity reality
L0120 | We decided: do **always-connected** for browser shell v1.
L0121 | Intermittent support is still planned for edge agents via:
L0122 | - local queues
L0123 | - store-and-forward
L0124 | - signed envelopes
L0125 | - idempotent commands
L0126 | 
L0127 | ---
L0128 | 
L0129 | ## 5) The critical architectural tension: compiled tools vs runtime tool recognition
L0130 | 
L0131 | You identified a major issue:
L0132 | - If tools are compiled into runtime, agents cannot add tools dynamically.
L0133 | 
L0134 | We resolved with a **two-class tool system**:
L0135 | 
L0136 | ### Class A: Kernel tools (built-in, Rust)
L0137 | Security-critical primitives:
L0138 | - verification
L0139 | - policy evaluation hooks
L0140 | - event ledger writer
L0141 | - capsule resolver
L0142 | - core registry plumbing
L0143 | 
L0144 | ### Class B: Extensible tools (dynamic, runtime-discoverable)
L0145 | Loaded/recognized at runtime via Tool Registry:
L0146 | - WASM tools (preferred future plugin format)
L0147 | - external services (Python “skills gateway”, HTTP/gRPC)
L0148 | - cloud jobs/containers for heavy dependencies
L0149 | 
L0150 | Rule: **No kernel rebuild required** to add a new tool.
L0151 | 
L0152 | ---
L0153 | 
L0154 | ## 6) WASM vs Rust vs Python (decisions and rationale)
L0155 | 
L0156 | ### 6.1 Keep Rust
L0157 | Yes:
L0158 | - Rust is best for the trust boundary: verification, policy, event ingest, capsule tooling, edge runtime.
L0159 | - It also keeps the door open for strong WASM hosting later.
L0160 | 
L0161 | ### 6.2 Is WASM necessary right now?
L0162 | - **Not necessary for MVP-1**.
L0163 | - But: you must **design the ToolABI and capsule schema now** to support WASM later **without refactor**.
L0164 | 
L0165 | Practical rule:
L0166 | - **Design for WASM now; execute WASM later.**
L0167 | 
L0168 | ### 6.3 Python integration
L0169 | Yes, but:
L0170 | - Python should be an **external Skills Gateway**, not embedded in the kernel trust boundary.
L0171 | - Python is best for speed and breadth of integrations.
L0172 | - All calls must be ToolABI mediated, policy checked, and event logged.
L0173 | 
L0174 | Trust boundary rule:
L0175 | - **Inside trust boundary:** Rust/WASM only  
L0176 | - **Outside trust boundary:** Python and other services as tool providers
L0177 | 
L0178 | ---
L0179 | 
L0180 | ## 7) Voice + Gizzi assistant + terminal “futurism” (but safety-first)
L0181 | 
L0182 | ### 7.1 Terminal becomes an action console
L0183 | - Browser terminal is persistent per workspace/tenant.
L0184 | - Gizzi assistant runs side-by-side:
L0185 |   - summarizes runs
L0186 |   - proposes actions
L0187 |   - explains failures using event stream context
L0188 | 
L0189 | ### 7.2 Voice I/O pipeline (safe)
L0190 | - Voice capture → STT transcript.
L0191 | - Assistant interprets transcript and produces either:
L0192 |   - response
L0193 |   - proposed actions (structured)
L0194 | - High-risk actions require confirmation.
L0195 | - Actions execute through a restricted **Action API** (not arbitrary tool execution).
L0196 | - Everything emits events and audit artifacts.
L0197 | 
L0198 | ### 7.3 Orb/waveform UI
L0199 | Not “eye candy”; it is a state machine indicator:
L0200 | - Idle / Listening / Thinking / Acting / Speaking
L0201 | Driven by real pipeline state.
L0202 | 
L0203 | ---
L0204 | 
L0205 | ## 8) Decisions we locked to avoid scope explosion
L0206 | 
L0207 | **Ship order:** Cloud runner first  
L0208 | **Connectivity (v1):** Always-connected  
L0209 | **Local connector daemon (v1):** Defer
L0210 | 
L0211 | Rationale:
L0212 | - Cloud runner makes “real work” happen now.
L0213 | - Offline-first browser shell introduces major complexity (sync, auth, policy offline).
L0214 | - Local connector daemon expands attack surface (USB/FS/BLE/camera) and should come after policy/verify maturity.
L0215 | 
L0216 | ---
L0217 | 
L0218 | ## 9) MVP sequencing (the plan)
L0219 | 
L0220 | ### MVP-1: Browser-OS shell + CLI + cloud runs (no WebVM execution yet)
L0221 | Goal: it already feels like an OS.
L0222 | 
L0223 | Deliver:
L0224 | - Shell with modes (Authoring/Ops/Shell)
L0225 | - Terminal panel (web PTY)
L0226 | - Capsule registry basics (upload/list/inspect)
L0227 | - Runs: start/stop/status + live event stream timeline
L0228 | - Artifacts view: list + verify status
L0229 | - Policy decisions surfaced (even if minimal)
L0230 | - Gizzi text assistant integrated in Shell Mode
L0231 | - Voice v0: capture → transcript displayed (no auto actions yet)
L0232 | 
L0233 | ### MVP-2: Strict capsule lifecycle + assistant actions
L0234 | Deliver:
L0235 | - Capsule build/verify required to run
L0236 | - Deny-by-default policy enforcement
L0237 | - Action API for assistant (safe subset)
L0238 | - Voice v1: speech → action suggestions → confirm → execute → audit
L0239 | 
L0240 | ### MVP-3: WebVM replay plugin
L0241 | Deliver:
L0242 | - “Replay run” loads capsule snapshot + event stream in WebVM
L0243 | - Read-only step-through debugger
L0244 | - Optional stubs/mocks to re-execute deterministically later
L0245 | 
L0246 | ---
L0247 | 
L0248 | ## 10) Integration-first: OSS projects we identified to avoid building from scratch
L0249 | 
L0250 | > These are the categories we agreed to use; final selection should be pinned as submodules/forks.
L0251 | 
L0252 | **Browser shell / web console**
L0253 | - Open WebUI (fast “OS shell” baseline) — fork + convert to modes
L0254 | - Alternative: Next.js SaaS shells/admin templates if you want lighter baseline
L0255 | 
L0256 | **Terminal in browser**
L0257 | - xterm.js + backend PTY bridge (ttyd/wetty or your own WS PTY)
L0258 | 
L0259 | **WebVM / sandbox**
L0260 | - WebVM (leaningtech/webvm) as replay/sandbox substrate
L0261 | 
L0262 | **Capsule signing / distribution**
L0263 | - Sigstore/cosign for signing
L0264 | - ORAS for OCI artifact distribution (capsules as OCI artifacts)
L0265 | 
L0266 | **Event streaming / ledger**
L0267 | - NATS (+ JetStream) as event bus + persistence (or equivalent)
L0268 | 
L0269 | **Policy engine**
L0270 | - OPA (Open Policy Agent) as policy core (or equivalent), integrated as PolicyDecisionArtifact generator
L0271 | 
L0272 | **Assistant orchestration**
L0273 | - LangGraph-style graph orchestration for safe action planning (optional; can be minimal at first)
L0274 | 
L0275 | **Voice**
L0276 | - whisper.cpp (STT) + Coqui TTS (open) as sovereign options
L0277 | - ElevenLabs as premium drop-in later
L0278 | 
L0279 | **Automation**
L0280 | - Playwright for browser automation (gated)
L0281 | - Home Assistant for device control (gated, later phase)
L0282 | 
L0283 | ---
L0284 | 
L0285 | ## 11) The “do not drift” rules we established
L0286 | 
L0287 | 1) Everything is a capsule or a tool.  
L0288 | 2) Everything emits events to the ledger.  
L0289 | 3) Every tool invocation is policy-gated.  
L0290 | 4) UI/CLI/voice all call the same Action layer.  
L0291 | 5) New tools must be recognized at runtime without kernel rebuild.  
L0292 | 6) Rust/WASM in trust boundary; Python is external tool provider.  
L0293 | 7) Cloud runner first; WebVM replay later; local connector daemon later.
L0294 | 
L0295 | ---
L0296 | 
L0297 | ## 12) Immediate execution checklist (what to implement first)
L0298 | 
L0299 | ### A) Lock contracts (prevents refactor later)
L0300 | - ToolABI schema v0 (WIT-shaped)
L0301 | - Capsule manifest schema v0 (payload types: service/container/wasm stub)
L0302 | - EventEnvelope schema v0
L0303 | - PolicyDecision schema v0
L0304 | - VerifyArtifacts requirements
L0305 | 
L0306 | ### B) Kernel services (Rust)
L0307 | - Tool Registry service (register/list/resolve)
L0308 | - Capsule service (store/verify/inspect)
L0309 | - Policy gate service (deny-by-default)
L0310 | - Event ledger service (append-only, stream)
L0311 | - Run dispatcher to cloud runner
L0312 | 
L0313 | ### C) CLI
L0314 | - `capsule build/verify/inspect`
L0315 | - `tool register/list`
L0316 | - `run start/inspect/tail`
L0317 | 
L0318 | ### D) Browser-OS shell
L0319 | - Mode switch + workspace tree
L0320 | - Terminal panel
L0321 | - Ops timeline viewer (events)
L0322 | - Capsules list/detail
L0323 | - Runs list/detail
L0324 | 
L0325 | ### E) Gizzi + voice v0
L0326 | - assistant pane contextual to workspace/run
L0327 | - voice capture → transcript (no auto actions yet)
L0328 | 
L0329 | ---
L0330 | 
L0331 | ## 13) Open questions we did not need to resolve yet (but tracked)
L0332 | 
L0333 | - Exact UI base fork: Open WebUI vs a Next.js template baseline.
L0334 | - Exact event storage backing (NATS JetStream vs DB + pubsub).
L0335 | - Whether to adopt OCI artifacts for capsules immediately or start with tar.zst and migrate.
L0336 | - When to introduce WASM tool execution (planned after MVP-1/2 once contracts stabilize).
L0337 | - When to add local connector daemon + capability prompts.
L0338 | 
L0339 | ---
L0340 | 
L0341 | ## 14) Outcome of the session (one sentence)
L0342 | 
L0343 | We aligned on shipping a **desktop-like Browser-OS shell + CLI** as the human gateway, while implementing the real agentic OS as a **contract-enforced kernel** centered on **Capsules + ToolABI + Policy + Verify + Event Ledger**, designed for WASM extensibility without forcing WASM execution in MVP-1.
```

```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — Memory Architecture, Proof-Gating, and External Memory Systems
L0002 | **Topic:** Memory v2 (truth-preserving memory), proof-gated verification, and evaluating external “memory engines” (Weaviate / Cognee) + meta-prompting workflows (Compound Engineering Plugin)  
L0003 | **Date:** 2026-01-26
L0004 | 
L0005 | ---
L0006 | 
L0007 | ## 1) Objective of this session
L0008 | Build an agent memory architecture that **scales**, preserves **truth over time**, avoids “fake remembering,” and is **provable** (not just claimed). The session focused on:
L0009 | - Designing / implementing **Memory v2** with truth maintenance, conflict resolution, decay/maintenance, and graph memory.
L0010 | - Establishing a **proof-gated audit process** so “implementation theater” cannot pass.
L0011 | - Reevaluating whether external projects (Cognee, Weaviate context-engineering) can replace or augment A2rchitech’s memory stack.
L0012 | - Identifying how a workflow plugin (EveryInc compound-engineering-plugin) can improve **meta prompting** and **agent learning loops**.
L0013 | 
L0014 | ---
L0015 | 
L0016 | ## 2) Key artifacts created / referenced (local)
L0017 | ### Specs & explainers
L0018 | - `A2rchitech_Memory_Architecture_Spec.md`  
L0019 |   Path: `/mnt/data/A2rchitech_Memory_Architecture_Spec.md`
L0020 | - `Memory_Structure_Quick_Explainer.md`  
L0021 |   Path: `/mnt/data/Memory_Structure_Quick_Explainer.md`
L0022 | 
L0023 | ### Implementation packs (zips)
L0024 | - `a2rchitech_memory_upgrade_pack.zip`  
L0025 |   Path: `/mnt/data/a2rchitech_memory_upgrade_pack.zip`
L0026 | - `a2rchitech_memory_v2_full.zip`  
L0027 |   Path: `/mnt/data/a2rchitech_memory_v2_full.zip`
L0028 | 
L0029 | ---
L0030 | 
L0031 | ## 3) Memory v2: what was intended
L0032 | ### Core properties (non-negotiables)
L0033 | - **Truth-preserving writes** (no overwrite truth loss):
L0034 |   - Supersession chains instead of overwrites
L0035 |   - `status`, `valid_from`, `valid_to`, `authority`, `confidence`, `supersedes_memory_id`
L0036 | - **Conflict resolution** strategies:
L0037 |   - Temporal shift (job changes, preference changes)
L0038 |   - Parallel truths (multiple valid contexts)
L0039 |   - Overwrite-with-archive (preserve history)
L0040 | - **Decay + maintenance**:
L0041 |   - Scheduled consolidation and pruning without deleting “source of truth”
L0042 |   - Nightly/weekly/monthly maintenance patterns
L0043 | - **Context decision trees**:
L0044 |   - Summaries-first → sufficiency check → drill-down
L0045 |   - Graph retrieval invoked only when routed
L0046 |   - Token budgets enforced by ContextBundle/ContextBudgets
L0047 | - **Graph memory**:
L0048 |   - Subject–predicate–object edges with provenance references
L0049 |   - Traversal that degrades gracefully if graph unavailable
L0050 | - **RLM integration**:
L0051 |   - Memory policies influenced by meta-learning outputs:
L0052 |     - write strength
L0053 |     - recency bias
L0054 |     - confidence bias
L0055 |     - decay aggressiveness
L0056 |     - memory budgets
L0057 | 
L0058 | ---
L0059 | 
L0060 | ## 4) The critical process breakthrough: proof-gating
L0061 | ### Why it mattered
L0062 | Without adversarial proof, you get **“implementation theater”**: structs/files exist, but kernel wiring and testability can be missing.
L0063 | 
L0064 | ### Proof-gated audit (what “PASS” must require)
L0065 | - Forbidden-pattern grep (no overwrite semantics / placeholders)
L0066 | - SQLite schema dumps + index verification
L0067 | - Minimal repro proving supersession chains (old -> superseded, new row inserted)
L0068 | - Runtime proof for maintenance daemon (tick logs/metrics)
L0069 | - `cargo test` proof, with regression traps
L0070 | 
L0071 | ---
L0072 | 
L0073 | ## 5) Results: MEMORY_V2_PROOF_REPORT.md (agent-provided)
L0074 | ### Executive verdict
L0075 | **PARTIAL** — largely complete, but with **critical integration/test gaps**.
L0076 | 
L0077 | ### Pass/Fail summary
L0078 | - **R1 Truth preservation:** PASS (no `INSERT OR REPLACE`; supersession logic present)
L0079 | - **R2 Schema:** PASS (new columns + indexes + `graph_edges`)
L0080 | - **R3 Daemon integration:** PARTIAL (daemon exists, kernel boot had placeholder wiring)
L0081 | - **R4 Context routing:** PASS (decision tree invoked; graph gated by route)
L0082 | - **R5 RLM governance:** PASS (policy outputs exist)
L0083 | - **R6 Graph safety:** PASS (additive store; errors handled)
L0084 | - **R7 Tests:** PARTIAL/FAIL (2 tests failed due to missing harness/schema/fs init)
L0085 | 
L0086 | ### Gaps discovered (the key takeaway)
L0087 | 1) **Maintenance daemon not actually integrated**  
L0088 |    - `main.rs` had placeholder spawn text (“would start here”).
L0089 | 2) **No runtime proof instrumentation**  
L0090 |    - Missing tick logs/metrics to prove daemon execution.
L0091 | 3) **Failing tests**  
L0092 |    - `Io(NotFound)` suggests missing test directories/schema initialization.
L0093 | 
L0094 | ### Conclusion
L0095 | Proof-gating prevented false confidence and exposed missing wiring.
L0096 | 
L0097 | ---
L0098 | 
L0099 | ## 6) External memory systems: Weaviate “Context Engineering” + Cognee
L0100 | ### Weaviate (context engineering model)
L0101 | - Conceptual alignment: working vs long-term memory, episodic/semantic/procedural separation, save/load task state, selective retrieval, avoiding naive “dump history”.
L0102 | Reference:
L0103 | - `https://weaviate.io/blog/context-engineering`
L0104 | 
L0105 | ### Cognee (open-source)
L0106 | Stance reached:
L0107 | - Cognee/Weaviate-style systems are best treated as **memory substrates** (graph/vector + ingestion).
L0108 | - A2rchitech Memory v2 is the **control plane** (truth semantics, governance, budgets, auditability).
L0109 | - Therefore, Cognee is a backend candidate under MemoryFabric—not a replacement for TruthEngine/Policy/Routing.
L0110 | 
L0111 | ---
L0112 | 
L0113 | ## 7) Meta prompting and agent learning: EveryInc “compound-engineering-plugin”
L0114 | Repo:
L0115 | - `https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin`
L0116 | 
L0117 | What it contributes (pattern-level):
L0118 | - A repeatable loop: **plan → work → review → compound**
L0119 | - Structured command/prompt scaffolding for decomposition and verification
L0120 | - A model for capturing “learning” into reusable artifacts (procedural memory)
L0121 | 
L0122 | Integration idea:
L0123 | - Use as a **meta-prompting workflow layer** feeding procedural memories + proofs into A2rchitech’s memory substrate.
L0124 | 
L0125 | ---
L0126 | 
L0127 | ## 8) Decisions and directional stance
L0128 | 1) Proof-gating is mandatory for kernel/memory-critical systems.  
L0129 | 2) External memory engines are **backends**, not authority layers.  
L0130 | 3) A2rchitech’s differentiator is **epistemic memory** (truth maintenance + governance + auditability).
L0131 | 
L0132 | ---
L0133 | 
L0134 | ## 9) Next actions (high priority)
L0135 | ### A) Close the verified gaps (R3 + R7)
L0136 | 1) Wire `MemoryMaintenanceDaemon` to real `Arc<MemoryFabric>` from AppState; delete placeholder boot code.  
L0137 | 2) Add tick instrumentation + dev/test interval override (env var).  
L0138 | 3) Fix tests with a proper harness: TempDir, db path creation, schema init, required FS dirs.
L0139 | 
L0140 | ### B) Add a single Proof Gate script
L0141 | Create `tools/proof/memory_v2_gate.sh`:
L0142 | - forbidden grep
L0143 | - schema dump
L0144 | - supersession repro
L0145 | - daemon tick proof
L0146 | - cargo tests
L0147 | Agents must run it and attach raw output for PASS.
L0148 | 
L0149 | ### C) Cognee evaluation (optional)
L0150 | Run a repo-cited adversarial audit:
L0151 | - versioning + conflict model
L0152 | - decay/maintenance jobs
L0153 | - provenance + validity timelines
L0154 | - integration mode (in-proc vs service)
L0155 | 
L0156 | ---
L0157 | 
L0158 | ## 10) One-line outcome
L0159 | This session established that **memory correctness requires proof, not promises**, and positioned A2rchitech Memory v2 as an **epistemically governed memory control-plane** with pluggable backends.
```

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech Session Summary — Skills Discovery (2026-01-26)
L0002 | 
L0003 | ## Scope
L0004 | This session focused on **agent skill discovery, installation, and packaging** as a modular capability layer for **A2rchitech** (agentic productivity OS / orchestration platform).
L0005 | 
L0006 | ---
L0007 | 
L0008 | ## Key Concepts Locked In
L0009 | 
L0010 | ### 1) “Skills” as portable procedural capability
L0011 | - A *skill* is a reusable, composable **procedure module** for agents (workflows, conventions, step lists, prompts, guardrails).
L0012 | - Skills differ from “docs grounding”: skills encode **how to do things** (process), not just information.
L0013 | 
L0014 | ### 2) Vercel `skills.sh` ecosystem
L0015 | - `skills.sh` is treated as a **centralized, searchable skill directory + install mechanism** (npm-like ergonomics for agent skills).
L0016 | - Installation pattern:
L0017 |   - `npx skills add <owner/repo>` to import a repo of skills into an agent environment.
L0018 | - Skills typically live as `SKILL.md` files with structured metadata + instructions.
L0019 | - The intended value: fast discovery + one-command install + telemetry-driven ranking.
L0020 | 
L0021 | ### 3) Context7 “skills”
L0022 | - Context7 is positioned as **docs grounding** for agents: “retrieve correct, current docs/examples” to reduce outdated guidance.
L0023 | - In skills ecosystems, “Context7 skills” are wrappers that let an agent:
L0024 |   1. search for a library/topic,
L0025 |   2. pull relevant docs snippets,
L0026 |   3. feed them into planning/coding.
L0027 | - Important separation:
L0028 |   - **Context7 = grounding substrate**
L0029 |   - **skills.sh = procedural capability distribution + install UX**
L0030 | 
L0031 | ---
L0032 | 
L0033 | ## Commands & Artifacts Mentioned
L0034 | 
L0035 | ### Context7 CLI usage (as referenced)
L0036 | - Example pattern:
L0037 |   - `npx ctx7 skills search "Better Auth"`
L0038 | - Claimed characteristics (as referenced):
L0039 |   - “Search across ~24k+ skills”
L0040 |   - “Single-command install”
L0041 |   - “Generate your own skills from Context7 docs” (coming soon)
L0042 | 
L0043 | ### skills.sh mention
L0044 | - `skills.sh` described as Vercel’s skill discovery + install surface.
L0045 | 
L0046 | ### Redirected link: agent-toolkit
L0047 | - A t.co link was shared and interpreted as routing to a GitHub repo:
L0048 |   - `softaworks/agent-toolkit`
L0049 | - Purpose: curated collection of skills for AI coding agents.
L0050 | - Suggested install pattern:
L0051 |   - `npx skills add softaworks/agent-toolkit`
L0052 | 
L0053 | ---
L0054 | 
L0055 | ## Decisions / Takeaways for A2rchitech
L0056 | 
L0057 | ### A) Discovery strategy
L0058 | - Treat A2rchitech “Skills Discovery” as a **pluggable provider layer**:
L0059 |   - Provider: `skills.sh` directory + install method
L0060 |   - Provider: Context7 doc-grounding endpoints (as a “grounding skill”)
L0061 |   - Provider: curated repos (e.g., `agent-toolkit`) as “skill bundles”
L0062 | 
L0063 | ### B) Architecture implications
L0064 | - A2rchitech should represent skills as first-class objects:
L0065 |   - `Skill` (metadata)
L0066 |   - `InstallSource` (repo/url/registry)
L0067 |   - `RuntimeBinding` (how installed skill is injected into agent context)
L0068 | - Separate “procedural skill” from “grounding connector”:
L0069 |   - Procedural = workflows
L0070 |   - Grounding = retrieve canonical docs/snippets
L0071 | 
L0072 | ### C) What this enables downstream
L0073 | - One-place discovery + install inside A2rchitech, with:
L0074 |   - search (registry/provider)
L0075 |   - install (one command / action)
L0076 |   - activation (attach to agent/profile/workspace)
L0077 |   - provenance (where it came from)
L0078 |   - version pinning (reproducible agent behavior)
L0079 | 
L0080 | ---
L0081 | 
L0082 | ## Open Items / Next Build Targets (Skills Discovery)
L0083 | 1. **Provider adapters**
L0084 |    - Adapter: skills.sh search + install
L0085 |    - Adapter: Context7 search + fetch docs
L0086 |    - Adapter: GitHub repo bundle import (SKILL.md discovery)
L0087 | 2. **Skill registry in A2rchitech**
L0088 |    - local index + provenance + versions + tags
L0089 | 3. **UX**
L0090 |    - search → preview → install → enable per agent/workspace
L0091 | 4. **Policy**
L0092 |    - allowlists/denylists; signature or trust scoring; pin versions by default
L0093 | 
L0094 | ---
L0095 | 
L0096 | ## Minimal “Provider Interface” Sketch (for internal design)
L0097 | - `search(query) -> [SkillHit]`
L0098 | - `resolve(skillId) -> SkillManifest`
L0099 | - `install(manifest, target) -> InstallResult`
L0100 | - `enable(skillRef, agentProfile/workspace) -> EnableResult`

##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # ElizaOS → A2rchitech Unified Integration Spec
L0002 | ## Agent Runtime, Plugins, Memory, Orchestration & Safety Mapping
L0003 | 
L0004 | ---
L0005 | 
L0006 | ## 0. Purpose of This Document
L0007 | 
L0008 | This document defines how primitives from ElizaOS are integrated into the A2rchitech Unified Architecture as first-class agent runtime components, while enforcing strict standards for determinism, safety, modularity, auditability, and long-term system evolution.
L0009 | 
L0010 | This document is binding integration policy, not exploratory research.
L0011 | 
L0012 | ---
L0013 | 
L0014 | ## 1. What ElizaOS Contributes (Abstracted)
L0015 | 
L0016 | ElizaOS contributes four core primitives:
L0017 | 
L0018 | 1. Stateful Agent Runtime  
L0019 | 2. Event-Driven Execution Model  
L0020 | 3. Plugin-Based Capability System  
L0021 | 4. Multi-Agent Coordination Fabric  
L0022 | 
L0023 | These primitives are adopted only after constraint, typing, and verification.
L0024 | 
L0025 | ---
L0026 | 
L0027 | ## 2. Canonical Mapping to A2rchitech Layers
L0028 | 
L0029 | | ElizaOS Primitive | A2rchitech Layer | Enhancement Applied |
L0030 | |------------------|------------------|---------------------|
L0031 | | Agent Runtime | Agent Execution Layer | Typed goals, quotas, lifecycle control |
L0032 | | Persistent Memory | Memory & Knowledge Layer | Knowledge graph + audit log |
L0033 | | Event Bus | Context & Orchestration Layer | Deterministic event calculus |
L0034 | | Plugins | Tool / Capability Layer | Contract-verified modules |
L0035 | | Multi-Agent Swarms | Coordination Layer | Role-based DAG execution |
L0036 | 
L0037 | ---
L0038 | 
L0039 | ## 3. Agent Runtime Integration
L0040 | 
L0041 | ElizaOS runtime operates as a subordinate execution engine under A2rchitech supervision.
L0042 | 
L0043 | Agents:
L0044 | - cannot self-authorize
L0045 | - cannot self-expand memory
L0046 | - cannot perform irreversible actions without verification
L0047 | 
L0048 | Lifecycle:
L0049 | 1. Initialization
L0050 | 2. Context hydration
L0051 | 3. Execution window
L0052 | 4. Verification
L0053 | 5. Commit / rollback
L0054 | 6. Audit logging
L0055 | 
L0056 | ---
L0057 | 
L0058 | ## 4. Memory & State Policy
L0059 | 
L0060 | Long-term memory is replaced with:
L0061 | - Structured knowledge graphs
L0062 | - Append-only audit trails
L0063 | - Explicit permissions
L0064 | - Versioned snapshots
L0065 | 
L0066 | Prohibited:
L0067 | - self-modifying memory
L0068 | - uncontrolled persistence
L0069 | 
L0070 | ---
L0071 | 
L0072 | ## 5. Event System
L0073 | 
L0074 | All events normalize into structured event objects with constraints and verification requirements.
L0075 | 
L0076 | No raw text reaches execution directly.
L0077 | 
L0078 | ---
L0079 | 
L0080 | ## 6. Plugin System
L0081 | 
L0082 | Plugins are treated as contracts with:
L0083 | - typed inputs/outputs
L0084 | - declared side effects
L0085 | - scoped permissions
L0086 | 
L0087 | Execution requires schema validation and policy compliance.
L0088 | 
L0089 | ---
L0090 | 
L0091 | ## 7. Multi-Agent Orchestration
L0092 | 
L0093 | Agents operate with explicit roles:
L0094 | - Planner
L0095 | - Executor
L0096 | - Verifier
L0097 | - Observer
L0098 | - Archivist
L0099 | 
L0100 | Tasks are DAG-based. Consensus is required for irreversible actions.
L0101 | 
L0102 | ---
L0103 | 
L0104 | ## 8. Safety & Constraints
L0105 | 
L0106 | Every action must satisfy:
L0107 | - intent constraints
L0108 | - system policy
L0109 | - role permissions
L0110 | - resource quotas
L0111 | 
L0112 | Violations terminate the agent instance.
L0113 | 
L0114 | ---
L0115 | 
L0116 | ## 9. Deployment Modes
L0117 | 
L0118 | Supported:
L0119 | - Local
L0120 | - Edge
L0121 | - Cloud
L0122 | - Hybrid
L0123 | 
L0124 | All synchronization is explicit and deterministic.
L0125 | 
L0126 | ---
L0127 | 
L0128 | ## 10. Gizzi OS Alignment
L0129 | 
L0130 | ElizaOS primitives enable autonomous agents.
L0131 | A2rchitech upgrades them into governed, verifiable digital assistants.
L0132 | 
L0133 | ---
L0134 | 
L0135 | ## 11. Hard Rules
L0136 | 
L0137 | 1. No agent is trusted by default
L0138 | 2. No memory is permanent by default
L0139 | 3. No action without validation
L0140 | 4. No plugin without a contract
L0141 | 5. No swarm without coordination logic
L0142 | 
L0143 | ---
L0144 | 
L0145 | ## 12. Status
L0146 | 
L0147 | Integration: Approved  
L0148 | Adoption: Selective  
L0149 | Modification: Mandatory  
L0150 | Governance: Strict  
L0151 | 
L0152 | This file is a canonical reference for future integrations.
L0153 | 
L0154 | END OF SPEC
```

##### Claims about architecture (tagged by keyword)
- L0001 [UNHEADED] Viable at scale: yes, if you implement it as a contract surface + optional daemon backends, not “spawn 50 binaries per minute forever.”
- L0003 [UNHEADED] What you’re locking in is a topology that is structurally aligned with swarms/orchestration because it turns the platform into a set of typed capabilities with deterministic IO, policy gates, artifact provenance, and version enforcement. Those are exactly the properties swarms need to avoid chaos.
- L0005 [UNHEADED] Why this topology scales with agent swarms
- L0007 [UNHEADED] Agent swarms fail for predictable reasons:
- L0010 [UNHEADED] •	side effects aren’t controlled (no single-writer, no policy)
- L0015 [UNHEADED] •	Each layer = a capability module (runtime, memory, browser, skills, artifacts, policy, repo cartography)
- L0016 [UNHEADED] •	Contracts = the swarm’s “physics” (what is callable, what is returned, what is denied)
- L0017 [UNHEADED] •	Artifacts = the swarm’s shared substrate (every step yields stable IDs; workflows are traceable and replayable)
- L0032 [UNHEADED] •	hot paths run via daemon mode / library mode
- L0033 [UNHEADED] •	the CLI becomes a thin client to the daemon when throughput matters
- L0036 [UNHEADED] •	Interface layer: CLI contract (schemas, exit codes, artifacts)
- L0037 [UNHEADED] •	Execution layer: daemon services (long-lived, pooled, concurrent)
- L0038 [UNHEADED] •	Routing layer: a2 dispatch + workflow runner
- L0039 [UNHEADED] •	Orchestration layer: swarms call the same tool surfaces, get the same artifacts
- L0047 [UNHEADED] •	UI bypasses tools (invariants split, policies bypassed)
- L0052 [UNHEADED] If you want this topology to be swarm-ready, you need these scale primitives:
- L0053 [Daemon mode for high-frequency layers] 1.	Daemon mode for high-frequency layers
- L0055 [Daemon mode for high-frequency layers] •	runtime, browser, memory, artifact store typically need long-lived processes
- L0084 [A2rchitech — Layered CLI Implementation Blueprint] > Goal: implement a modular, contract-driven CLI layer system with a thin router, artifact-first IO, workflow runner, compatibility enforcement, and CI gates.
