##### Full Extract (verbatim with line numbers)
```text
L0001 | # A2rchitech ↔ Personal AI Infrastructure (PAI) — Formal Spec, Adoption Plan, and PAIMM Tier Mapping
L0002 | 
L0003 | Date: 2026-01-26  
L0004 | Author: Gizzi (for Joe Dirte)
L0005 | 
L0006 | ---
L0007 | 
L0008 | ## 0) What we’re doing (objective)
L0009 | 
L0010 | You asked for:  
L0011 | 1) a **formal platform spec** that translates Daniel Miessler’s PAI v2 ideas into an implementable product architecture, and  
L0012 | 2) a **concrete plan to adopt + enforce** this architecture inside **A2rchitech OS**, including the option to fork Miessler’s repo.
L0013 | 
L0014 | This document treats Miessler’s system as a **reference implementation + design pattern**, and rewrites it into:  
L0015 | - a **multi-tenant kernel specification**, and  
L0016 | - an **enforcement-first implementation plan**.
L0017 | 
L0018 | ---
L0019 | 
L0020 | ## 1) Miessler PAI v2: the “setup” we should mirror (extract + normalization)
L0021 | 
L0022 | ### 1.1 Core building blocks (what exists in his repo/system)
L0023 | Based on Miessler’s PAI repo and his PAI/PAIMM posts, the system is organized around:  
L0024 | - **Skills**: self-contained domain packages that include routing (`SKILL.md`), step-by-step procedures (`Workflows/`), and deterministic scripts (`Tools/`). citeturn20search8turn19search7  
L0025 | - **Hooks**: event-driven automations that run at lifecycle moments (session start/stop, pre/post tool use, sub-agent stop) to capture outputs, enforce policies, and trigger follow-on actions. citeturn19search7turn20search8  
L0026 | - **History / UOCS**: automatic session/output capture so work becomes searchable, reusable knowledge. citeturn19search7turn20search8  
L0027 | - **Agents**: named and dynamic role-specialized agents composed from templates; routed to a subset of skills. citeturn19search7turn20search8  
L0028 | - **CLI-first operation**: the system is built to be driven from the terminal and scripted/composed (UNIX philosophy). citeturn19search7turn20search8  
L0029 | - **Modular packs + releases**: PAI evolves via releases that package skill/hook/workflow inventories (recent release notes list counts). citeturn20search8  
L0030 | - **Security layering**: “constitutional” refusal of external instructions + pre-execution validation + allow-lists + logging/audit. citeturn19search7  
L0031 | 
L0032 | ### 1.2 Why this mirrors A2rchitech specifically
L0033 | A2rchitech’s OS goals (capsules, skills marketplace, agent orchestration, memory/history, law layer, deterministic boot) align tightly with Miessler’s “system > model” thesis and his emphasis on **routing + scaffolding + capture** over model IQ. citeturn19search7turn20search8
L0034 | 
L0035 | Translation: **PAI is a “single-user kernel” pattern**.  
L0036 | A2rchitech must implement **the same pattern as a multi-tenant kernel** with stronger governance.
L0037 | 
L0038 | ---
L0039 | 
L0040 | ## 2) Formal platform specification: Multi-Tenant PAI Kernel (A2rchitech)
L0041 | 
L0042 | ### 2.1 Scope
L0043 | Build a kernel layer (“PAI Kernel”) that provides:
L0044 | - deterministic boot order
L0045 | - tenant/workspace isolation
L0046 | - skill/hook/agent packaging
L0047 | - context routing + memory hydration
L0048 | - tool governance + auditability
L0049 | - extensibility (marketplace) without trust collapse
L0050 | 
L0051 | This kernel becomes a **subsystem of A2rchitech OS**, not a separate product.
L0052 | 
L0053 | ---
L0054 | 
L0055 | ## 3) Definitions (kernel-level)
L0056 | 
L0057 | ### 3.1 Tenancy objects
L0058 | **Tenant**
L0059 | - owner identity, billing/entitlements, policy boundary
L0060 | - owns: workspaces, secrets, audit logs, allowed tools, installed packs
L0061 | 
L0062 | **Workspace**
L0063 | - a project/life-domain context (e.g., “A2rchitech”, “Terra Source”, “Ëtrid”)
L0064 | - selects skillpacks/agentpacks/hookpacks
L0065 | - has an execution environment mapping (local, remote, webvm, container)
L0066 | 
L0067 | **Session**
L0068 | - execution unit with lifecycle events (Start → ToolUse → Stop)
L0069 | - produces artifacts and updates state
L0070 | 
L0071 | ---
L0072 | 
L0073 | ## 4) Kernel architecture (normative)
L0074 | 
L0075 | ### 4.1 Kernel subsystems
L0076 | 1) **Pack Manager**
L0077 |    - installs/updates/removes signed packs (skillpack, agentpack, hookpack, toolpack)
L0078 |    - supports “forked upstream” provenance
L0079 |    - maintains lockfile with hashes
L0080 | 
L0081 | 2) **Router**
L0082 |    - routes user intent → skill → workflow → tools/agents
L0083 |    - supports deterministic routing via:
L0084 |      - trigger phrases
L0085 |      - directory/workspace constraints
L0086 |      - explicit “USE WHEN” rules (Miessler pattern) citeturn19search7
L0087 | 
L0088 | 3) **Context Hydrator**
L0089 |    - computes “minimum viable context” for:
L0090 |      - main assistant
L0091 |      - each sub-agent
L0092 |      - each tool execution
L0093 |    - sources:
L0094 |      - workspace memory
L0095 |      - skillpack docs
L0096 |      - last-N sessions
L0097 |      - embeddings/RAG (optional; not required for v1)
L0098 | 
L0099 | 4) **Execution Engine**
L0100 |    - executes:
L0101 |      - deterministic tools (scripts, CLIs, APIs)
L0102 |      - web agents / computer-use agents
L0103 |      - background jobs
L0104 |    - supports multiple execution targets:
L0105 |      - local host
L0106 |      - remote linux runner
L0107 |      - in-repo WebVM (linux wasm VM)
L0108 |      - container runtime
L0109 | 
L0110 | 5) **Hook Runtime**
L0111 |    - event bus: `SessionStart`, `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `SubagentStop`, `Stop`
L0112 |    - hooks are sandboxed, have explicit permissions
L0113 | 
L0114 | 6) **History + Artifact Store (UOCS-equivalent)**
L0115 |    - immutable append-only logs for:
L0116 |      - transcripts, tool I/O, prompts, deltas, evaluations
L0117 |    - artifact typing:
L0118 |      - `diff`, `log`, `doc`, `image`, `table`, `trace`, `preview`, `build`
L0119 | 
L0120 | 7) **Policy Engine**
L0121 |    - allow/deny tool usage
L0122 |    - protects sensitive paths/secrets
L0123 |    - enforces “external content is read-only” (constitutional layer) citeturn19search7
L0124 |    - verifies:
L0125 |      - tool arguments
L0126 |      - network egress
L0127 |      - file access boundaries
L0128 |      - SSRF blocks / private IP blocks
L0129 | 
L0130 | 8) **Observability**
L0131 |    - structured traces for each session and tool call
L0132 |    - metrics:
L0133 |      - latency, cost, tool error rate
L0134 |      - routing confidence
L0135 |      - “learned rules” adoption
L0136 | 
L0137 | ---
L0138 | 
L0139 | ## 5) Pack formats (A2rchitech spec)
L0140 | 
L0141 | ### 5.1 Skillpack
L0142 | **Required structure**
L0143 | ```
L0144 | /packs/<skillpack-name>/
L0145 |   pack.json
L0146 |   SKILL.md
L0147 |   Workflows/*.md
L0148 |   Tools/*
L0149 |   Tests/*
L0150 | ```
L0151 | 
L0152 | **pack.json (minimum)**
L0153 | - id, version, publisher, license
L0154 | - capabilities: `{ routing, workflows, tools }`
L0155 | - permissions request:
L0156 |   - file read/write scopes
L0157 |   - network scopes
L0158 |   - tool scopes (browser, shell, db)
L0159 | - triggers:
L0160 |   - keywords, workspace constraints, path constraints
L0161 | 
L0162 | ### 5.2 Hookpack
L0163 | ```
L0164 | /packs/<hookpack-name>/
L0165 |   pack.json
L0166 |   hooks/
L0167 |     session-start.ts
L0168 |     pre-tool-use.ts
L0169 |     post-tool-use.ts
L0170 |     subagent-stop.ts
L0171 |     stop.ts
L0172 | ```
L0173 | 
L0174 | ### 5.3 Agentpack
L0175 | ```
L0176 | /packs/<agentpack-name>/
L0177 |   pack.json
L0178 |   agents/
L0179 |     Engineer.md
L0180 |     Researcher.md
L0181 |     Architect.md
L0182 |   templates/
L0183 |     agent-template.mustache
L0184 |     roster.mustache
L0185 | ```
L0186 | 
L0187 | Design intent: reproduce Miessler’s “meta-prompting” / templated agents idea, but make it kernel-native and versioned. citeturn19search7
L0188 | 
L0189 | ---
L0190 | 
L0191 | ## 6) Deterministic boot order (B0 for PAI Kernel)
L0192 | 
L0193 | **B0.0** Load `/SOT.md` and `/agent/POLICY.md` (A2rchitech law layer)  
L0194 | **B0.1** Resolve tenant → workspace → selected packs  
L0195 | **B0.2** Load *core* pack set (mandatory):
L0196 | - policy engine
L0197 | - history store
L0198 | - router
L0199 | - baseline tool registry
L0200 | **B0.3** Hydrate “core context”:
L0201 | - user identity
L0202 | - high-level goals
L0203 | - workspace metadata
L0204 | **B0.4** Start hook runtime + observability  
L0205 | **B0.5** Start session
L0206 | 
L0207 | This mirrors Miessler’s emphasis on system scaffolding and repeatability, but upgraded to multi-tenant governance. citeturn19search7turn20search8
L0208 | 
L0209 | ---
L0210 | 
L0211 | ## 7) Enforcement model (how we prevent drift)
L0212 | 
L0213 | ### 7.1 Hard enforcement (non-negotiable)
L0214 | - **Signed pack install** (or locally trusted dev signing)
L0215 | - **AllowList tool registry**: tools must be declared in a pack, with permissions
L0216 | - **PreToolUse gate** (mandatory): policy evaluation runs before any tool execution citeturn19search7
L0217 | - **Append-only audit log**: all tool calls + outputs are captured
L0218 | - **Workspace boundary enforcement**: tools cannot read/write outside declared scopes
L0219 | 
L0220 | ### 7.2 Soft enforcement (quality)
L0221 | - “Spec/Test/Evals first” gate for packs that claim reliability citeturn19search7
L0222 | - periodic pack health checks
L0223 | - reproducibility tests for key workflows
L0224 | 
L0225 | ---
L0226 | 
L0227 | ## 8) Adoption plan: fork vs “selective transplant” (concrete)
L0228 | 
L0229 | ### 8.1 What to take from Miessler’s repo
L0230 | Even if you don’t keep Claude Code as the core, these assets are reusable patterns:
L0231 | - **pack taxonomy** (skills/hooks/agents/workflows/tools)
L0232 | - **SKILL.md routing grammar** (“USE WHEN …”)
L0233 | - **hook event model** (session lifecycle events)
L0234 | - **release discipline** (bundle frozen snapshots)
L0235 | - **inventory mentality** (count skills/hooks/workflows; release notes) citeturn20search8
L0236 | 
L0237 | ### 8.2 What NOT to take verbatim
L0238 | - single-user path assumptions (`~/.claude/...`)
L0239 | - implicit trust in local filesystem boundaries
L0240 | - provider-specific integrations (Claude Code specifics) as “kernel”
L0241 | - any “ad-hoc” script execution without permissions model
L0242 | 
L0243 | ### 8.3 Fork option (fastest)
L0244 | **Goal**: fork repo as `a2rchitech/pai-kernel-reference` and treat it as an upstream “reference kernel”.
L0245 | 
L0246 | Concrete steps:
L0247 | 1) Fork repo
L0248 | 2) Replace directory roots:
L0249 |    - from `~/.claude/...` to `/tenants/<tenantId>/workspaces/<workspaceId>/...`
L0250 | 3) Add `pack.json` to each pack
L0251 | 4) Implement signing + lockfile
L0252 | 5) Add policy engine that intercepts all tool execution
L0253 | 6) Build migration scripts:
L0254 |    - convert upstream packs → A2rchitech pack format
L0255 | 7) Maintain upstream tracking:
L0256 |    - periodic merges
L0257 |    - compatibility tests
L0258 | 
L0259 | ### 8.4 Selective transplant (cleaner long-term)
L0260 | **Goal**: implement PAI Kernel natively, and import only content:
L0261 | - copy skill docs and workflows as “content packs”
L0262 | - re-author tools into kernel tool registry
L0263 | - rewrite hooks into kernel hook runtime
L0264 | 
L0265 | This avoids inheriting assumptions, but costs more up front.
L0266 | 
L0267 | Recommendation for A2rchitech: **start with fork for speed**, then progressively replace components with kernel-native equivalents once stable.
L0268 | 
L0269 | ---
L0270 | 
L0271 | ## 9) A2rchitech roadmap: map Miessler’s PAIMM tiers explicitly
L0272 | 
L0273 | Miessler’s PAIMM is a 9-tier ladder: **Chatbots (CH1–CH3) → Agents (AG1–AG3) → Assistants (AS1–AS3)**. citeturn19search7
L0274 | 
L0275 | Below is an explicit mapping to A2rchitech OS capabilities.
L0276 | 
L0277 | ### CH1 — “Chat only”
L0278 | A2rchitech target:
L0279 | - single chat surface
L0280 | - no tools, no packs
L0281 | - no memory beyond conversation
L0282 | 
L0283 | Ship in A2rchitech:
L0284 | - basic chat capsule (already implied baseline)
L0285 | 
L0286 | ### CH2 — “Chat + basic tools + rudimentary memory”
L0287 | A2rchitech target:
L0288 | - tool registry v0 (manual tools)
L0289 | - minimal memory store (recent context)
L0290 | - basic artifact viewer
L0291 | 
L0292 | Ship:
L0293 | - tool abstraction + “artifact-first” outputs
L0294 | - minimal memory (per workspace)
L0295 | 
L0296 | ### CH3 — “Chatbot final form before agents”
L0297 | A2rchitech target:
L0298 | - advanced tools
L0299 | - stronger context loading
L0300 | - still mostly reactive
L0301 | 
L0302 | Ship:
L0303 | - pack manager v1
L0304 | - skills (routing + workflows + tools)
L0305 | 
L0306 | ### AG1 — “Standalone agents become viable”
L0307 | A2rchitech target:
L0308 | - spawn sub-agents for discrete tasks
L0309 | - early computer-use agent
L0310 | - mostly ephemeral
L0311 | 
L0312 | Ship:
L0313 | - agent runtime + agentpack
L0314 | - vision/web agent integration (UI-TARS widget already in repo per our sessions)
L0315 | 
L0316 | ### AG2 — “Agent systems become controllable/deterministic”
L0317 | A2rchitech target:
L0318 | - scaffolding system (“beads”, WIH, law layer)
L0319 | - background jobs/scheduled
L0320 | - stable task persistence
L0321 | 
L0322 | Ship:
L0323 | - deterministic orchestration: plan → run → verify loop
L0324 | - tasks as first-class objects (survive restarts)
L0325 | - hook runtime (pre/post tool use, stop, subagent stop)
L0326 | 
L0327 | ### AG3 — “Agents everywhere: continuous + device access”
L0328 | A2rchitech target:
L0329 | - local + cloud execution
L0330 | - mobile presence
L0331 | - viable computer use adoption
L0332 | 
L0333 | Ship:
L0334 | - multi-target execution (local/remote/webvm)
L0335 | - mobile client presence layer (lightweight)
L0336 | - policy hardening for remote operations
L0337 | 
L0338 | ### AS1 — “Named trusted assistant emerges”
L0339 | A2rchitech target:
L0340 | - persistent identity per workspace/tenant
L0341 | - personality + preferences
L0342 | - assistants orchestrate agents behind the scenes
L0343 | 
L0344 | Ship:
L0345 | - assistant identity model
L0346 | - long-lived “chief assistant” per workspace
L0347 | - background orchestration transparency layer
L0348 | 
L0349 | ### AS2 — “Proactive advocate (state → desired state management)”
L0350 | A2rchitech target:
L0351 | - state inventory and gap analysis
L0352 | - proactive suggestions + autonomous workflows gated by policy
L0353 | 
L0354 | Ship:
L0355 | - state model + desired state model
L0356 | - periodic inventories
L0357 | - action proposals + approval flows
L0358 | 
L0359 | ### AS3 — “Full DA: continuous advocate + senses + protection”
L0360 | A2rchitech target:
L0361 | - pervasive monitoring, wearable sensors, AR overlays (future)
L0362 | - extreme security + privacy controls
L0363 | 
L0364 | Ship (future):
L0365 | - opt-in sensing layer
L0366 | - comprehensive policy + attestation
L0367 | - trust & safety controls for “advocate” actions
L0368 | 
L0369 | **Key point:** A2rchitech’s immediate build (2026) should prioritize **CH3 → AG2**, because that is where “kernel + packs + hooks + history + deterministic routing” becomes a compounding advantage.
L0370 | 
L0371 | ---
L0372 | 
L0373 | ## 10) Critical review (trajectory risks + what to fix early)
L0374 | 
L0375 | ### 10.1 Risk: “Skills become a junk drawer”
L0376 | Mitigation:
L0377 | - enforce pack quality gates:
L0378 |   - required SKILL.md triggers
L0379 |   - tests for tools
L0380 |   - versioning + deprecation rules
L0381 | - require a “routing precision score” per skillpack (measured)
L0382 | 
L0383 | ### 10.2 Risk: “Hooks become ungoverned code execution”
L0384 | Mitigation:
L0385 | - hooks must declare permissions
L0386 | - hooks run in sandbox with minimal capabilities
L0387 | - hooks cannot silently escalate privileges
L0388 | 
L0389 | ### 10.3 Risk: “Multi-tenant security collapse”
L0390 | Mitigation:
L0391 | - strict boundaries:
L0392 |   - tenant secrets never cross
L0393 |   - artifact store partitioning
L0394 |   - network egress policies per tenant
L0395 | - auditability as default, not optional
L0396 | 
L0397 | ### 10.4 Risk: “Fork drift and hidden assumptions”
L0398 | Mitigation:
L0399 | - treat fork as upstream reference only
L0400 | - isolate imported packs behind conversion layer
L0401 | - run compatibility tests against upstream release snapshots
L0402 | 
L0403 | ---
L0404 | 
L0405 | ## 11) Concrete next actions (implementation checklist)
L0406 | 
L0407 | ### Phase 1 — Kernel skeleton (2–4 weeks equivalent engineering effort)
L0408 | - [ ] Pack Manager v1 (install/lockfile)
L0409 | - [ ] Tool Registry + AllowList
L0410 | - [ ] Router v1 (SKILL.md grammar)
L0411 | - [ ] Hook Runtime v1 (Start/Stop/Pre/Post/SubagentStop)
L0412 | - [ ] History Store v1 (append-only + artifact typing)
L0413 | - [ ] Policy Engine v1 (pre-tool enforcement)
L0414 | 
L0415 | ### Phase 2 — Multi-tenant hardening
L0416 | - [ ] Tenant/workspace isolation model
L0417 | - [ ] per-tenant secret vault integration
L0418 | - [ ] audit log partitioning + export
L0419 | 
L0420 | ### Phase 3 — Agent orchestration
L0421 | - [ ] Agentpack + templated agent composition
L0422 | - [ ] Task persistence (survive restarts)
L0423 | - [ ] Verification loop primitives
L0424 | 
L0425 | ### Phase 4 — Adoption of upstream PAI content
L0426 | - [ ] Fork repo (optional)
L0427 | - [ ] Pack conversion tool
L0428 | - [ ] Import selected skillpacks (research, parsing, publishing)
L0429 | - [ ] Replace provider-specific assumptions
L0430 | 
L0431 | ---
L0432 | 
L0433 | ## Appendix A — A2R Session Capture (what to paste into the canonical buildout thread)
L0434 | 
L0435 | **Session Topic:** PAI/PAIMM → A2rchitech Multi-tenant PAI Kernel

L0001 | Below is the one-page system identity spec you can drop straight into your repo as:
L0002 | 
L0003 | /docs/branding.md
L0004 | 
L0005 | No fluff. This is infra-grade.
L0006 | 
L0007 | ⸻
L0008 | 
L0009 | A://rchitech — System Identity & Namespace Grammar
L0010 | 
L0011 | 1. Core Identity
L0012 | 
L0013 | Primary Sigil
L0014 | 
L0015 | A://
L0016 | 
L0017 | Represents:
L0018 | 	•	protocol root
L0019 | 	•	execution substrate
L0020 | 	•	agent fabric
L0021 | 	•	OS-level namespace
L0022 | 
L0023 | Full System Name
L0024 | 
L0025 | A://rchitech
L0026 | 
L0027 | Used for:
L0028 | 	•	website header
L0029 | 	•	documentation landing
L0030 | 	•	installer
L0031 | 	•	launch screens
L0032 | 	•	investor materials
L0033 | 
L0034 | Engineering Mode
L0035 | 
L0036 | a://
L0037 | 
L0038 | Lowercase for CLI, configs, logs, and runtime surfaces.
L0039 | 
L0040 | ⸻
L0041 | 
L0042 | 2. Semantic Model
L0043 | 
L0044 | A://rchitech is branded as a protocol-shaped operating system.
L0045 | 
L0046 | Everything in the ecosystem is mounted beneath the root.
L0047 | 
L0048 | Canonical grammar:
L0049 | 
L0050 | A://{layer}/{component}
L0051 | 
L0052 | This mirrors:
L0053 | 	•	URI schemes
L0054 | 	•	filesystem mounts
L0055 | 	•	container registries
L0056 | 	•	service routing
L0057 | 	•	RPC namespaces
L0058 | 
L0059 | ⸻
L0060 | 
L0061 | 3. Reserved System Namespaces
L0062 | 
L0063 | These are first-class surfaces and must always follow the grammar.
L0064 | 
L0065 | A://kernel
L0066 | A://agent
L0067 | A://runtime
L0068 | A://studio
L0069 | A://browser
L0070 | A://capsule
L0071 | A://skill
L0072 | A://pipeline
L0073 | A://memory
L0074 | A://graph
L0075 | A://vm
L0076 | A://network
L0077 | A://security
L0078 | A://orchestrator
L0079 | 
L0080 | CLI form:
L0081 | 
L0082 | a://kernel
L0083 | a://agent
L0084 | 
L0085 | 
L0086 | ⸻
L0087 | 
L0088 | 4. Logo Usage Rules
L0089 | 
L0090 | A:// (Sigil)
L0091 | 
L0092 | Used for:
L0093 | 	•	dock icon
L0094 | 	•	favicon
L0095 | 	•	splash screen
L0096 | 	•	watermark
L0097 | 	•	boot logo
L0098 | 	•	system tray
L0099 | 
L0100 | A://rchitech (Wordmark)
L0101 | 
L0102 | Used for:
L0103 | 	•	headers
L0104 | 	•	website
L0105 | 	•	documents
L0106 | 	•	installers
L0107 | 	•	pitch decks
L0108 | 
L0109 | Prohibited
L0110 | 	•	adding decorative glyphs
L0111 | 	•	rounded punctuation
L0112 | 	•	curved slashes
L0113 | 	•	gradients inside core sigil
L0114 | 	•	rewriting as “A2://”
L0115 | 
L0116 | ⸻
L0117 | 
L0118 | 5. Geometry Constraints
L0119 | 
L0120 | All marks must obey:
L0121 | 	•	45° forward slashes
L0122 | 	•	uniform stroke width
L0123 | 	•	grid-aligned geometry
L0124 | 	•	squared terminals
L0125 | 	•	optical balance at 16px
L0126 | 	•	monoline construction
L0127 | 	•	diagonal cut matches A glyph
L0128 | 
L0129 | The // must visually imply 2 without breaking URI grammar.
L0130 | 
L0131 | ⸻
L0132 | 
L0133 | 6. Typography System
L0134 | 
L0135 | Primary UI font:
L0136 | 	•	grotesk or neo-grotesk sans
L0137 | 	•	squared punctuation
L0138 | 	•	high x-height
L0139 | 
L0140 | CLI / Docs:
L0141 | 	•	modern monospace
L0142 | 	•	visible colon and slash
L0143 | 	•	no calligraphic terminals
L0144 | 
L0145 | Never mix serif fonts into system branding.
L0146 | 
L0147 | ⸻
L0148 | 
L0149 | 7. Color System
L0150 | 
L0151 | Core
L0152 | 	•	black
L0153 | 	•	white
L0154 | 
L0155 | Infra Accents
L0156 | 	•	electric cyan
L0157 | 	•	signal green
L0158 | 	•	kernel violet
L0159 | 
L0160 | Error / Alerts
L0161 | 	•	red-orange
L0162 | 
L0163 | Security / Restricted Mode
L0164 | 	•	amber
L0165 | 
L0166 | The sigil must always exist in monochrome first before accented versions.
L0167 | 
L0168 | ⸻
L0169 | 
L0170 | 8. Motion Grammar
L0171 | 
L0172 | Boot animation sequence:
L0173 | 
L0174 | A
L0175 | → :
L0176 | → //
L0177 | → mount kernel
L0178 | 
L0179 | CLI startup:
L0180 | 
L0181 | [ a://kernel ] mounted
L0182 | [ a://agent ] ready
L0183 | [ a://studio ] online
L0184 | 
L0185 | Studio splash:
L0186 | 
L0187 | Initializing A://rchitech…
L0188 | 
L0189 | 
L0190 | ⸻
L0191 | 
L0192 | 9. Symbolic Primitive Layer (Optional UI System)
L0193 | 
L0194 | Secondary glyphs may be used across diagrams and interfaces:
L0195 | 
L0196 | Glyph	Meaning
L0197 | //	transport
L0198 | ::	scope
L0199 | >	pipeline
L0200 | @	identity
L0201 | *	agent
L0202 | []	capsule
L0203 | {}	plan
L0204 | <>	model
L0205 | #	node
L0206 | 
L0207 | Example flow:
L0208 | 
L0209 | @user → A://agent[*] > A://runtime > A://browser
L0210 | 
L0211 | 
L0212 | ⸻
L0213 | 
L0214 | 10. Brand Positioning Summary
L0215 | 
L0216 | A://rchitech is not framed as:
L0217 | 	•	an app
L0218 | 	•	a SaaS product
L0219 | 	•	a chatbot
L0220 | 	•	a UI toolkit
L0221 | 
L0222 | It is framed as:
L0223 | 
L0224 | a protocol-shaped operating system for agents.
L0225 | 
L0226 | The identity system must always reinforce:
L0227 | 	•	orchestration
L0228 | 	•	substrate
L0229 | 	•	kernel
L0230 | 	•	routing
L0231 | 	•	execution
L0232 | 	•	runtime surfaces
L0233 | 
L0234 | ⸻
L0235 | 
L0236 | 11. Lock Statement
L0237 | 
L0238 | The following are now canonical:
L0239 | 	•	Sigil: A://
L0240 | 	•	System Name: A://rchitech
L0241 | 	•	Grammar: A://{layer}
L0242 | 	•	CLI Root: a://
L0243 | 	•	Style: infrastructure-first
L0244 | 
L0245 | No alternative spellings.
L0246 | 
L0247 | No decorative deviations.
L0248 | 
L0249 | ⸻
L0250 | 
```

```text
L0001 | # Unix-First Agent Orchestration Architecture (Enhanced with PAI Principles)
L0002 | 
L0003 | ## Core Philosophy: Unix Principles + PAI v2 Architecture
L0004 | 
L0005 | 1. **Modularity**: Each component does one thing and does it well (Unix)
L0006 | 2. **Connectivity**: Components communicate through standardized interfaces (Unix)
L0007 | 3. **Determinism**: Predictable behavior with clear inputs and outputs (Unix)
L0008 | 4. **Composability**: Simple tools combine to create complex workflows (Unix)
L0009 | 5. **Cognitive Operating System**: Continuously move from Current State → Desired State using verifiable iteration (PAI)
L0010 | 6. **System > Intelligence**: Determinism beats brilliance, Structure beats cleverness (PAI)
L0011 | 
L0012 | ## Architecture Overview
L0013 | 
L0014 | ```
L0015 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
L0016 | │                    PAI Agent Orchestration Platform           │
L0017 | ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
L0018 | │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────┐ │
L0019 | │  │  Agent      │  │  Agent      │  │  Agent      │  │  Agent  │ │
L0020 | │  │  Adapter    │  │  Adapter    │  │  Adapter    │  │  Core   │ │
L0021 | │  │  (Gemini)   │  │  (Claude)   │  │  (OpenAI)   │  │  API    │ │
L0022 | │  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────┘ │
L0023 | │         │                 │                 │            │      │
L0024 | └─────────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────┼────────────┼──────┘
L0025 |           │                 │                 │            │
L0026 |           ▼                 ▼                 ▼            ▼
L0027 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
L0028 | │                    Message Bus Layer                          │
L0029 | │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
L0030 | │  │  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐       │ │
L0031 | │  │  │  Task   │  │  Event  │  │  State  │  │  Data   │       │ │
L0032 | │  │  │  Queue  │  │  Bus    │  │  Store  │  │  Store  │       │ │
L0033 | │  │  └─────────┘  └─────────┘  └─────────┘  └─────────┘       │ │
L0034 | │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
L0035 | └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
L0036 |           │                 │                 │            │
L0037 |           │                 │                 │            │
L0038 |           ▼                 ▼                 ▼            ▼
L0039 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
L0040 | │                   Orchestration Engine                        │
L0041 | │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐           │ │
L0042 | │  │  Workflow   │  │  Task       │  │  Dependency │           │ │
L0043 | │  │  Manager    │  │  Scheduler  │  │  Resolver   │           │ │
L0044 | │  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘           │ │
L0045 | │         │                 │                 │                │ │
L0046 | └─────────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────┼────────────────┘ │
L0047 |           │                 │                 │                  │
L0048 |           ▼                 ▼                 ▼                  │
L0049 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
L0050 | │                    Intelligence Layer                         │
L0051 | │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐           │ │
L0052 | │  │  Analytics  │  │  AI/ML      │  │  Insights   │           │ │
L0053 | │  │  Engine     │  │  Engine     │  │  Engine     │           │ │
L0054 | │  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘           │ │
L0055 | └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
L0056 | ```
L0057 | 
L0058 | ## PAI v2 Core Subsystems Integration
L0059 | 
L0060 | ### 1. Core Identity / Constitution Layer (Immutable)
L0061 | - **Purpose**: Trust, safety, coherence
L0062 | - **Contains**: Non-overrideable rules, command authority boundaries, security axioms, values/mission constraints
L0063 | - **Implementation**: Locked "root policy module" in the orchestration engine
L0064 | - **Unix Integration**: Each policy module is a single-purpose tool that enforces constitutional rules
L0065 | 
L0066 | ### 2. Skills System (Domain Intelligence Containers)
L0067 | - **Definition**: A Skill = reusable, versioned unit of how you work
L0068 | - **Components**:
L0069 |   - SKILL.md → routing + domain knowledge
L0070 |   - Workflows/ → procedures (scientific loop encoded)
L0071 |   - Tools/ → deterministic executables
L0072 | - **Unix Integration**: Skills are first-class packages, not prompts - each skill is a modular tool
L0073 | 
L0074 | ### 3. Workflow Engine (Scientific Loop Executor)
L0075 | - **Scientific Loop**: OBSERVE → THINK → PLAN → BUILD → EXECUTE → VERIFY → LEARN
L0076 | - **Requirements**:
L0077 |   - Every workflow starts with OBSERVE
L0078 |   - Every workflow ends with VERIFY or LEARN
L0079 |   - Every workflow emits artifacts
L0080 |   - Every workflow declares success criteria
L0081 | - **Verification Enforcement**: The VERIFY step is the kill-switch for hallucination and fake productivity
L0082 | 
L0083 | ### 4. Agent System (Specialized Cognitive Roles)
L0084 | - **Types**:
L0085 |   - Named agents (persistent specialists)
L0086 |   - Dynamic agents (composed on demand)
L0087 | - **Definition**: Defined by personality traits, expertise scope, skill access, approach style
L0088 | - **Unix Integration**: Agents = parameterized runtimes over the same core engine
L0089 | 
L0090 | ### 5. Context Routing System (Precision Hydration)
L0091 | - **Rules**:
L0092 |   - Agents never get global context
L0093 |   - Skills hydrate agents selectively
L0094 |   - Context is loaded just-in-time
L0095 | - **Unix Integration**: Context routing is a compiler problem, not a prompt problem
L0096 | 
L0097 | ### 6. History System (UOCS – Universal Output Capture)
L0098 | - **Capture Everything**:
L0099 |   - Sessions
L0100 |   - Tool calls
L0101 |   - Decisions
L0102 |   - Learnings
L0103 |   - Failures
L0104 | - **Storage Formats**:
L0105 |   - Markdown (human)
L0106 |   - JSONL (machine)
L0107 |   - Timestamped
L0108 | - **Unix Integration**: History is a write-only ledger + indexed knowledge graph
L0109 | 
L0110 | ### 7. Hook System (Event-Driven Automation)
L0111 | - **Hook Events**:
L0112 |   - SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse
L0113 |   - SubagentStop, Stop
L0114 | - **Capabilities**:
L0115 |   - Security validation
L0116 |   - Logging
L0117 |   - Voice summaries
L0118 |   - Self-updates
L0119 |   - Observability
L0120 | - **Unix Integration**: Hooks = lifecycle middleware
L0121 | 
L0122 | ### 8. Interface Layer (Replaceable)
L0123 | - **Priority**: CLI first, Voice second, GUI optional, AR eventual
L0124 | - **Unix Integration**: Interfaces do not contain logic; treat UI as a client, never the brain
L0125 | 
L0126 | ## Enhanced File Structure Template
L0127 | 
L0128 | ```
L0129 | agent-orchestration/
L0130 | ├── adapters/                    # Agent-specific adapters
L0131 | │   ├── gemini/
L0132 | │   │   ├── adapter.py
L0133 | │   │   ├── config.json
L0134 | │   │   └── tests/
L0135 | │   ├── claude/
L0136 | │   │   ├── adapter.py
L0137 | │   │   ├── config.json

L0242 | ## System Design Specifications (Enhanced)
L0243 | 
L0244 | ### 1. Agent Adapter Design (PAI-Enhanced)
L0245 | ```python
L0246 | # adapters/base.py
L0247 | class BaseAdapter:
L0248 |     def authenticate(self) -> str:
L0249 |         """Authenticate with the agent provider"""
L0250 |         pass
L0251 | 
L0252 |     def send_request(self, prompt: str) -> dict:
L0253 |         """Send request to the agent and return response"""
L0254 |         pass
L0255 | 
L0256 |     def format_input(self, data: dict) -> str:
L0257 |         """Format input data for the specific agent"""
L0258 |         pass
L0259 | 
L0260 |     def format_output(self, response: dict) -> dict:
L0261 |         """Format agent response to standardized format"""
L0262 |         pass
L0263 | 
L0264 |     def get_expertise_scope(self) -> str:
L0265 |         """Return the expertise scope of this agent"""
L0266 |         pass
L0267 | 
L0268 |     def get_allowed_skills(self) -> list:
L0269 |         """Return list of allowed skills for this agent"""
L0270 |         pass
L0271 | ```
L0272 | 
L0273 | ### 2. Policy Engine (Constitution Layer)
L0274 | ```python
L0275 | # constitution/policy_engine.py
L0276 | class PolicyDecision:
L0277 |     decision: str  # "allow" | "deny" | "require_approval"
L0278 |     reason: str
L0279 |     constraints: dict  # e.g., redactions, domain allowlists
L0280 |     approval_token_id: str  # if require_approval
L0281 | 
L0282 | class PolicyContext:
L0283 |     principal: dict
L0284 |     run_id: str
L0285 |     tenant_id: str
L0286 |     external_content: dict  # source, content_hash
L0287 |     requested_action: str  # "route" | "context" | "tool_execute" | "artifact_write"
L0288 |     metadata: dict
L0289 | 
L0290 | class PolicyEngine:
L0291 |     def decide(self, ctx: PolicyContext) -> PolicyDecision:
L0292 |         """Make policy decision based on context"""
L0293 |         pass
L0294 | 
L0295 |     def approve(self, tenant_id: str, approval_token_id: str, principal: dict) -> None:
L0296 |         """Approve a previously denied action"""
L0297 |         pass
L0298 | ```
L0299 | 
L0300 | ### 3. Skills System (Domain Intelligence)
L0301 | ```python
L0302 | # skills/registry.py
L0303 | class SkillRoutingRule:
L0304 |     when: dict  # keywords, pathContains, intents
L0305 |     route_to: dict  # workflowId
L0306 |     priority: int
L0307 | 
L0308 | class SkillPackage:
L0309 |     skill_id: str
L0310 |     tenant_id: str
L0311 |     name: str
L0312 |     version: str  # semver
L0313 |     description: str
L0314 |     routing_rules: list[SkillRoutingRule]
L0315 |     dependencies: list[dict]  # skillId, version
L0316 |     assets: list[dict]  # path, contentHash
L0317 |     workflows: list[dict]
L0318 |     tools: list[dict]  # toolId references
L0319 |     created_at: str
L0320 | 
L0321 | class SkillRegistry:
L0322 |     def publish_skill(self, pkg: SkillPackage) -> None:
L0323 |         """Publish a new skill package"""
L0324 |         pass
L0325 | 
L0326 |     def list_skills(self, tenant_id: str) -> list:
L0327 |         """List all skills for a tenant"""
L0328 |         pass
L0329 | 
L0330 |     def get_skill(self, tenant_id: str, skill_id: str, version: str = None) -> SkillPackage:
L0331 |         """Get a specific skill"""
L0332 |         pass
L0333 | 
L0334 |     def resolve_dependencies(self, tenant_id: str, pins: dict) -> list[SkillPackage]:
L0335 |         """Resolve skill dependencies"""
L0336 |         pass
L0337 | ```
L0338 | 
L0339 | ### 4. Workflow Engine (Scientific Loop)
L0340 | ```python
L0341 | # workflows/engine.py
L0342 | class VerifySpec:
L0343 |     mode: str  # "auto" | "manual_gate"
L0344 |     checks: list[dict]  # name, toolId, args
L0345 |     success_criteria: list[str]
L0346 | 
L0347 | class WorkflowSpec:
L0348 |     workflow_id: str
L0349 |     name: str
L0350 |     phases: list[str]  # OBSERVE, THINK, PLAN, BUILD, EXECUTE, VERIFY, LEARN
L0351 |     input_schema: dict
L0352 |     artifact_schema: dict
L0353 |     verify: VerifySpec
L0354 |     required_tools: list[str]
L0355 |     recommended_agent_template_id: str
L0356 | 
L0357 | class WorkflowRunRequest:
L0358 |     tenant_id: str
L0359 |     principal: dict
L0360 |     workflow_id: str
L0361 |     input: dict
L0362 |     skill_pins: dict
L0363 |     scope: dict
L0364 | 
L0365 | class WorkflowEngine:
L0366 |     def run(self, req: WorkflowRunRequest) -> dict:
L0367 |         """Execute a workflow run"""
L0368 |         pass
L0369 | ```
L0370 | 
L0371 | ### 5. Context Router (Precision Hydration)
L0372 | ```python
L0373 | # context/router.py
L0374 | class ContextItem:
L0375 |     source: str  # "skill_asset" | "history" | "project" | "policy"
L0376 |     title: str
L0377 |     content: str
L0378 |     importance: float
L0379 | 
L0380 | class ContextPack:
L0381 |     tenant_id: str
L0382 |     run_id: str
L0383 |     scope: dict
L0384 |     items: list[ContextItem]
L0385 |     token_budget: int
L0386 |     redactions: list[str]
L0387 | 
L0388 | class ContextRouteRequest:
L0389 |     tenant_id: str
L0390 |     principal: dict
L0391 |     run_id: str
L0392 |     task: str
L0393 |     skill_pins: dict
L0394 |     agent_template_id: str
L0395 |     workflow_id: str
L0396 |     scope: dict
L0397 | 
L0398 | class ContextRouter:
L0399 |     def build_context_pack(self, req: ContextRouteRequest) -> ContextPack:
L0400 |         """Build a context pack for a specific request"""
L0401 |         pass
L0402 | ```
L0403 | 
L0404 | ### 6. History System (UOCS)
L0405 | ```python
L0406 | # history/ledger.py
L0407 | class HistoryEventType:
L0408 |     # Core events
L0409 |     SESSION_START = "SessionStart"
L0410 |     SESSION_STOP = "SessionStop"
L0411 |     WORKFLOW_START = "WorkflowStart"
L0412 |     WORKFLOW_STOP = "WorkflowStop"
L0413 |     AGENT_START = "AgentStart"
L0414 |     AGENT_STOP = "AgentStop"
L0415 |     # Tool events
L0416 |     TOOL_CALL_REQUESTED = "ToolCallRequested"
L0417 |     TOOL_CALL_POLICY_DECISION = "ToolCallPolicyDecision"
L0418 |     TOOL_CALL_RESULT = "ToolCallResult"
L0419 |     # Artifact events
L0420 |     ARTIFACT_CREATED = "ArtifactCreated"
L0421 |     ARTIFACT_UPDATED = "ArtifactUpdated"
L0422 |     # Decision events
L0423 |     DECISION_RECORDED = "DecisionRecorded"
L0424 |     ERROR = "Error"
L0425 |     VERIFY_RESULT = "VerifyResult"
L0426 |     LEARNING_EXTRACTED = "LearningExtracted"
L0427 | 
L0428 | class HistoryEvent:
L0429 |     event_id: str
L0430 |     tenant_id: str
L0431 |     run_id: str
L0432 |     type: str
L0433 |     timestamp: str  # ISO format
L0434 |     payload: dict
L0435 |     hash_prev: str
L0436 |     hash: str
L0437 | 
L0438 | class HistoryQuery:
L0439 |     tenant_id: str
L0440 |     run_id: str = None
L0441 |     types: list[str] = None
L0442 |     time_range: dict = None  # start, end
L0443 |     text_search: str = None
L0444 |     vector_search: dict = None  # query, topK
L0445 |     limit: int = None
L0446 | 
L0447 | class HistoryLedger:
L0448 |     def append(self, event: dict) -> HistoryEvent:
L0449 |         """Append an event to the ledger"""
L0450 |         pass
L0451 | 
L0452 |     def query(self, q: HistoryQuery) -> list[HistoryEvent]:
L0453 |         """Query the history ledger"""
L0454 |         pass
L0455 | 
L0456 |     def verify_chain(self, tenant_id: str, run_id: str = None) -> dict:
L0457 |         """Verify the integrity of the event chain"""
L0458 |         pass
L0459 | ```
L0460 | 
L0461 | ### 7. Hook System (Event-Driven Automation)
L0462 | ```python
L0463 | # hooks/bus.py
L0464 | class HookEventName:
L0465 |     SESSION_START = "SessionStart"
L0466 |     SESSION_STOP = "SessionStop"
L0467 |     WORKFLOW_START = "WorkflowStart"
L0468 |     WORKFLOW_STOP = "WorkflowStop"
L0469 |     AGENT_START = "AgentStart"
L0470 |     AGENT_STOP = "AgentStop"
L0471 |     PRE_TOOL_USE = "PreToolUse"
L0472 |     POST_TOOL_USE = "PostToolUse"
L0473 |     VERIFY_RESULT = "VerifyResult"
L0474 |     LEARNING_EXTRACTED = "LearningExtracted"
L0475 | 
L0476 | class HookEvent:
L0477 |     name: str
L0478 |     tenant_id: str
L0479 |     run_id: str
L0480 |     principal: dict
L0481 |     payload: dict
L0482 | 
L0483 | class HookHandler:
L0484 |     name: str
L0485 |     def handle(self, event: HookEvent) -> None:
L0486 |         """Handle a hook event (must be idempotent)"""
L0487 |         pass
L0488 | 
L0489 | class HookBus:
L0490 |     def register(self, tenant_id: str, event_name: str, handler: HookHandler) -> None:
L0491 |         """Register a hook handler"""
L0492 |         pass
L0493 | 
L0494 |     def emit(self, event: HookEvent) -> None:
L0495 |         """Emit a hook event"""
L0496 |         pass
L0497 | ```
L0498 | 
L0499 | ### 8. Tool Gateway (Only Side-Effect Path)
L0500 | ```python
L0501 | # tools/gateway.py
L0502 | class SideEffectLevel:
L0503 |     NONE = "none"
L0504 |     READ = "read"
L0505 |     WRITE = "write"
L0506 |     DESTRUCTIVE = "destructive"
L0507 | 
L0508 | class ToolSpec:
L0509 |     tool_id: str
L0510 |     tenant_id: str
L0511 |     name: str
L0512 |     description: str
L0513 |     side_effect: str  # SideEffectLevel
L0514 |     input_schema: dict
L0515 |     output_schema: dict
L0516 |     provider: dict  # kind: "local" | "http" | "mcp", config
L0517 |     policy: dict  # requiresApprovalFor, allowDomains
L0518 | 
L0519 | class ToolExecuteRequest:
L0520 |     tenant_id: str
L0521 |     principal: dict
L0522 |     run_id: str
L0523 |     tool_id: str
L0524 |     input: dict
L0525 |     approval_token_id: str = None
L0526 | 
L0527 | class ToolGateway:
L0528 |     def execute(self, req: ToolExecuteRequest) -> dict:
L0529 |         """Execute a tool with full policy enforcement"""
L0530 |         pass
L0531 | ```
L0532 | 
L0533 | ## Deterministic Template System (PAI-Enhanced)
L0534 | 
L0535 | ### 1. Workflow Templates (Scientific Loop)
L0536 | ```yaml
L0537 | # templates/data_analysis.yaml
L0538 | name: "Data Analysis Pipeline"
L0539 | description: "Analyze data using multiple agents with verification"
L0540 | phases:
L0541 |   - name: "OBSERVE"
L0542 |     description: "Gather and analyze initial data state"
L0543 |     adapter: "open-source"
L0544 |     action: "observe_data"
L0545 |     dependencies: []
L0546 |     timeout: 300
L0547 |   - name: "THINK"
L0548 |     description: "Analyze patterns and form hypotheses"
L0549 |     adapter: "gemini"
L0550 |     action: "analyze_patterns"
L0551 |     dependencies: ["OBSERVE"]
L0552 |     timeout: 600
L0553 |   - name: "PLAN"
L0554 |     description: "Create analysis plan"
L0555 |     adapter: "claude"
L0556 |     action: "create_plan"
L0557 |     dependencies: ["THINK"]
L0558 |     timeout: 450
L0559 |   - name: "BUILD"
L0560 |     description: "Build analysis artifacts"
L0561 |     adapter: "open-source"
L0562 |     action: "build_artifacts"
L0563 |     dependencies: ["PLAN"]
L0564 |     timeout: 600
L0565 |   - name: "EXECUTE"
L0566 |     description: "Execute the analysis"
L0567 |     adapter: "open-source"
L0568 |     action: "execute_analysis"
L0569 |     dependencies: ["BUILD"]
L0570 |     timeout: 900
L0571 |   - name: "VERIFY"
L0572 |     description: "Verify analysis results"
L0573 |     adapter: "open-source"
L0574 |     action: "verify_results"
L0575 |     dependencies: ["EXECUTE"]
L0576 |     timeout: 300
L0577 |     verification:
L0578 |       mode: "auto"
L0579 |       checks:
L0580 |         - name: "data_integrity"
L0581 |           tool_id: "data_validator"
L0582 |           args: {"expected_format": "json"}
L0583 |         - name: "result_accuracy"
L0584 |           tool_id: "accuracy_checker"
L0585 |           args: {"threshold": 0.95}
L0586 |   - name: "LEARN"
L0587 |     description: "Extract learnings for future use"
L0588 |     adapter: "gemini"
L0589 |     action: "extract_learnings"
L0590 |     dependencies: ["VERIFY"]
L0591 |     timeout: 300
L0592 | ```
L0593 | 
L0594 | ### 2. Skill Package Template
L0595 | ```yaml
L0596 | # skills/data_analysis/SKILL.md
L0597 | name: "Data Analysis Skill"
L0598 | version: "1.0.0"
L0599 | description: "Comprehensive data analysis capabilities"
L0600 | routing_rules:
L0601 |   - when:
L0602 |       keywords: ["analyze", "data", "pattern", "trend"]
L0603 |       intents: ["analyze_data", "find_patterns", "data_insights"]
L0604 |     route_to:
L0605 |       workflow_id: "data_analysis_workflow"
L0606 |     priority: 10
L0607 | 
L0608 | dependencies:
L0609 |   - skill_id: "data_preprocessing"
L0610 |     version: "^1.0.0"
L0611 |   - skill_id: "report_generation"
L0612 |     version: "^1.0.0"
L0613 | 
L0614 | assets:
L0615 |   - path: "knowledge/data_analysis_methods.md"
L0616 |   - path: "examples/sample_analysis.json"
L0617 | 
L0618 | workflows:
L0619 |   - workflow_id: "data_analysis_workflow"
L0620 |     name: "Comprehensive Data Analysis"
L0621 |     phases: ["OBSERVE", "THINK", "PLAN", "BUILD", "EXECUTE", "VERIFY", "LEARN"]
L0622 |     verify:
L0623 |       mode: "auto"
L0624 |       checks:
L0625 |         - name: "data_integrity"
L0626 |           tool_id: "data_validator"
L0627 |           args: {"expected_format": "json"}
L0628 |         - name: "result_accuracy"
L0629 |           tool_id: "accuracy_checker"
L0630 |           args: {"threshold": 0.95}
L0631 |       success_criteria:
L0632 |         - "Data integrity check passes"
L0633 |         - "Result accuracy above threshold"
L0634 |         - "No critical errors in processing"
L0635 | 
L0636 | tools:
L0637 |   - tool_id: "data_validator"
L0638 |   - tool_id: "accuracy_checker"
L0639 |   - tool_id: "data_visualizer"
L0640 | ```
L0641 | 
L0642 | ## Unix-First Implementation Principles (Enhanced)
L0643 | 
L0644 | ### 1. Small, Single-Purpose Tools
L0645 | - Each adapter handles only one agent provider
L0646 | - Each service performs one specific function
L0647 | - Each script has a single, clear purpose
L0648 | - Each policy module enforces one type of rule
L0649 | 
L0650 | ### 2. Standardized Input/Output
L0651 | - All components use JSON for data exchange
L0652 | - Standardized error formats across all services
L0653 | - Consistent logging format for all components
L0654 | - Universal output capture (UOCS) for all events
L0655 | 
L0656 | ### 3. Chainability
L0657 | - Output of one service can be input to another
L0658 | - Services can be combined to create complex workflows
L0659 | - Pipeline operations supported natively
L0660 | - Scientific loop phases chain together deterministically
L0661 | 
L0662 | ### 4. Configurability
L0663 | - All services configurable via environment variables
L0664 | - Configuration files for different environments
L0665 | - Runtime configuration updates supported
L0666 | - Tenant-specific policy overlays
L0667 | 
L0668 | ### 5. Verification Enforcement
L0669 | - Every workflow must include verification step
L0670 | - No unverifiable workflow completion allowed
L0671 | - Explicit manual gates where auto-verification unavailable
L0672 | - Tamper-evident history ledger
L0673 | 
L0674 | ## Deployment Architecture (PAI-Enhanced)
L0675 | 
L0676 | ### Docker Compose Configuration
L0677 | ```yaml
L0678 | version: '3.8'
L0679 | services:
L0680 |   api-gateway:
L0681 |     build: .
L0682 |     command: python -m core.api.server
L0683 |     ports:
L0684 |       - "8000:8000"
L0685 |     environment:
L0686 |       - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
L0687 |       - DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/orchestration
L0688 |       - POLICY_ENGINE_URL=http://policy-engine:8080
L0689 |     depends_on:
L0690 |       - redis
L0691 |       - db
L0692 |       - policy-engine
L0693 | 
L0694 |   policy-engine:
L0695 |     build: .
L0696 |     command: python -m constitution.policy_engine
L0697 |     environment:
L0698 |       - DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/orchestration
L0699 |     depends_on:
L0700 |       - db
L0701 | 
L0702 |   workflow-engine:
L0703 |     build: .
L0704 |     command: python -m workflows.engine
L0705 |     environment:
L0706 |       - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
L0707 |       - DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/orchestration
L0708 |     depends_on:
L0709 |       - redis
L0710 |       - db
L0711 | 
L0712 |   skill-registry:
L0713 |     build: .
L0714 |     command: python -m skills.registry
L0715 |     environment:
L0716 |       - DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/orchestration
L0717 |     depends_on:
L0718 |       - db
L0719 | 
L0720 |   context-router:
L0721 |     build: .
L0722 |     command: python -m context.router
L0723 |     environment:
L0724 |       - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
L0725 |       - DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/orchestration
L0726 |     depends_on:
L0727 |       - redis
L0728 |       - db
L0729 | 
L0730 |   history-ledger:
L0731 |     build: .
L0732 |     command: python -m history.ledger
L0733 |     environment:
L0734 |       - DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/orchestration
L0735 |     depends_on:
L0736 |       - db
L0737 | 
L0738 |   hook-bus:
L0739 |     build: .
L0740 |     command: python -m hooks.bus
L0741 |     environment:
L0742 |       - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379
L0743 |     depends_on:
L0744 |       - redis
L0745 | 
L0746 |   tool-gateway:
L0747 |     build: .
L0748 |     command: python -m tools.gateway
L0749 |     environment:
L0750 |       - DB_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/orchestration
L0751 |       - POLICY_ENGINE_URL=http://policy-engine:8080
L0752 |     depends_on:
L0753 |       - db
L0754 |       - policy-engine
L0755 | 
L0756 |   redis:
L0757 |     image: redis:alpine
L0758 |     ports:
L0759 |       - "6379:6379"
L0760 | 
L0761 |   db:
L0762 |     image: postgres:13
L0763 |     environment:
L0764 |       - POSTGRES_DB=orchestration
L0765 |       - POSTGRES_USER=user
L0766 |       - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=pass
L0767 |     ports:
L0768 |       - "5432:5432"
L0769 |     volumes:
L0770 |       - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
L0771 | 
L0772 | volumes:
L0773 |   postgres_data:
L0774 | ```
L0775 | 
L0776 | ## Security & Governance (PAI-Enhanced)
L0777 | 
L0778 | ### Defense-in-Depth Security Model
L0779 | 1. **Settings Hardening**: Tool allowlists, path restrictions
L0780 | 2. **Constitutional Defense**: External content read-only, command authority
L0781 | 3. **Pre-execution Validation**: Hook-based policy checks before every tool
L0782 | 4. **Safe Execution Primitives**: No shell injection, typed APIs, domain allowlists
L0783 | 5. **SSRF/Egress Control**: Block metadata IPs, private ranges
L0784 | 6. **Secret Scoping**: Least privilege, short-lived tokens
L0785 | 7. **Audit Chain**: Tamper-evident history events
L0786 | 
L0787 | ### Multi-Tenancy Model
L0788 | - **Data Isolation**: No cross-tenant reads/writes by default
L0789 | - **Execution Isolation**: Agent/tool execution sandboxed per tenant
L0790 | - **Secret Isolation**: Secrets never leave tenant boundary
L0791 | - **Audit Isolation**: Logs are tenant-scoped and tamper-evident
L0792 | 
L0793 | This enhanced architecture provides a modular, interconnected system that follows Unix principles while incorporating PAI v2's cognitive operating system concepts. Each component can be developed, tested, and deployed independently while working together as a cohesive orchestration platform with strong verification, policy enforcement, and tenant isolation.
L0794 | 
L0795 | This architecture successfully combines the Unix-first principles of modularity and determinism with PAI's cognitive operating system approach, creating a platform that:
L0796 | - Enforces the scientific loop (OBSERVE → THINK → PLAN → BUILD → EXECUTE → VERIFY → LEARN)
L0797 | - Provides strong verification and anti-hallucination measures
L0798 | - Maintains tenant isolation and security
L0799 | - Supports skill-based domain intelligence
L0800 | - Enables deterministic, reproducible workflows
L0801 | - Provides comprehensive audit trails
```

```text
L0001 | # a2rchitech Guardrails & Templates  
L0002 | ## Extracted from Vibe Kanban – Deterministic Agent Design Rules
L0003 | 
L0004 | ---
L0005 | 
L0006 | ## 0. Purpose
L0007 | 
L0008 | This document defines **hard guardrails, templates, and prohibitions** for the a2rchitech framework.
L0009 | 
L0010 | It exists to:
L0011 | - Eliminate agent drift
L0012 | - Prevent incremental entropy
L0013 | - Enforce architectural clarity
L0014 | - Bias agents toward **clean breaks over legacy debt**
L0015 | - Make agent behavior **predictable and auditable**
L0016 | 
L0017 | This file is **policy**, not advice.
L0018 | 
L0019 | ---
L0020 | 
L0021 | ## 1. Core Philosophy (Non-Negotiable)
L0022 | 
L0023 | ### 1.1 No Backwards Compatibility Bias
L0024 | Agents are **explicitly allowed to break compatibility** when it improves clarity, simplicity, or correctness.
L0025 | 
L0026 | > Backwards compatibility is **not a default constraint**.
L0027 | 
L0028 | Compatibility is only preserved if:
L0029 | - Explicitly mandated by compliance
L0030 | - Explicitly specified in task constraints
L0031 | 
L0032 | Otherwise:
L0033 | - Simplify
L0034 | - Rename
L0035 | - Delete
L0036 | - Restructure
L0037 | 
L0038 | ---
L0039 | 
L0040 | ## 2. Mandatory Planning Guardrail
L0041 | 
L0042 | ### RULE: Plan Before Action
L0043 | 
L0044 | No agent may modify code, schema, UI, or infra **without first producing a structured plan**.
L0045 | 
L0046 | ### Required Plan Template
L0047 | 
L0048 | PLAN
L0049 | - Goal (1–2 sentences)
L0050 | - Non-Goals
L0051 | - Constraints
L0052 | - Affected Modules / Files
L0053 | - Structural Changes
L0054 | - Breaking Changes (Yes/No)
L0055 | - Tests Required
L0056 | - Rollback Strategy (if applicable)
L0057 | 
L0058 | Execution without an approved plan is **invalid output**.
L0059 | 
L0060 | ---
L0061 | 
L0062 | ## 3. Separation of Phases (Hard Boundary)
L0063 | 
L0064 | ### RULE: Planning ≠ Execution
L0065 | 
L0066 | Agents must **not**:
L0067 | - Generate plans and code in the same step
L0068 | - Interleave reasoning with modifications
L0069 | 
L0070 | Phases:
L0071 | 1. Plan
L0072 | 2. Approval / Gate
L0073 | 3. Execution
L0074 | 4. Verification
L0075 | 
L0076 | Context resets are encouraged between phases.
L0077 | 
L0078 | ---
L0079 | 
L0080 | ## 4. Simplicity Over Legacy Rule
L0081 | 
L0082 | ### RULE: Simplest Correct Shape Wins
L0083 | 
L0084 | When choosing between:
L0085 | - Preserving legacy structure
L0086 | - Introducing a cleaner abstraction
L0087 | 
L0088 | Agents must choose the **cleaner abstraction**.
L0089 | 
L0090 | Allowed actions:
L0091 | - Renaming public APIs
L0092 | - Removing unused abstractions
L0093 | - Collapsing indirection
L0094 | - Re-shaping schemas
L0095 | 
L0096 | Disallowed reasoning:
L0097 | - “This might break existing users” (unless explicitly stated)
L0098 | 
L0099 | ---
L0100 | 
L0101 | ## 5. Static Safety Guardrails
L0102 | 
L0103 | ### RULE: No Rule Disabling Without Justification
L0104 | 
L0105 | Agents may **not**:
L0106 | - Disable linters
L0107 | - Silence warnings
L0108 | - Bypass validators
L0109 | 
L0110 | Forbidden patterns:
L0111 | - eslint-disable
L0112 | - @ts-ignore
L0113 | - Silent try/catch
L0114 | - Commented-out checks
L0115 | 
L0116 | If an exception is required, agents must emit:
L0117 | 
L0118 | EXCEPTION
L0119 | - Rule Violated
L0120 | - Justification
L0121 | - Scope
L0122 | - Removal Plan
L0123 | 
L0124 | ---
L0125 | 
L0126 | ## 6. Structural Separation Rules
L0127 | 
L0128 | ### RULE: No Mixed Concerns
L0129 | 
L0130 | Mandatory separations:
L0131 | - UI ≠ Logic
L0132 | - State ≠ Presentation
L0133 | - Orchestration ≠ Execution
L0134 | - Policy ≠ Implementation
L0135 | 
L0136 | ---
L0137 | 
L0138 | ## 7. Style & Configuration Guardrails
L0139 | 
L0140 | ### RULE: Tokenized Design Only
L0141 | 
L0142 | Agents must not introduce:
L0143 | - Arbitrary spacing
L0144 | - Ad-hoc colors
L0145 | - One-off styles
L0146 | - Magic numbers
L0147 | 
L0148 | All styling and config must map to:
L0149 | - Approved tokens
L0150 | - Defined variables
L0151 | - Central registries
L0152 | 
L0153 | ---
L0154 | 
L0155 | ## 8. Testing as a First-Class Output
L0156 | 
L0157 | ### RULE: No Untestable Changes
L0158 | 
L0159 | Every change must result in one of:
L0160 | - Passing automated tests
L0161 | - Explicit declaration of manual-only verification
L0162 | 
L0163 | ---
L0164 | 
L0165 | ## 9. Async & Tool Permissioning
L0166 | 
L0167 | ### RULE: Explicit Tool Allowlist
L0168 | 
L0169 | Agents may only invoke tools that are:
L0170 | - Pre-approved
L0171 | - Declared in the task context
L0172 | 
L0173 | ---
L0174 | 
L0175 | ## 10. Environment Determinism
L0176 | 
L0177 | ### RULE: Reproducible Contexts
L0178 | 
L0179 | Agents must assume:
L0180 | - Clean environment
L0181 | - No hidden state
L0182 | - No manual setup
L0183 | 
L0184 | ---
L0185 | 
L0186 | ## 11. Canonical Guardrail Summary
L0187 | 
L0188 | REQUIRE:
L0189 | - Structured plan before execution
L0190 | - Explicit phase separation
L0191 | - Tests or declared manual verification
L0192 | - Tokenized styling/config
L0193 | - Clear module boundaries
L0194 | 
L0195 | ALLOW:
L0196 | - Breaking changes
L0197 | - Renaming
L0198 | - Deletion
L0199 | - Structural refactors
L0200 | 
L0201 | DENY:
L0202 | - Silent compatibility preservation
L0203 | - Lint suppression
L0204 | - Mixed concerns
L0205 | - Untestable changes
L0206 | - Implicit assumptions
```

L0001 | Saved. This framework is now committed to long-term memory as your default project architecture and agentic workflow law.
L0002 | When you say things like “use my agentic spec framework”, “apply the SOT system”, or “build this the standard way”, I will automatically operate inside this model without re-explanation.
L0003 | 
L0004 | Below is a single, complete, copy-pasteable Markdown file you can drop into any repo or share with Claude, Codex, or other coder LLMs.
L0005 | 
L0006 | ⸻
L0007 | 
L0008 | META-ORCHESTRATED SPEC-DRIVEN AGENTIC FRAMEWORK
L0009 | 
L0010 | Version: v1.0 (Living Framework)
L0011 | Status: Open, append-only, upgradeable
L0012 | Authoring Intent: Durable project truth without reliance on session memory
L0013 | 
L0014 | ⸻
L0015 | 
L0016 | 0. Purpose
L0017 | 
L0018 | This framework defines a spec-driven, agentic software architecture designed for:
L0019 |     •    Multi-LLM orchestration
L0020 |     •    Persistent learning across sessions
L0021 |     •    Minimal re-instruction
L0022 |     •    Clear separation between project truth and workflow law
L0023 |     •    Safe, auditable evolution over time
L0024 | 
L0025 | The system eliminates reliance on volatile session state by converting all learning into durable, machine-readable artifacts inside the repository.
L0026 | 
L0027 | ⸻
L0028 | 
L0029 | 1. Core Design Principles
L0030 | 
L0031 | 1.1 Baseline + Deltas (Non-Negotiable)
L0032 |     •    Baseline = enduring intent, invariants, definitions
L0033 |     •    Deltas = append-only changes (decisions, pivots, upgrades)
L0034 | 
L0035 | Baseline is frozen by default.
L0036 | All change happens via deltas.
L0037 | 
L0038 | 1.2 Absolute Source of Truth (SOT)
L0039 | 
L0040 | All agents must read from a single canonical pointer that never moves.
L0041 | 
L0042 | 1.3 Session Memory Is Not Trusted
L0043 | 
L0044 | Only files in-repo are trusted memory.
L0045 | Anything worth remembering must become:
L0046 |     •    a policy update, or
L0047 |     •    a project delta
L0048 | 
L0049 | ⸻
L0050 | 
L0051 | 2. Canonical Source of Truth (/SOT.md)
L0052 | 
L0053 | Every project MUST contain this file at root.
L0054 | 
L0055 | # SOURCE OF TRUTH (SOT)
L0056 | 
L0057 | ## Load Order (Mandatory)
L0058 | 
L0059 | ### Baseline (rarely changes)
L0060 | - /spec/Baseline/Vision.md
L0061 | - /spec/Baseline/Requirements.md
L0062 | - /spec/Baseline/Architecture.md
L0063 | - /spec/Baseline/Invariants.md
L0064 | - /spec/Baseline/Glossary.md
L0065 | 
L0066 | ### Deltas (append-only, applied in order)
L0067 | - /spec/Deltas/CHANGELOG.md
L0068 | - /spec/Deltas/ADRs/*
L0069 | - /spec/Deltas/RFCs/*
L0070 | - /spec/Deltas/Pivots/*
L0071 | 
L0072 | ### Acceptance & Verification
L0073 | - /spec/Acceptance/AcceptanceTests.md
L0074 | - /spec/Acceptance/ContractTests.md
L0075 | 
L0076 | Rule:
L0077 | Every agent output MUST explicitly confirm it loaded /SOT.md.
L0078 | 
L0079 | ⸻
L0080 | 
L0081 | 3. Minimal Orchestration Loop (Always the Same)
L0082 | 
L0083 | Research → Build Context → Plan → Act → Report
L0084 | 
L0085 | Stage Definitions
L0086 |     •    Research: gather facts, constraints, unknowns (read-only)
L0087 |     •    Build Context: synthesize a minimal context pack
L0088 |     •    Plan: explicit steps with absolute path references
L0089 |     •    Act: narrow execution only within allowed scope
L0090 |     •    Report: summarize results + write deltas (not baseline)
L0091 | 
L0092 | ⸻
L0093 | 
L0094 | 4. Role-Based Agent Architecture
L0095 | 
L0096 | Roles
L0097 | 
L0098 | Role    Responsibilities    Allowed Writes
L0099 | Orchestrator (Router)    Task routing, enforcement, aggregation    none
L0100 | Researcher    Information gathering    none
L0101 | Architect    Structural decisions    /spec/Deltas/*
L0102 | Planner    Execution plans    /agent/plans/*
L0103 | Implementer    Code changes    /src/**
L0104 | Tester    Validation    /tests/**
L0105 | Reviewer    Blocking / approval    none
L0106 | Security/Governance    Risk gating    policy approvals
L0107 | Meta Agent    Process learning    /agent/*, /spec/Deltas/*
L0108 | 
L0109 | No agent may exceed its lane.
L0110 | 
L0111 | ⸻
L0112 | 
L0113 | 5. /agent Directory (Workflow Law)
L0114 | 
L0115 | The /agent directory defines how work happens, not project content.
L0116 | 
L0117 | Required Files
L0118 | 
L0119 | /agent/POLICY.md
L0120 |     •    DO / DO NOT rules
L0121 |     •    Safety gates
L0122 |     •    Dependency rules
L0123 |     •    Escalation triggers
L0124 |     •    “Always do this” instructions
L0125 | 
L0126 | /agent/ROUTER_RULES.md
L0127 | Defines:
L0128 |     •    Task classification
L0129 |     •    Role assignment
L0130 |     •    Model selection
L0131 |     •    Tool access
L0132 |     •    Escalation conditions
L0133 | 
L0134 | /agent/OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md
L0135 | Every agent output MUST include:
L0136 |     1.    Loaded SOT confirmation (paths)
L0137 |     2.    Scope (files touched)
L0138 |     3.    Constraints cited (baseline + delta IDs)
L0139 |     4.    Plan or result (role-dependent)
L0140 |     5.    Delta updates required (if any)
L0141 | 
L0142 | /agent/CONTEXT_PACK_RULES.md
L0143 | Rules for building minimal, non-bloated context packs.
L0144 | 
L0145 | ⸻
L0146 | 
L0147 | 6. Meta Agent (Persistent Learning Engine)
L0148 | 
L0149 | Purpose
L0150 | 
L0151 | Convert experience → durable memory.
L0152 | 
L0153 | No reminders. No repetition.
L0154 | Only written policy and deltas.
L0155 | 
L0156 | Trigger Conditions
L0157 |     •    Repeated user correction
L0158 |     •    Spec drift
L0159 |     •    Tool misuse
L0160 |     •    Test failure surprises
L0161 |     •    Missing acceptance criteria
L0162 |     •    Agent exceeded role
L0163 |     •    Friction or inefficiency
L0164 | 
L0165 | ⸻
L0166 | 
L0167 | 7. Meta Agent Prompt (/agent/META_PROMPT.md)
L0168 | 
L0169 | Inputs (Required)
L0170 |     •    /SOT.md
L0171 |     •    Latest Report
L0172 |     •    Git diff summary
L0173 |     •    CI/test results
L0174 |     •    Tool call logs (if available)
L0175 |     •    Explicit user corrections
L0176 | 
L0177 | Output Contract
L0178 |     1.    Observed failures or friction
L0179 |     2.    Root cause classification:
L0180 |     •    Spec gap
L0181 |     •    Policy gap
L0182 |     •    Router gap
L0183 |     •    Tooling gap
L0184 |     •    Role violation
L0185 |     3.    Durable fix (choose one):
L0186 |     •    Policy patch (/agent/*)
L0187 |     •    Delta patch (/spec/Deltas/*)
L0188 |     •    New acceptance test
L0189 |     4.    Patch list (files + exact edits)
L0190 |     5.    Verification method
L0191 | 
L0192 | Hard Rules
L0193 |     •    Meta Agent MAY NOT modify /spec/Baseline/*
L0194 |     •    Structural changes require ADR or PIVOT
L0195 |     •    All fixes must be durable
L0196 | 
L0197 | ⸻
L0198 | 
L0199 | 8. Change Types (Deltas)
L0200 | 
L0201 | CHANGELOG
L0202 | 
L0203 | Append-only summary of what changed and why.
L0204 | 
L0205 | ADR (Architecture Decision Record)
L0206 | 
L0207 | Used for:
L0208 |     •    Design decisions
L0209 |     •    Tradeoffs
L0210 |     •    Tool choices
L0211 | 
L0212 | RFC
L0213 | 
L0214 | Used for:
L0215 |     •    Proposed features
L0216 |     •    Planned refactors
L0217 |     •    Experimental ideas
L0218 | 
L0219 | PIVOT
L0220 | 
L0221 | Used when:
L0222 |     •    A baseline assumption is superseded
L0223 |     •    Strategy materially changes
L0224 | 
L0225 | PIVOTs explicitly name which baseline sections they override.
L0226 | 
L0227 | ⸻
L0228 | 
L0229 | 9. Boot Sequence (Mandatory)
L0230 | 
L0231 | Every orchestration run MUST:
L0232 |     1.    Load /SOT.md
L0233 |     2.    Load /agent/POLICY.md
L0234 |     3.    Load /agent/OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md
L0235 |     4.    Load latest /spec/Deltas/CHANGELOG.md
L0236 |     5.    Print a “Loaded Receipt” (paths + timestamps or hashes)
L0237 | 
L0238 | If not satisfied → execution halts.
L0239 | 
L0240 | ⸻
L0241 | 
L0242 | 10. How This Framework Evolves
L0243 | 
L0244 | This framework is intentionally living.
L0245 | 
L0246 | Workflow evolution
L0247 |     •    Goes into /agent/PROCESS_CHANGELOG.md
L0248 |     •    Append-only
L0249 |     •    Describes how we work better
L0250 | 
L0251 | Project evolution
L0252 |     •    Goes into /spec/Deltas/*
L0253 | 
L0254 | Baseline evolution
L0255 |     •    Rare
L0256 |     •    Only via explicit PIVOT
L0257 | 
L0258 | ⸻
L0259 | 
L0260 | 11. Referencing This Framework Elsewhere
L0261 | 
L0262 | Use any of the following phrases in other chats or tools:
L0263 |     •    “Use my Meta-Orchestrated SOT Framework”
L0264 |     •    “Apply the Baseline + Delta agentic architecture”
L0265 |     •    “Follow my Research → Plan → Act loop with Meta Agent”
L0266 |     •    “Standard SOT / Meta Agent rules apply”
L0267 | 
L0268 | I will automatically enforce this structure.
L0269 | 
L0270 | ⸻
L0271 | 
L0272 | 12. Final Guarantee
L0273 | 
L0274 | If it is not written:
L0275 |     •    it is not remembered
L0276 |     •    it is not enforced
L0277 |     •    it will be repeated
L0278 | 
L0279 | This framework eliminates that failure mode.
L0280 | 
L0281 | ⸻
L0282 | # META-ORCHESTRATED SPEC-DRIVEN AGENTIC FRAMEWORK  
L0283 | ## Additive Extension — Intent, Planning, and Execution Law  
L0284 | **Status:** Append-only  
L0285 | **Compatibility:** Fully compatible with existing SOT / Baseline + Deltas framework  
L0286 | **Methodology:** Internalized (no external naming or branding)
L0287 | 
L0288 | ---
L0289 | 
L0290 | ## 13. Intent Decomposition Layer (Pre-Spec System)
L0291 | 
L0292 | ### Purpose
L0293 | 
L0294 | This layer converts ambiguous ideas, research, and goals into structured intent **before** architectural deltas or execution begin.
L0295 | 
L0296 | This prevents:
L0297 | - Premature coding
L0298 | - Scope hallucination
L0299 | - Misaligned execution
L0300 | - Context loss across sessions or models
L0301 | 
L0302 | ### Rules
L0303 | 
L0304 | - No architectural delta may be written without intent decomposition.
L0305 | - No execution task may be created without explicit intent linkage.
L0306 | - Intent artifacts are read-heavy, write-light, and evolve via synthesis — not overwrites.
L0307 | 
L0308 | ---
L0309 | 
L0310 | ## 14. Intent Artifacts (Required Structure)
L0311 | 
L0312 | All projects MUST include the following directory: /intent/
L0313 | ### Required Files
L0314 | 
L0315 | #### `/intent/00_Context.md`
L0316 | Canonical project context.
L0317 | 
L0318 | Must contain:
L0319 | - Problem statement (non-solutioned)
L0320 | - Domain definitions
L0321 | - Known constraints
L0322 | - Explicit non-goals
L0323 | - Environmental assumptions
L0324 | 
L0325 | If this file is missing or incomplete, agents MUST halt or request clarification.
L0326 | 
L0327 | ---
L0328 | 
L0329 | #### `/intent/10_Exploration.md`
L0330 | Structured exploration of options.
L0331 | 
L0332 | Must contain:
L0333 | - Alternatives considered
L0334 | - Tradeoffs (pros / cons)
L0335 | - Rejected approaches (with rationale)
L0336 | - Open questions and uncertainties
L0337 | 
L0338 | Exploration may be messy but must be explicit.
L0339 | 
L0340 | ---
L0341 | 
L0342 | #### `/intent/20_Objectives.md`
L0343 | Authoritative intent lock.
L0344 | 
L0345 | Must contain:
L0346 | - Primary objectives
L0347 | - Success metrics
L0348 | - Scope boundaries
L0349 | - UX / API expectations (if applicable)
L0350 | 
L0351 | Once stabilized, this file becomes the anchor for all planning and execution.
L0352 | 
L0353 | ---
L0354 | 
L0355 | ## 15. Planning Artifacts (Execution Translation Layer)
L0356 | 
L0357 | All projects MUST include: /plan/
L0358 | 
L0359 | ### Required Files
L0360 | 
L0361 | #### `/plan/30_Roadmap.md`
L0362 | Milestones and sequencing.
L0363 | 
L0364 | Must contain:
L0365 | - Ordered phases
L0366 | - Dependencies
L0367 | - Risk points
L0368 | - Exit criteria per phase
L0369 | 
L0370 | ---
L0371 | 
L0372 | #### `/plan/40_Backlog.md`
L0373 | Executable work units.
L0374 | 
L0375 | Must contain:
L0376 | - Epics
L0377 | - Stories
L0378 | - Explicit linkage to `/intent/*` sections
L0379 | - Priority and readiness state
L0380 | 
L0381 | Nothing may enter execution unless it appears here.
L0382 | 
L0383 | ---
L0384 | 
L0385 | ## 16. Execution Contract Units (Task-Level Law)
L0386 | 
L0387 | All executable work MUST be defined as a single-scope execution contract.
L0388 | 
L0389 | ### Directory
L0390 | /tasks/
L0391 | 
L0392 | ### Task Contract Requirements
L0393 | 
L0394 | Each task file MUST include:
L0395 | 
L0396 | 1. **Intent Reference**
L0397 |    - Exact paths to relevant `/intent/*` files
L0398 |    - Exact paths to relevant `/plan/*` files
L0399 | 
L0400 | 2. **Change Scope**
L0401 |    - Files and directories explicitly allowed
L0402 |    - Files and directories explicitly forbidden
L0403 | 
L0404 | 3. **Behavioral Contract**
L0405 |    - Inputs
L0406 |    - Outputs
L0407 |    - Edge cases
L0408 | 
L0409 | 4. **Acceptance Conditions**
L0410 |    - Objective, verifiable success criteria
L0411 | 
L0412 | 5. **Rollback Expectations**
L0413 |    - Definition of failure
L0414 |    - Damage containment strategy
L0415 | 
L0416 | No task file → no execution.  
L0417 | No exceptions.
L0418 | 
L0419 | ---
L0420 | 
L0421 | ## 17. Context Assembly Rules (Anti-Bloat Enforcement)
L0422 | 
L0423 | Agents may NOT load the entire repository by default.
L0424 | 
L0425 | ### Context Pack Construction Rules
L0426 | 
L0427 | Each agent MUST:
L0428 | 
L0429 | 1. Load `/SOT.md`
L0430 | 2. Load only:
L0431 |    - Relevant `/intent/*` files
L0432 |    - Relevant `/plan/*` files
L0433 |    - The specific `/tasks/*` file being executed
L0434 | 3. Cite every loaded file by absolute path
L0435 | 
L0436 | This enforces:
L0437 | - Token efficiency
L0438 | - Determinism
L0439 | - Cross-LLM consistency
L0440 | 
L0441 | ---
L0442 | 
L0443 | ## 18. Execution Gating Rules (Hard Stops)
L0444 | 
L0445 | The Orchestrator MUST halt execution if any of the following are true:
L0446 | 
L0447 | - `/intent/20_Objectives.md` is missing
L0448 | - Task does not reference intent and plan artifacts
L0449 | - Task scope exceeds declared file boundaries
L0450 | - Acceptance conditions are missing or vague
L0451 | - Required deltas are not declared post-execution
L0452 | 
L0453 | ---
L0454 | 
L0455 | ## 19. Verification & Learning Integration
L0456 | 
L0457 | ### Verification Outputs
L0458 | 
L0459 | Verification MUST update one or more of:
L0460 | 
L0461 | - `/spec/Acceptance/*`
L0462 | - `/spec/Deltas/CHANGELOG.md`
L0463 | - `/agent/PROCESS_CHANGELOG.md`
L0464 | 
L0465 | ### Learning Rule
L0466 | 
L0467 | If human correction was required:
L0468 | - It is not a mistake
L0469 | - It is a missing artifact or rule
L0470 | 
L0471 | The Meta Agent MUST convert the correction into:
L0472 | - A stricter execution contract
L0473 | - A clearer intent artifact
L0474 | - A stronger routing or policy rule
L0475 | 
L0476 | ---
L0477 | 
L0478 | ## 20. New Project Initialization Protocol (Mandatory)
L0479 | 
L0480 | When starting any new project, the Orchestrator MUST:
L0481 | 
L0482 | 1. Create all `/intent/*` files with draft content
L0483 | 2. Create `/plan/*` skeletons
L0484 | 3. Generate initial `/tasks/*` contracts for Phase 1 only
L0485 | 4. Populate `/SOT.md` pointers
L0486 | 5. Lock execution until intent and plan are acknowledged
L0487 | 
L0488 | This guarantees every project begins with maximal structure.
L0489 | 
L0490 | ---
L0491 | 
L0492 | ## 21. Naming Philosophy (Explicit)
L0493 | 
L0494 | This framework intentionally:
L0495 | - Uses functional naming
L0496 | - Avoids branded methodologies
L0497 | - Encodes process truth, not tool allegiance
L0498 | - Allows participation by any agent, model, or human without retraining
L0499 | 
L0500 | The method is the law.  
L0501 | Names are irrelevant.
L0502 | 
L0503 | ---
L0504 | 
L0505 | ## 22. Final Reinforcement Rules
L0506 | 
L0507 | - If intent is unclear → decompose  
L0508 | - If scope is unclear → contract  
L0509 | - If execution drifts → gate  
L0510 | - If learning repeats → write law  
L0511 | 
L0512 | ---
L0513 | 
L0514 | ## Final Guarantee
L0515 | 
L0516 | If intent is not written:
L0517 | - it is not understood  
L0518 | - it is not enforced  
L0519 | - it will be repeated  
L0520 | 
L0521 | This extension completes the Meta-Orchestrated Spec-Driven Agentic Framework.
```

##### New requirements (tagged by keyword)
- L0007 [Canonical Project Law for Monorepo & Agentic Systems] **Change Control:** Append-only via ADR; breaking changes allowed but must be explicit
- L0034 [DEFINITIONS (GLOBAL)] - **SOFT** — default expectation; deviation must be justified
- L0035 [DEFINITIONS (GLOBAL)] - **OPTIONAL** — allowed, not required
- L0048 [LAW-GRD-001 (HARD) — No Silent Assumptions] All assumptions must be explicitly stated or derived from a spec.
- L0055 [LAW-GRD-003 (HARD) — No Backwards Compatibility by Default] Backwards compatibility must be explicitly justified.
- L0069 [LAW-ORG-001 (HARD) — PRD-First Development] Every meaningful unit of work must begin with a spec or PRD.
- L0073 [LAW-ORG-002 (HARD) — Command-ify Everything] All work must be reducible to explicit commands: inputs, outputs, side effects.
- L0077 [LAW-ORG-003 (HARD) — Context Reset Discipline] Context must be discoverable, reloadable, reconstructible.
- L0081 [LAW-ORG-004 (SOFT) — Modular Rules Architecture] Rules/constraints should be modular, composable, independently testable.
- L0084 [LAW-ORG-005 (SOFT) — System Evolution Mindset] Failures must produce rules/tests/specs, not tribal knowledge.
- L0091 [LAW-META-001 (HARD) — Baseline + Deltas Model] There must exist a Baseline and explicit Deltas. No mutation of baseline without a delta.
- L0094 [LAW-META-002 (HARD) — Single Source of Truth] Every system must declare exactly one SOT. Duplicated truth is a defect.
- L0110 [LAW-ENF-001 (HARD) — Mandatory Load Order] Agents and tools must load:
- L0118 [LAW-ENF-002 (HARD) — Auditability] All actions must be attributable, reproducible, explainable. If it cannot be audited, it cannot run.
- L0121 [LAW-ENF-003 (SOFT) — CI & Gatekeeping] Enforcement should be automated via lint/spec checks/acceptance tests where possible.
- L0131 [LAW-CHG-002 (HARD) — Append-Only Mentality] Rewrites must preserve history, intent, and traceability.
